          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [
            {
              "id": 351478,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{arrows}\n\\usetikzlibrary{intersections}\n\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween}\n\n\\definecolor{verylightgray}{RGB}{242, 242, 242}\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\n\\begin{tikzpicture}\n\n\\begin{axis}[\ntrig format plots=rad,\nlegend pos=outer north east,\ntick label style={font=\\footnotesize},\nlabel style={font=\\small},\nlegend style={font=\\footnotesize},\ntick style={draw=none},\naxis line style = thick,\naxis y line = center,\naxis x line = middle,\naxis equal,\n%grid = both,\nxmin = -4.9,\nxmax = 4.9,\nymin = -9.8,\nymax = 9.8,\nxtick = {-16,-8,...,16},\nytick = {-16,-8,...,16},\nxlabel = $x$,\nylabel = {$y$},\nxlabel style={below right},\nylabel style={above right},\n%% Here, we can control the positioning of each individual tick.\nx tick label style={xshift={(\\tick==-8)*-8pt},\n\t\t\t     xshift={(\\tick==8)*4pt},\n\t\t\t    yshift=2pt},\ny tick label style={yshift={(\\tick==-8)*-6pt},\n\t\t\t     yshift={(\\tick==8)*5pt}, \n\t\t\t    xshift=3pt},\nevery axis x label/.style={\n\tat={(ticklabel* cs:1.02)},\n\tanchor=west,\n},\nevery axis y label/.style={\n\tat={(ticklabel* cs:1.02)},\n\tanchor=south,\n}\n]\n\n\\def\\lcolor{deepskyblue};\n\\def\\pcolor{orangepeel};\n\n\n\\addplot [domain=0:2*pi, samples=200, color=\\lcolor, very thick]({8*sin(x)},{8*cos(x)});\n\n\\end{axis}\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "6a19539b449cf86d44c11e17cd2193da"

              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{arrows}\n\\usetikzlibrary{intersections}\n\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween}\n\n\\definecolor{verylightgray}{RGB}{242, 242, 242}\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\n\\begin{tikzpicture}\n\n\\begin{axis}[\ntrig format plots=rad,\nlegend pos=outer north east,\ntick label style={font=\\footnotesize},\nlabel style={font=\\small},\nlegend style={font=\\footnotesize},\ntick style={draw=none},\naxis line style = thick,\naxis y line = center,\naxis x line = middle,\naxis equal,\n%grid = both,\nxmin = -4.9,\nxmax = 4.9,\nymin = -9.8,\nymax = 9.8,\nxtick = {-16,-8,...,16},\nytick = {-16,-8,...,16},\nxlabel = $x$,\nylabel = {$y$},\nxlabel style={below right},\nylabel style={above right},\n%% Here, we can control the positioning of each individual tick.\nx tick label style={xshift={(\\tick==-8)*-8pt},\n\t\t\t     xshift={(\\tick==8)*4pt},\n\t\t\t    yshift=2pt},\ny tick label style={yshift={(\\tick==-8)*-6pt},\n\t\t\t     yshift={(\\tick==8)*5pt}, \n\t\t\t    xshift=3pt},\nevery axis x label/.style={\n\tat={(ticklabel* cs:1.02)},\n\tanchor=west,\n},\nevery axis y label/.style={\n\tat={(ticklabel* cs:1.02)},\n\tanchor=south,\n}\n]\n\n\\def\\lcolor{deepskyblue};\n\\def\\pcolor{orangepeel};\n\n\n\\addplot [domain=0:2*pi, samples=200, color=\\lcolor, very thick]({8*sin(x)},{8*cos(x)});\n\n\\end{axis}\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-71508-e-0"
            }

        {
          "number": 7,
          "question_id": 14552,
          "task_id": 2830906,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 842,
          "topic_name": "Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin",
          "example_id": 3225,
          "example_title": "Finding the Equation of a Circle Given Endpoints of a Diameter",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T05:59:18.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 5:59 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 77019,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:17",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that the points $X$ and $Y$ with coordinates $(5,1)$ and $(-5,-1),$ respectively, are the endpoints of a diameter of a circle centered at the origin, what is the equation of the circle?</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that the points $X$ and $Y$ with coordinates $(5,1)$ and $(-5,-1),$ respectively, are the endpoints of a diameter of a circle centered at the origin, what is the equation of the circle?",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The equation of a circle with radius $r,$ centered at the origin, is \n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]</p>\n<p>First, we calculate the length of the diameter $\\overline{XY}\\mathbin{:}$\n\\begin{align*}\nXY &= \\sqrt{{(5-(-5))}^2+{(1-(-1))}^2}\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\sqrt{{10}^2+2^2}\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\sqrt{100+4}\\\\[5pt]\n&=2\\sqrt{26}\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Since $\\overline{XY}$ is a diameter, the radius is\n\\[\nr = \\dfrac{XY}{2} =\\dfrac {2\\sqrt{26}}{2}=\\sqrt{26}.\n\\]\nSo, the equation of the circle is</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nx^2 + y^2 & =  r^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 & = {\\left(\\sqrt{26}\\right)}^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 & =  26.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The equation of a circle with radius $r,$ centered at the origin, is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nFirst, we calculate the length of the diameter $\\overline{XY}\\mathbin{:}$\n\\begin{align*}\nXY &= \\sqrt{{(5-(-5))}^2+{(1-(-1))}^2}\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\sqrt{{10}^2+2^2}\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\sqrt{100+4}\\\\[5pt]\n&=2\\sqrt{26}\n\\end{align*}\nSince $\\overline{XY}$ is a diameter, the radius is\n\\[\nr = \\dfrac{XY}{2} =\\dfrac {2\\sqrt{26}}{2}=\\sqrt{26}.\n\\]\nSo, the equation of the circle is\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2 + y^2 & = r^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 & = {\\left(\\sqrt{26}\\right)}^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 & = 26.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$x^2 + y^2  =  26$",
              "text": "$x^2 + y^2 = 26$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,

"\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{arrows}\n\\usetikzlibrary{intersections}\n\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween}\n\n\\definecolor{verylightgray}{RGB}{242, 242, 242}\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\n\\begin{tikzpicture}\n\n\\begin{axis}[\ntrig format plots=rad,\nlegend pos=outer north east,\ntick label style={font=\\footnotesize},\nlabel style={font=\\small},\nlegend style={font=\\footnotesize},\ntick style={draw=none},\naxis line style = thick,\naxis y line = center,\naxis x line = middle,\naxis equal,\n%grid = both,\nxmin = -1.5,\nxmax = 1.5,\nymin = -1.5,\nymax = 1.5,\nxtick = {0},\n%xticklabels = {$-\\sqrt2$,$\\sqrt2$},\nytick = {0},\n%yticklabels = {$-\\sqrt2$,$\\sqrt2$},\nxlabel = $x$,\nylabel = {$y$},\nxlabel style={below right},\nylabel style={above right},\n%% Here, we can control the positioning of each individual tick.\nx tick label style={xshift={(\\tick==-1)*-11pt},\n\t\t\t     xshift={(\\tick==1)*6pt},\n                yshift=3pt},\ny tick label style={yshift={(\\tick==-1)*-6pt},\n\t\t\t     yshift={(\\tick==1)*6pt},\n                    xshift=2pt},\nevery axis x label/.style={\n\tat={(ticklabel* cs:1.02)},\n\tanchor=west,\n},\nevery axis y label/.style={\n\tat={(ticklabel* cs:1.02)},\n\tanchor=south,\n}\n]\n\n\n\\def\\Ax{5}\n\\def\\Ay{1}\n\\pgfmathtruncatemacro\\Bx{-\\Ax}\n\\pgfmathtruncatemacro\\By{-\\Ay}\n\n\\pgfmathsetmacro\\R{sqrt((\\Ax)^2+(\\Ay)^2)}\n\n\\def\\lcolor{deepskyblue};\n\\def\\pcolor{orangepeel};\n\n% Diameter\n\\draw[dashed, \\pcolor, line width=0.8pt] (axis cs:{\\Ax/\\R},{\\Ay/\\R}) -- (axis cs:{\\Bx/\\R},{\\By/\\R});\n\n% Circle\n\\addplot [domain=0:2*pi, samples=200, color=\\lcolor, line width=1.0pt]({sin(x)},{cos(x)});\n\n% Points\n\\node[circle,color=deepskyblue,fill,inner sep=1.8pt, label={0,xshift=1pt,yshift=3pt:{$(\\Ax,\\Ay)$}}] at (axis cs:{\\Ax/\\R},{\\Ay/\\R}) {};\n\\node[circle,color=deepskyblue,fill,inner sep=1.8pt, label={180,xshift=-1pt,yshift=-3pt:{$(\\Bx,\\By)$}}] at (axis cs:{\\Bx/\\R},{\\By/\\R}) {};\n\n\\end{axis}\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",

        {
          "number": 8,
          "question_id": 116928,
          "task_id": 2830906,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 842,
          "topic_name": "Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin",
          "example_id": 3225,
          "example_title": "Finding the Equation of a Circle Given Endpoints of a Diameter",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T05:59:49.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 5:59 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 28756,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:29",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that the points $X$ and $Y$ with coordinates $(-4,1)$ and $(4,-1),$ respectively, are the endpoints of a diameter of a circle centered at the origin, what is the equation of the circle?</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that the points $X$ and $Y$ with coordinates $(-4,1)$ and $(4,-1),$ respectively, are the endpoints of a diameter of a circle centered at the origin, what is the equation of the circle?",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The equation of a circle with radius $r,$ centered at the origin, is \n\\[\n\tx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]</p>\n<p>First, we calculate the length of the diameter $XY\\mathbin{:}$\n\\begin{align*}\n\tXY\t&= \\sqrt{ (-4-4)^2+  (1-(-1))^2}\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&= \\sqrt{ (-8)^2+2^2}\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&= \\sqrt{64+4}\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&= 2\\sqrt{17}\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Since $\\overline{XY}$ is a diameter, the radius is\n\\[\n\tr=\\dfrac{XY}{2} = \\dfrac {2\\sqrt{17}}{2}=\\sqrt{17}. \n\\]</p>\n<p>So, the equation of the circle is</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\tx^2 + y^2 & =  r^2 \\\\[5pt]\n\tx^2 + y^2 & =\\left(\\sqrt{17}\\right)^2 \\\\[5pt]\n\tx^2 + y^2 & = 17.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The equation of a circle with radius $r,$ centered at the origin, is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nFirst, we calculate the length of the diameter $XY\\mathbin{:}$\n\\begin{align*}\nXY &= \\sqrt{ (-4-4)^2+ (1-(-1))^2}\\\\[5pt]\n&= \\sqrt{ (-8)^2+2^2}\\\\[5pt]\n&= \\sqrt{64+4}\\\\[5pt]\n&= 2\\sqrt{17}\n\\end{align*}\nSince $\\overline{XY}$ is a diameter, the radius is\n\\[\nr=\\dfrac{XY}{2} = \\dfrac {2\\sqrt{17}}{2}=\\sqrt{17}.\n\\]\nSo, the equation of the circle is\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2 + y^2 & = r^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 & =\\left(\\sqrt{17}\\right)^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 & = 17.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$x^2 + y^2 = 17$",
              "text": "$x^2 + y^2 = 17$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$x^2 + y^2 = \\sqrt{17}$",
              "text": "$x^2 + y^2 = \\sqrt{17}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$x^2 + y^2 = \\sqrt{34}$",
              "text": "$x^2 + y^2 = \\sqrt{34}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$x^2 + y^2 = 34$",
              "text": "$x^2 + y^2 = 34$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$x^2 + y^2 = 21$",
              "text": "$x^2 + y^2 = 21$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$x^2 + y^2 = 17$",
            "text": "$x^2 + y^2 = 17$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",

        {
          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 141074,
          "task_id": 2830905,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 468,
          "topic_name": "Solving Quadratic Inequalities From Graphs",
          "example_id": 5061,
          "example_title": "Solving a \"Greater Than or Equal To\" Inequality Given the Graph of the Corresponding Parabola",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T05:43:39.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 5:43 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 139377,
          "time_elapsed_display": "2:19",
          "result": "incorrect",
          "correct": false,
          "question_html": "<p>Given the graph above, solve the inequality $(x-1)(x-3) \\geq 0.$ </p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given the graph above, solve the inequality $(x-1)(x-3) \\geq 0.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Notice that our inequality</p>\n<p>\\[\n(x-1)(x-3) \\geq 0\n\\]</p>\n<p>is a &quot;<em>greater than or equal to</em>&quot; inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola </p>\n<p>\\[\ny = (x-1)(x-3)\n\\]</p>\n<p>is <em>above</em> the $x$-axis or <em>touches</em> this axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-9c5134d328b5f6b660f98ca5cc6cd69b\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/9c5134d328b5f6b660f98ca5cc6cd69b\" /></div>\n\n<p>So, the solution to the inequality is $x \\leq 1$ or $x \\geq 3.$ </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Notice that our inequality\n\\[\n(x-1)(x-3) \\geq 0\n\\]\nis a \"greater than or equal to\" inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola\n\\[\ny = (x-1)(x-3)\n\\]\nis above the $x$-axis or touches this axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.\nSo, the solution to the inequality is $x \\leq 1$ or $x \\geq 3.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$x \\leq 1$ or $x \\geq 3$ ",
              "text": "$x \\leq 1$ or $x \\geq 3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$x \\leq 1$ ",
              "text": "$x \\leq 1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": " $x \\geq 3$ ",
              "text": "$x \\geq 3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$1 \\leq x \\leq 3$   ",
              "text": "$1 \\leq x \\leq 3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$-3 \\leq x \\leq 1$  ",
              "text": "$-3 \\leq x \\leq 1$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 3,
            "html": " $x \\geq 3$ ",
            "text": "$x \\geq 3$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [
            {
              "id": 529280,
              "tex": "\\documentclass[border=1mm]{standalone}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\t\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\t\n\\begin{tikzpicture}\n[\ndeclare function={f(\\x) = (\\x-1)*(\\x-3;}\n]\n\n%Zeros\n\\def\\xOne{1}\n\\def\\xTwo{3}\n\n\\begin{axis}[\ntick label style={font=\\footnotesize},\nlabel style={font=\\small},\ntick style={line width=0.8pt, color=black},\naxis line style = thick,\naxis y line = center,\naxis x line = middle,\n%axis equal,\n%grid = both,\nxmin = -1,\nxmax = 5,\nymin = -2,\nymax = 4,\nxtick = {\\xOne, \\xTwo},\nytick = {-20,20},\nx tick label style={xshift={(\\tick==\\xOne)*-6pt}},\nx tick label style={xshift={(\\tick==\\xTwo)*4pt}},    \nxlabel = {$x$},\nylabel = {$y$},\nxlabel style={below right},\nylabel style={above right},\nevery axis x label/.style={at={(ticklabel* cs:1.02)},anchor=west},\nevery axis y label/.style={at={(ticklabel* cs:1.02)},anchor=south}\n]\n\n\\plot[domain=\\xOne:\\xTwo, samples=100, color=deepskyblue, line width=1pt, dashed](\\x,{f(\\x)});\n\n\\plot[domain=-6:\\xOne, samples=100, color=deepskyblue, line width=2pt](\\x,{f(\\x)});\n\\plot[domain=\\xTwo:6, samples=100, color=deepskyblue, line width=2pt](\\x,{f(\\x)});\n\n\\node[] at (axis cs:2.0,-1.5) {\\small $y=(x-1)(x-3)$};  \n\n% Intervals\n\\draw [   ->, orangepeel, line width=2pt, shorten <          = 2pt] (axis cs:\\xOne,0) -- (axis cs: -1.01,0);\n\\draw [   ->, orangepeel, line width=2pt, shorten <          = 2pt] (axis cs:\\xTwo,0) -- (axis cs: 5,0); \n\n% Roots\n\\coordinate [] (A1) at (axis cs:\\xOne,0);\n\\coordinate [] (A2) at (axis cs:\\xTwo,0);\n\n\\node[circle, draw=orangepeel, fill=orangepeel, inner sep=2pt] at (A1) {};\n\\node[circle, draw=orangepeel, fill=orangepeel, inner sep=2pt] at (A2) {};\n\n\\end{axis}\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "9c5134d328b5f6b660f98ca5cc6cd69b"
            }
          ],
          "raw": {

            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        },

          "question_html": "<p>Given the graph above, solve the inequality $-(x+2)(x+6) \\geq 0.$ </p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given the graph above, solve the inequality $-(x+2)(x+6) \\geq 0.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Notice that our inequality</p>\n<p>\\[\n-(x+2)(x+6) \\geq 0,\n\\]</p>\n<p>is a &quot;<em>greater than or equal to</em>&quot; inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola </p>\n<p>\\[\ny = -(x+2)(x+6)\n\\]</p>\n<p>is <em>above</em> the $x$-axis or <em>touches</em> this axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-7087c96c65f514efcac84c7ba04bc873\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/7087c96c65f514efcac84c7ba04bc873\" /></div>\n\n<p>So, the solution to the inequality is $-6 \\leq x \\leq    -2.$ </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Notice that our inequality\n\\[\n-(x+2)(x+6) \\geq 0,\n\\]\nis a \"greater than or equal to\" inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola\n\\[\ny = -(x+2)(x+6)\n\\]\nis above the $x$-axis or touches this axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.\nSo, the solution to the inequality is $-6 \\leq x \\leq -2.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",

            {
              "id": 529299,
              "tex": "\\documentclass[border=1mm]{standalone}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\t\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\t\n\\begin{tikzpicture}\n[\ndeclare function={f(\\x) = -(\\x+6)*(\\x+2);}\n]\n\n%Zeros\n\\def\\xOne{-6}\n\\def\\xTwo{-2}\n\n\\begin{axis}[\ntick label style={font=\\footnotesize},\nlabel style={font=\\small},\ntick style={line width=0.8pt, color=black},\naxis line style = thick,\naxis y line = center,\naxis x line = middle,\n%axis equal,\n%grid = both,\nxmin = -10,\nxmax = 4,\nymin = -10,\nymax = 8,\nxtick = {\\xOne, \\xTwo}, \nytick = {-20,20},\nx tick label style={xshift={(\\tick==\\xOne)*4pt}},\nx tick label style={xshift={(\\tick==\\xTwo)*-6pt}},    \nxlabel = {$x$},\nylabel = {$y$},\nxlabel style={below right},\nylabel style={above right},\nevery axis x label/.style={at={(ticklabel* cs:1.02)},anchor=west},\nevery axis y label/.style={at={(ticklabel* cs:1.02)},anchor=south}\n]\n\n\\plot[domain=\\xOne:\\xTwo, samples=100, color=deepskyblue, line width=2pt](\\x,{f(\\x)});\n\n\\plot[domain=-10:\\xOne, samples=100, color=deepskyblue, line width=1pt, dashed](\\x,{f(\\x)});\n\\plot[domain=\\xTwo:10, samples=100, color=deepskyblue, line width=1pt, dashed](\\x,{f(\\x)});\n\\node[] at (axis cs:-4,5) {\\small $y=-(x+2)(x+6)$};  \n\n% Intervals\n\\draw [<               - >, orangepeel, line width=2pt, shorten <               = 2pt, shorten > = 2pt] (axis cs:\\xOne,0) -- (axis cs:\\xTwo,0);\n\n% Roots\n\\coordinate [] (A1) at (axis cs:\\xOne,0);\n\\coordinate [] (A2) at (axis cs:\\xTwo,0);\n\n\\node[circle, draw=orangepeel, fill=orangepeel, inner sep=2pt] at (A1) {};\n\\node[circle, draw=orangepeel, fill=orangepeel, inner sep=2pt] at (A2) {};\n\n\\end{axis}\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "7087c96c65f514efcac84c7ba04bc873"
            }

        {
          "number": 3,
          "question_id": 141079,
          "task_id": 2830905,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 468,
          "topic_name": "Solving Quadratic Inequalities From Graphs",
          "example_id": 5061,
          "example_title": "Solving a \"Greater Than or Equal To\" Inequality Given the Graph of the Corresponding Parabola",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T05:45:24.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 5:45 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 19292,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:19",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given the graph above, solve the inequality $-(x-3)(x+1) \\geq 0.$ </p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given the graph above, solve the inequality $-(x-3)(x+1) \\geq 0.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Notice that our inequality</p>\n<p>\\[\n-(x-3)(x+1) \\geq 0, \n\\]</p>\n<p>is a &quot;<em>greater than or equal to</em>&quot; inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola </p>\n<p>\\[\ny = -(x-3)(x+1) \n\\]</p>\n<p>is <em>above</em> the $x$-axis or <em>touches</em> this axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-4ad455a47aa6d8360081b89dd44fd6a2\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/4ad455a47aa6d8360081b89dd44fd6a2\" /></div>\n\n<p>So, the solution to the inequality is $-1 \\leq x \\leq    3.$  </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Notice that our inequality\n\\[\n-(x-3)(x+1) \\geq 0,\n\\]\nis a \"greater than or equal to\" inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola\n\\[\ny = -(x-3)(x+1)\n\\]\nis above the $x$-axis or touches this axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.\nSo, the solution to the inequality is $-1 \\leq x \\leq 3.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$-1 \\leq x \\leq    3$  ",
              "text": "$-1 \\leq x \\leq 3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$-3 \\leq x \\leq    1$   ",
              "text": "$-3 \\leq x \\leq 1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$x \\leq -1$  or  $ x \\geq    3$   ",
              "text": "$x \\leq -1$ or $ x \\geq 3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$x \\leq -3$  or  $ x \\geq    1$    ",
              "text": "$x \\leq -3$ or $ x \\geq 1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$ x \\geq    1$    ",
              "text": "$ x \\geq 1$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$-1 \\leq x \\leq    3$  ",
            "text": "$-1 \\leq x \\leq 3$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",

          "question_html": "<p>Given the graph above, solve the inequality $x^2+x-2 \\leq 0.$    </p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given the graph above, solve the inequality $x^2+x-2 \\leq 0.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Notice that our inequality</p>\n<p>\\[\nx^2+x -2 \\leq 0, \n\\]</p>\n<p>is a &quot;<em>less than or equal to</em>&quot; inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola </p>\n<p>\\[\ny =x^2+x-2\n\\]</p>\n<p>is <em>below</em> the $x$-axis or <em>touches</em> this axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-bc46564c2e758e0e381fc6e745fc60dc\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/bc46564c2e758e0e381fc6e745fc60dc\" /></div>\n\n<p>So, the solution to the inequality is $-2 \\leq x \\leq 1.$  </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Notice that our inequality\n\\[\nx^2+x -2 \\leq 0,\n\\]\nis a \"less than or equal to\" inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola\n\\[\ny =x^2+x-2\n\\]\nis below the $x$-axis or touches this axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.\nSo, the solution to the inequality is $-2 \\leq x \\leq 1.$",

          "question_html": "<p>Given the graph above, solve the inequality $-(x+2)(x-4) \\leq 0.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given the graph above, solve the inequality $-(x+2)(x-4) \\leq 0.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Notice that our inequality</p>\n<p>\\[\n-(x+2)(x-4) \\leq 0,\n\\]</p>\n<p>is a &quot;<em>less than or equal to</em>&quot; inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola </p>\n<p>\\[\ny = -(x+2)(x-4)\n\\]</p>\n<p>is <em>below</em> the $x$-axis or <em>touches</em> this axis. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola\n\\[\ny = -(x+2)(x-4)\n\\]\nis below the $x$-axis or touches this axis.           "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [
            {
              "id": 529330,
              "tex": "\\documentclass[border=1mm]{standalone}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\t\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\t\n\\begin{tikzpicture}\n[\ndeclare function={f(\\x) =(\\x+1)*(\\x-1);} \n]\n\n%Zeros\n\\def\\xOne{-1}\n\\def\\xTwo{1}\n\n\\begin{axis}[\ntick label style={font=\\footnotesize},\nlabel style={font=\\small},\ntick style={line width=0.8pt, color=black},\naxis line style = thick,\naxis y line = center,\naxis x line = middle,\n%axis equal,\n%grid = both,\nxmin = -2.5,\nxmax = 2.5, \nymin = -2,\nymax = 2,\nxtick = {\\xOne, \\xTwo},\nytick = {-20,20},\nx tick label style={xshift={(\\tick==\\xOne)*-5pt}},\nx tick label style={xshift={(\\tick==\\xTwo)*3pt}},      \nxlabel = {$x$},\nylabel = {$y$},\nxlabel style={below right},\nylabel style={above right},\nevery axis x label/.style={at={(ticklabel* cs:1.02)},anchor=west},\nevery axis y label/.style={at={(ticklabel* cs:1.02)},anchor=south}\n]\n\n\\plot[domain=\\xOne:\\xTwo, samples=100, color=deepskyblue, line width=2pt](\\x,{f(\\x)});\n\n\\plot[domain=-6:\\xOne, samples=100, color=deepskyblue, line width=1pt, dashed](\\x,{f(\\x)});\n\\plot[domain=\\xTwo:6, samples=100, color=deepskyblue, line width=1pt, dashed](\\x,{f(\\x)});\n\\node[] at (axis cs:1,-1.0) {\\small $y=x^2-1 $};   \n\n% Intervals\n\\draw [<             - >, orangepeel, line width=2pt, shorten <             = 2pt, shorten > = 2pt] (axis cs:\\xOne,0) -- (axis cs:\\xTwo,0);\n\n% Roots\n\\coordinate [] (A1) at (axis cs:\\xOne,0);\n\\coordinate [] (A2) at (axis cs:\\xTwo,0);\n\n\\node[circle, draw=orangepeel, fill=orangepeel, inner sep=2pt] at (A1) {};\n\\node[circle, draw=orangepeel, fill=orangepeel, inner sep=2pt] at (A2) {};\n\n\\end{axis}\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",

          "topic_id": 468,
          "topic_name": "Solving Quadratic Inequalities From Graphs",
          "example_id": 9976,
          "example_title": "Solving a \"Less Than\" Inequality Given the Graph of the Corresponding Parabola",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T05:51:25.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 5:51 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 26782,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:27",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given the graph above, solve the inequality $x(x-5) \\lt 0.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given the graph above, solve the inequality $x(x-5) \\lt 0.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Notice that our inequality</p>\n<p>\\[\nx(x-5) \\lt 0\n\\] </p>\n<p>is a &quot;<em>less than</em>&quot; inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola </p>\n<p>\\[\ny = x(x-5)\n\\]</p>\n<p>is <em>below </em>the $x$-axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-98d6664059516d3a5b2ffa183567f2a9\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/98d6664059516d3a5b2ffa183567f2a9\" /></div>\n\n\n<p>So, the solution to the inequality is $0 \\lt x \\lt 5.$ </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Notice that our inequality\n\\[\nx(x-5) \\lt 0\n\\]\nis a \"less than\" inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola\n\\[\ny = x(x-5)\n\\]\nis below the $x$-axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.\nSo, the solution to the inequality is $0 \\lt x \\lt 5.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$0 <    x <    5$     ",
              "text": "$0 < x < 5$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$x <    0$ or $x>5$ ",
              "text": "$x < 0$ or $x>5$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$-5 <    x <    5$ ",
              "text": "$-5 < x < 5$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$x <    -5$ or $x>5$ ",
              "text": "$x < -5$ or $x>5$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$x <    -5$ or $x>0$ ",
              "text": "$x < -5$ or $x>0$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$0 <    x <    5$     ",
            "text": "$0 < x < 5$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",

          "question_html": "<p>Given the graph above, solve the inequality $x(x+2) \\lt 0.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given the graph above, solve the inequality $x(x+2) \\lt 0.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Notice that our inequality</p>\n<p>\\[\nx(x+2) \\lt 0\n\\] </p>\n<p>is a &quot;<em>less than</em>&quot; inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola </p>\n<p>\\[\ny = x(x+2)\n\\]</p>\n<p>is <em>below </em>the $x$-axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-a517137a68bd5d1ecab453a82a58aa82\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/a517137a68bd5d1ecab453a82a58aa82\" /></div>\n\n\n<p>So, the solution to the inequality is $-2 \\lt x \\lt 0.$ </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Notice that our inequality\n\\[\nx(x+2) \\lt 0\n\\]\nis a \"less than\" inequality.           "question_html": "<p>Given the graph above, solve the inequality $4 - x^2>0.$  </p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given the graph above, solve the inequality $4 - x^2>0.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Notice that our inequality</p>\n<p>\\[\n4 - x^2> 0,\n\\]</p>\n<p>is a &quot;<em>greater than</em>&quot; inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola  </p>\n<p>\\[\ny =4 - x^2\n\\]</p>\n<p>is <em>above</em> the $x$-axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-a23e029654306628faaa87bbd54cccca\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/a23e029654306628faaa87bbd54cccca\" /></div>\n\n<p>So, the solution to the inequality is $-2 <  x <  2.$ </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Notice that our inequality\n\\[\n4 - x^2> 0,\n\\]\nis a \"greater than\" inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola\n\\[\ny =4 - x^2\n\\]\nis above the $x$-axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.\nSo, the solution to the inequality is $-2 < x < 2.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [

            },

          "question_html": "<p>Given the graph above, solve the inequality $x^2+5x-6>0.$  </p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given the graph above, solve the inequality $x^2+5x-6>0.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Notice that our inequality</p>\n<p>\\[\nx^2+5x-6 > 0,\n\\]</p>\n<p>is a &quot;<em>greater than</em>&quot; inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola </p>\n<p>\\[\ny = x^2+5x-6\n\\]</p>\n<p>is <em>above</em> the $x$-axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-0992a8a6df736412b396ec36a11a352b\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/0992a8a6df736412b396ec36a11a352b\" /></div>\n\n<p>So, the solution to the inequality is $x <   -6$ or $x > 1.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Notice that our inequality\n\\[\nx^2+5x-6 > 0,\n\\]\nis a \"greater than\" inequality. Therefore, we need to find the values of $x$ for which the parabola\n\\[\ny = x^2+5x-6\n\\]\nis above the $x$-axis. We mark these values of $x$ in blue in the diagram below.\nSo, the solution to the inequality is $x < -6$ or $x > 1.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$x <   -6$ or $x > 1$\n",
              "text": "$x < -6$ or $x > 1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$-6 <     x <                  0$",
              "text": "$-6 < x < 0$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$x <     -1$ or $x > 6$\n",

            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$-6 <     x <     1$",
              "text": "$-6 < x < 1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$  -10 <     x <     0$",
              "text": "$ -10 < x < 0$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$x <   -6$ or $x > 1$\n",
            "text": "$x < -6$ or $x > 1$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [
            {
              "id": 529565,
              "tex": "\\documentclass[border=1mm]{standalone}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\t\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\t\n\\begin{tikzpicture}\n[\ndeclare function={f(\\x) = (\\x)^2+5*\\x-6;} \n]\n\n%Zeros\n\\def\\xOne{-6}\n\\def\\xTwo{1}\n\n\\begin{axis}[\ntick label style={font=\\footnotesize},\nlabel style={font=\\small},\ntick style={line width=0.8pt, color=black},\naxis line style = thick,\naxis y line = center,\naxis x line = middle,\n%axis equal,\n%grid = both,\nxmin = -9,\nxmax = 4,\nymin = -14,\nymax = 6, \nxtick = {\\xOne, \\xTwo},\nytick = {-20,20},\nx tick label style={xshift={(\\tick==\\xOne)*-6pt}},\nx tick label style={xshift={(\\tick==\\xTwo)*4pt}},    \nxlabel = {$x$},\nylabel = {$y$},\nxlabel style={below right},\nylabel style={above right},\nevery axis x label/.style={at={(ticklabel* cs:1.02)},anchor=west},\nevery axis y label/.style={at={(ticklabel* cs:1.02)},anchor=south}\n]\n \n\\plot[domain=\\xOne:\\xTwo, samples=100, color=deepskyblue, line width=1pt, dashed](\\x,{f(\\x)});\n\n\\plot[domain=-9:\\xOne, samples=100, color=deepskyblue, line width=2pt](\\x,{f(\\x)});\n\\plot[domain=\\xTwo:7, samples=100, color=deepskyblue, line width=2pt](\\x,{f(\\x)});\n\n\\node[] at (axis cs:-6.5,-10.6) {\\small $y=x^2+5x-6$}; \n\n% Intervals\n\\draw [   ->, orangepeel, line width=2pt, shorten <            = 2pt] (axis cs:\\xOne,0) -- (axis cs: -9.01,0);\n\\draw [   ->, orangepeel, line width=2pt, shorten <            = 2pt] (axis cs:\\xTwo,0) -- (axis cs: 4,0);\n\n% Roots\n\\coordinate [] (A1) at (axis cs:\\xOne,0);\n\\coordinate [] (A2) at (axis cs:\\xTwo,0);\n \n\\node[circle, draw=orangepeel, fill=white, inner sep=2pt, very thick] at (A1) {};\n\\node[circle, draw=orangepeel, fill=white, inner sep=2pt, very thick] at (A2) {};\n \n\\end{axis}\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "0992a8a6df736412b396ec36a11a352b"
            }
          ],

    {
      "task_id": 2831736,

      "completed_at": "2025-01-13T05:40:08.000Z",

      "review_url": "/learn?taskId=2831736",

        {
          "id": 149,
          "name": "Elementary Row Operations",

      "question_count": 8,
      "questions": [
        {
          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 130435,
          "task_id": 2831736,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 3029,
          "topic_name": "Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination",
          "example_id": 8050,
          "example_title": "Using Row Operations to Create a Column of Zeros in a 3x3 Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T05:24:32.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 5:24 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 20821,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:21",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>\\[A =\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}5 & \\color{blue}7 \\\\\n        -3 & 1 & -5 \\\\\n        2 & 9 & 7 \n\t\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}5 & \\color{blue}7 \\\\\n                \\color{red}0 & 16 & 16 \\\\\n                \\color{red}0 & -1 & -7\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n<p>By applying elementary row operations of the form $R_i:=R_i+kR_1,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the elementary row operations?</p>\n",
          "question_text": "\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}5 & \\color{blue}7 \\\\\n-3 & 1 & -5 \\\\\n2 & 9 & 7\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}5 & \\color{blue}7 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 & 16 & 16 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 & -1 & -7\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nBy applying elementary row operations of the form $R_i:=R_i+kR_1,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the elementary row operations?",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make the entries below $a_{11}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$</p>\n<p>To do this, we can apply the following elementary row operations:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\tA\n\t&=\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & 5 & 7 \\\\\n                -3 & 1 & -5 \\\\\n                 2 & 9 & 7\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tR_2:=R_2+3R_1\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & 5 & 7 \\\\\n                \\color{red}0 & 16 & 16 \\\\\n                 2 & 9 & 7\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tR_3:=R_3-2R_1\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & 5 & 7 \\\\\n                \\color{red}0 & 16 & 16 \\\\\n                \\color{red}0 & -1 & -7\n\t\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n\t&=  B\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the elementary row operations are</p>\n<p>$\\qquad$ $R_2:=R_2+3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3-2R_1.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make the entries below $a_{11}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary row operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & 5 & 7 \\\\\n-3 & 1 & -5 \\\\\n2 & 9 & 7\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_2:=R_2+3R_1\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & 5 & 7 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 & 16 & 16 \\\\\n2 & 9 & 7\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_3:=R_3-2R_1\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & 5 & 7 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 & 16 & 16 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 & -1 & -7\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&= B\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the elementary row operations are\n$\\qquad$ $R_2:=R_2+3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3-2R_1.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$R_2:=R_2+3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3-2R_1$",
              "text": "$R_2:=R_2+3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3-2R_1$"
            },
            {

            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$R_2:=R_2+3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3+2R_1$",
              "text": "$R_2:=R_2+3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3+2R_1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$R_2:=R_2-3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3+2R_1$",
              "text": "$R_2:=R_2-3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3+2R_1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$R_2:=R_2+2R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3-3R_1$",
              "text": "$R_2:=R_2+2R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3-3R_1$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$R_2:=R_2+3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3-2R_1$",
            "text": "$R_2:=R_2+3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3-2R_1$",
            "free_response": [],

          "hints": [],

        {
          "number": 2,
          "question_id": 130444,
          "task_id": 2831736,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 3029,
          "topic_name": "Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination",
          "example_id": 8050,
          "example_title": "Using Row Operations to Create a Column of Zeros in a 3x3 Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T05:25:07.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 5:25 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 31408,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:31",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>\\[A =\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t7 & 4 & 2\\\\\n \t-9 & -6 & -12 \\\\\n \t\\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} & \\color{blue}5 \\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t-5 &  \\color{red}0 & -8\\\\\n \t\t9 &  \\color{red}0   & 3\\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} & \\color{blue}5 \\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n<p>By applying elementary row operations of the form $R_i:=R_i+kR_3,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the elementary row operations?</p>\n",
          "question_text": "\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 & 4 & 2\\\\\n-9 & -6 & -12 \\\\\n\\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} & \\color{blue}5 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n-5 & \\color{red}0 & -8\\\\\n9 & \\color{red}0 & 3\\\\\n\\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} & \\color{blue}5 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nBy applying elementary row operations of the form $R_i:=R_i+kR_3,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the elementary row operations?",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make the entries above $a_{32}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$</p>\n<p>To do this, we can apply the following elementary row operations:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\tA\n\t&=\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t7 & 4 & 2\\\\\n \t\t-9 & -6 & -12 \\\\\n \t\t6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} & 5 \\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tR_1:=R_1-2R_3\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t-5 &  {\\color{red}0} & -8\\\\\n \t\t-9 & -6 & -12 \\\\\n \t\t6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} & 5 \\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tR_2:=R_2+3R_3\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t-5 &  {\\color{red}0} & -8\\\\\n \t\t9 &  {\\color{red}0}   & 3\\\\\n \t\t6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} & 5 \\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n\t&=  B\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the elementary row operations are</p>\n<p>$\\qquad$ $R_1:=R_1-2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_2:=R_2+3R_3.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make the entries above $a_{32}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary row operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 & 4 & 2\\\\\n-9 & -6 & -12 \\\\\n6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} & 5 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_1:=R_1-2R_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-5 & {\\color{red}0} & -8\\\\\n-9 & -6 & -12 \\\\\n6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} & 5 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_2:=R_2+3R_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-5 & {\\color{red}0} & -8\\\\\n9 & {\\color{red}0} & 3\\\\\n6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} & 5 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&= B\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the elementary row operations are\n$\\qquad$ $R_1:=R_1-2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_2:=R_2+3R_3.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$R_1:=R_1-2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_2:=R_2+3R_3$",
              "text": "$R_1:=R_1-2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_2:=R_2+3R_3$"

            },

            {

              "html": "$R_1:=R_1-3R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_2:=R_2+2R_3$",

            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {

            "html": "$R_1:=R_1-2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_2:=R_2+3R_3$",

        {
          "number": 3,
          "question_id": 91181,
          "task_id": 2831736,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 3029,
          "topic_name": "Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination",
          "example_id": 8048,
          "example_title": "Using Row Operations to Create a Column of Zeros in a 4x4 Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T05:29:23.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 5:29 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 102273,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:42",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>\\[A =\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t-1 & 4 & 3&  7\\\\\n \t\t-9 & 2 & 2 & -1\\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}-5 & \\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}7\\\\\n \t\t4 & -2 & 2 & 6\n\t\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t14 & -14 & \\color{red}0 & -14\\\\\n \t\t1 & -10 & \\color{red}0 & -15\\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}-5 & \\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}7\\\\\n \t\t14 & -14 & \\color{red}0 & -8\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n<p>Consider the matrices $A$ and $B$ above. By applying three matrix elementary row operations </p>\n<ol class=\"questionStatements\">\n<li>  $R_1 := R_1 - 3R_3$ </li>\n<li>  $R_2:=\\ldots$ </li>\n<li>  $R_4:= \\ldots$ </li>\n</ol>\n\n<p>each of the form $R_i:=R_i+kR_3,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the missing elementary row operations? </p>\n",
          "question_text": "\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-1 & 4 & 3& 7\\\\\n-9 & 2 & 2 & -1\\\\\n\\color{blue}-5 & \\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}7\\\\\n4 & -2 & 2 & 6\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n14 & -14 & \\color{red}0 & -14\\\\\n1 & -10 & \\color{red}0 & -15\\\\\n\\color{blue}-5 & \\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}7\\\\\n14 & -14 & \\color{red}0 & -8\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nConsider the matrices $A$ and $B$ above. By applying three matrix elementary row operations\n$R_1 := R_1 - 3R_3$\n$R_2:=\\ldots$\n$R_4:= \\ldots$\neach of the form $R_i:=R_i+kR_3,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the missing elementary row operations?",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries above and below $a_{33}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$</p>\n<p>To do this, we can apply the following elementary row operations:\n\\begin{align*}\n\tA\n\t&=\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t-1 & 4 & 3&  7\\\\\n \t\t-9 & 2 & 2 & -1\\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}-5 & \\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}7\\\\\n \t\t4 & -2 & 2 & 6\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tR_1:=R_1-3R_3\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim  \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t14 & -14 & {\\color{red}0} & -14\\\\\n \t\t-9 & 2 & 2 & -1\\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}-5 & \\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}7\\\\\n \t\t4 & -2 & 2 & 6\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tR_2:=R_2-2R_3\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim  \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t14 & -14 & {\\color{red}0} & -14\\\\\n \t\t1 & -10 & {\\color{red}0} & -15\\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}-5 & \\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}7\\\\\n \t\t4 & -2 & 2 & 6\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tR_4:=R_4-2R_3\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim    \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t14 & -14 & {\\color{red}0} & -14\\\\\n \t\t1 & -10 & {\\color{red}0} & -15\\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}-5 & \\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}7\\\\\n \t\t14 & -14 & {\\color{red}0}& -8\n\t\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&= B.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the missing elementary row operations are</p>\n<p>$\\qquad$ $R_2 := R_2 - 2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4-2R_3.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries above and below $a_{33}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary row operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-1 & 4 & 3& 7\\\\\n-9 & 2 & 2 & -1\\\\\n\\color{blue}-5 & \\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}7\\\\\n4 & -2 & 2 & 6\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_1:=R_1-3R_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n14 & -14 & {\\color{red}0} & -14\\\\\n-9 & 2 & 2 & -1\\\\\n\\color{blue}-5 & \\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}7\\\\\n4 & -2 & 2 & 6\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_2:=R_2-2R_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n14 & -14 & {\\color{red}0} & -14\\\\\n1 & -10 & {\\color{red}0} & -15\\\\\n\\color{blue}-5 & \\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}7\\\\\n4 & -2 & 2 & 6\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_4:=R_4-2R_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n14 & -14 & {\\color{red}0} & -14\\\\\n1 & -10 & {\\color{red}0} & -15\\\\\n\\color{blue}-5 & \\color{blue}6 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}7\\\\\n14 & -14 & {\\color{red}0}& -8\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&= B.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the missing elementary row operations are\n$\\qquad$ $R_2 := R_2 - 2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4-2R_3.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",

          ],

        {
          "number": 4,
          "question_id": 130464,
          "task_id": 2831736,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 3029,
          "topic_name": "Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination",
          "example_id": 8048,
          "example_title": "Using Row Operations to Create a Column of Zeros in a 4x4 Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T05:36:24.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 5:36 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 398417,
          "time_elapsed_display": "6:38",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>\\[A =\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t-10 & 6 & 6&  2\\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}8 & \\color{blue}-3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3} & \\color{blue}0\\\\\n \t\t3 & -8 & -9 & 0\\\\\n \t\t0 & 8 & 3 & 8\n\t\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t6 & 0  & \\color{red}0 & 2\\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}8 & \\color{blue}-3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3} & \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n \t\t-21 & 1 & \\color{red}0 & 0\\\\\n \t\t8 & 5 & \\color{red}0 & 8\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n<p>Consider the matrices $A$ and $B$ above. By applying three matrix elementary row operations </p>\n<ol class=\"questionStatements\">\n<li>  $R_1:= \\ldots$ </li>\n<li>  $R_3 := R_3 - 3R_2$ </li>\n<li>  $R_4:= \\ldots$ </li>\n</ol>\n\n<p>each of the form $R_i:=R_i+kR_2,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the missing elementary row operations? </p>\n",
          "question_text": "\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-10 & 6 & 6& 2\\\\\n\\color{blue}8 & \\color{blue}-3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3} & \\color{blue}0\\\\\n3 & -8 & -9 & 0\\\\\n0 & 8 & 3 & 8\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n6 & 0 & \\color{red}0 & 2\\\\\n\\color{blue}8 & \\color{blue}-3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3} & \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n-21 & 1 & \\color{red}0 & 0\\\\\n8 & 5 & \\color{red}0 & 8\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nConsider the matrices $A$ and $B$ above. By applying three matrix elementary row operations\n$R_1:= \\ldots$\n$R_3 := R_3 - 3R_2$\n$R_4:= \\ldots$\neach of the form $R_i:=R_i+kR_2,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the missing elementary row operations?",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries above and below $a_{23}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$</p>\n<p>To do this, we can apply the following elementary row operations:\n\\begin{align*}\n\tA\n\t&=\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t-10 & 6 & 6&  2\\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}8 & \\color{blue}-3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3}  &  \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n \t\t3 & -8 & -9 & 0\\\\\n \t\t0 & 8 & 3 & 8\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tR_1:=R_1+2R_2\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t6 & 0  & {\\color{red}0}&  2\\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}8 & \\color{blue}-3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3}  &  \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n                3 & -8 & -9 & 0\\\\\n \t\t0 & 8 & 3 & 8\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tR_3:=R_3-3R_2\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim  \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t6 & 0  & {\\color{red}0}&  2\\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}8 & \\color{blue}-3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3}  &  \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n                -21 & 1 & {\\color{red}0} & 0\\\\\n \t\t0 & 8 & 3 & 8\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tR_4:=R_4+R_2\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim  \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t6 & 0  & {\\color{red}0}&  2\\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}8 & \\color{blue}-3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3}  &  \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n                -21 & 1 & {\\color{red}0} & 0\\\\\n \t\t8 & 5 & {\\color{red}0} & 8\n\t\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]   \n &= B.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the missing elementary row operations are</p>\n<p>$\\qquad$ $R_1 := R_1 + 2 R_2\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4+ R_2.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries above and below $a_{23}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary row operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-10 & 6 & 6& 2\\\\\n\\color{blue}8 & \\color{blue}-3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3} & \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n3 & -8 & -9 & 0\\\\\n0 & 8 & 3 & 8\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_1:=R_1+2R_2\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n6 & 0 & {\\color{red}0}& 2\\\\\n\\color{blue}8 & \\color{blue}-3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3} & \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n3 & -8 & -9 & 0\\\\\n0 & 8 & 3 & 8\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_3:=R_3-3R_2\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n6 & 0 & {\\color{red}0}& 2\\\\\n\\color{blue}8 & \\color{blue}-3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3} & \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n-21 & 1 & {\\color{red}0} & 0\\\\\n0 & 8 & 3 & 8\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_4:=R_4+R_2\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n6 & 0 & {\\color{red}0}& 2\\\\\n\\color{blue}8 & \\color{blue}-3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3} & \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n-21 & 1 & {\\color{red}0} & 0\\\\\n8 & 5 & {\\color{red}0} & 8\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&= B.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the missing elementary row operations are\n$\\qquad$ $R_1 := R_1 + 2 R_2\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4+ R_2.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",

          ],
          "student_answer": {

        {
          "number": 5,
          "question_id": 130609,
          "task_id": 2831736,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 3029,
          "topic_name": "Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination",
          "example_id": 8051,
          "example_title": "Using Column Operations to Create a Row of Zeros in a 3x3 Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T05:37:17.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 5:37 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 11557,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:12",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>\\[A =\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t\\color{blue}9 & -2 & -16 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} & 2 & 8 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}0 & 4 & 2 \\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t\\color{blue}9 & 7 & 20 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} & \\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}0 & 4 & 2 \\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n<p>By applying elementary column operations of the form $C_i:=C_i+kC_1,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the elementary column operations? </p>\n",
          "question_text": "\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}9 & -2 & -16 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} & 2 & 8 \\\\\n\\color{blue}0 & 4 & 2 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}9 & 7 & 20 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} & \\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 \\\\\n\\color{blue}0 & 4 & 2 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nBy applying elementary column operations of the form $C_i:=C_i+kC_1,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the elementary column operations?",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries to the right of $a_{21}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$</p>\n<p>To do this, we can apply the following elementary column operations:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\tA\n\t&=\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t\\color{blue}9 & -2 & -16 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} & 2 & 8 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}0 & 4 & 2 \\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tC_2:=C_2+C_1\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t\\color{blue}9 & 7 & -16 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} & \\color{red}0 & 8 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}0 & 4 & 2 \\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tC_3:=C_3+4C_1\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t\\color{blue}9 & 7 & 20 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} & \\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}0 & 4 & 2 \\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&= B\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the elementary column operations are</p>\n<p>$\\qquad$ $C_2:=C_2+C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+4C_1.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries to the right of $a_{21}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary column operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}9 & -2 & -16 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} & 2 & 8 \\\\\n\\color{blue}0 & 4 & 2 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_2:=C_2+C_1\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}9 & 7 & -16 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} & \\color{red}0 & 8 \\\\\n\\color{blue}0 & 4 & 2 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_3:=C_3+4C_1\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}9 & 7 & 20 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} & \\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 \\\\\n\\color{blue}0 & 4 & 2 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\\\\[5pt]\n&= B\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the elementary column operations are\n$\\qquad$ $C_2:=C_2+C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+4C_1.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [

              "number": 1,
              "html": "$C_2:=C_2+C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+4C_1$",

          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>\\[A =\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t\\color{blue}0 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}3 & 7 & 4 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} & 6 & 3\\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t\\color{blue}0 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}3 & 1 & 1 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} & \\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0\\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n<p>By applying elementary column operations of the form $C_i:=C_i+kC_1,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the elementary column operations? </p>\n",
          "question_text": "\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}0 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n\\color{blue}3 & 7 & 4 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} & 6 & 3\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB= \\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}0 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n\\color{blue}3 & 1 & 1 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} & \\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nBy applying elementary column operations of the form $C_i:=C_i+kC_1,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the elementary column operations?",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries to the right of $a_{31}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$</p>\n<p>To do this, we can apply the following elementary column operations:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\tA\n\t&=\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t\\color{blue}0 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}3 & 7 & 4 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} & 6 & 3\\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tC_2:=C_2-2C_1\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t\\color{blue}0 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}3 & 1 & 4 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} & \\color{red}0 & 3\\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tC_3:=C_3-C_1\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t\\color{blue}0 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}3 & 1 & 1 \\\\\n \t\t\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} & \\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0\\\\\n\t\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&= B\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the elementary column operations are</p>\n<p>$\\qquad$ $C_2:=C_2-2C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-C_1.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries to the right of $a_{31}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary column operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}0 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n\\color{blue}3 & 7 & 4 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} & 6 & 3\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_2:=C_2-2C_1\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}0 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n\\color{blue}3 & 1 & 4 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} & \\color{red}0 & 3\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_3:=C_3-C_1\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}0 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n\\color{blue}3 & 1 & 1 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} & \\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&= B\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the elementary column operations are\n$\\qquad$ $C_2:=C_2-2C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-C_1.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$C_2:=C_2-2C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-C_1$\n",
              "text": "$C_2:=C_2-2C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-C_1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$C_2:=C_2-2C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+C_1$",
              "text": "$C_2:=C_2-2C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+C_1$"

            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$C_2:=C_2+2C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-C_1$",
              "text": "$C_2:=C_2+2C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-C_1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$C_2:=C_2-C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-2C_1$",
              "text": "$C_2:=C_2-C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-2C_1$"

            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$C_2:=C_2+3C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+2C_1$",
              "text": "$C_2:=C_2+3C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+2C_1$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$C_2:=C_2-2C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-C_1$\n",
            "text": "$C_2:=C_2-2C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-C_1$",

        {
          "number": 7,
          "question_id": 91211,
          "task_id": 2831736,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 3029,
          "topic_name": "Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination",
          "example_id": 8049,
          "example_title": "Using Column Operations to Create a Row of Zeros in a 4x4 Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T05:39:39.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 5:39 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 86942,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:27",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>\\[A =\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t1 & 1 & 6 & \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n \t8 & 1 & 2 & \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n \t0 & 9 & -5 & \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n \t4 & 5 & 3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\t\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t-3 & -4 & 3 & \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n \t-12 & -24 & -13 & \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n \t-16 & -11 & -17 & \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n \t\\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n<p>Consider the matrices $A$ and $B$ above. By applying three matrix elementary column operations </p>\n<ol class=\"questionStatements\">\n<li>  $C_1:=C_1-4C_4$ </li>\n<li>  $C_2:= \\ldots$ </li>\n<li>  $C_3:= \\ldots$ </li>\n</ol>\n\n<p>each of the form $C_i:=C_i+kC_4,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the missing elementary column operations? </p>\n",
          "question_text": "\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 1 & 6 & \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n8 & 1 & 2 & \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n0 & 9 & -5 & \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n4 & 5 & 3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB= \\begin{bmatrix}\n-3 & -4 & 3 & \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n-12 & -24 & -13 & \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n-16 & -11 & -17 & \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nConsider the matrices $A$ and $B$ above. By applying three matrix elementary column operations\n$C_1:=C_1-4C_4$\n$C_2:= \\ldots$\n$C_3:= \\ldots$\neach of the form $C_i:=C_i+kC_4,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the missing elementary column operations?",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries to the left of $a_{44}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$</p>\n<p>To do this, we can apply the following elementary column operations:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\tA\n\t&=\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t1 & 1 & 6 & \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n \t8 & 1 & 2 & \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n \t0 & 9 & -5 & \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n \t4 & 5 & 3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tC_1:=C_1-4C_4\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t-3 & 1 & 6 & \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n \t-12 & 1 & 2 & \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n \t-16 & 9 & -5 & \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n \t\\color{red}0 & 5 & 3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tC_2:=C_2-5C_4\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t-3 & -4 & 6 & \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n \t-12 & -24 & 2 & \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n \t-16 & -11 & -5 & \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n \t\\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & 3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tC_3:=C_3-3C_4\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t-3 & -4 & 3 & \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n \t-12 & -24 & -13 & \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n \t-16 & -11 & -17 & \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n \t\\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\t\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&= B.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the corresponding elementary column operations are</p>\n<p>$\\qquad$ $C_2:=C_2-5C_4\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-3C_4.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries to the left of $a_{44}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary column operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 1 & 6 & \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n8 & 1 & 2 & \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n0 & 9 & -5 & \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n4 & 5 & 3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_1:=C_1-4C_4\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-3 & 1 & 6 & \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n-12 & 1 & 2 & \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n-16 & 9 & -5 & \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 & 5 & 3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_2:=C_2-5C_4\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-3 & -4 & 6 & \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n-12 & -24 & 2 & \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n-16 & -11 & -5 & \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & 3 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_3:=C_3-3C_4\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-3 & -4 & 3 & \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n-12 & -24 & -13 & \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n-16 & -11 & -17 & \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&= B.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the corresponding elementary column operations are\n$\\qquad$ $C_2:=C_2-5C_4\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-3C_4.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",

              "html": "$C_2:=C_2-5C_4\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-3C_4$",
