        {
          "number": 8,
          "question_id": 91213,
          "task_id": 2831736,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 3029,
          "topic_name": "Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination",
          "example_id": 8049,
          "example_title": "Using Column Operations to Create a Row of Zeros in a 4x4 Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T05:40:07.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 5:40 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 10697,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:11",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>\\[A =\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t1 & 5 & \\color{blue}1&  3\\\\\n \t\t3 & 9 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & 4\\\\\n \t\t4 & 0 & \\color{blue}1 & 7\\\\\n \t\t2 & 3 & \\color{blue}0 & 1\n\t\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t-2 & -4 & \\color{blue}1&  -1\\\\\n \t\t\\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{red}0\\\\\n \t\t1 & -9 & \\color{blue}1 & 3\\\\\n \t\t2 & 3 & \\color{blue}0 & 1\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n<p>Consider the matrices $A$ and $B$ above. By applying three matrix elementary column operations </p>\n<ol class=\"questionStatements\">\n<li>  $C_1:=C_1 - 3C_3$ </li>\n<li>  $C_2:= \\ldots$ </li>\n<li>  $C_4:= \\ldots$ </li>\n</ol>\n\n<p>each of the form $C_i:=C_i+kC_3,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the missing elementary column operations? </p>\n",
          "question_text": "\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 5 & \\color{blue}1& 3\\\\\n3 & 9 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & 4\\\\\n4 & 0 & \\color{blue}1 & 7\\\\\n2 & 3 & \\color{blue}0 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB= \\begin{bmatrix}\n-2 & -4 & \\color{blue}1& -1\\\\\n\\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{red}0\\\\\n1 & -9 & \\color{blue}1 & 3\\\\\n2 & 3 & \\color{blue}0 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nConsider the matrices $A$ and $B$ above. By applying three matrix elementary column operations\n$C_1:=C_1 - 3C_3$\n$C_2:= \\ldots$\n$C_4:= \\ldots$\neach of the form $C_i:=C_i+kC_3,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the missing elementary column operations?",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries to the left and to the right of $a_{23}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$</p>\n<p>To do this, we can apply the following elementary column operations:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\tA\n\t&=\n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t1 & 5 & \\color{blue}1&  3\\\\\n \t\t3 & 9 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & 4\\\\\n \t\t4 & 0 & \\color{blue}1 & 7\\\\\n \t\t2 & 3 & \\color{blue}0 & 1\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tC_1:=C_1-3C_3\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t-2 & 5 & \\color{blue}1&  3\\\\\n \t\t\\color{red}0 & 9 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & 4\\\\\n \t\t1 & 0 & \\color{blue}1 & 7\\\\\n \t\t2 & 3 & \\color{blue}0 & 1\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tC_2:=C_2-9C_3\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t-2 & -4 & \\color{blue}1&  3\\\\\n \t\t\\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & 4\\\\\n \t\t1 & -9 & \\color{blue}1 & 7\\\\\n \t\t2 & 3 & \\color{blue}0 & 1\n\t\\end{bmatrix}\n\t&& \\qquad\n\t\t\\begin{array}{l}\n\t\t\tC_4:=C_4-4C_3\n\t\t\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&\\sim  \n\t\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t\t-2 & -4 & \\color{blue}1&  -1\\\\\n \t\t\\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{red}0\\\\\n \t\t1 & -9 & \\color{blue}1 & 3\\\\\n \t\t2 & 3 & \\color{blue}0 & 1\n\t\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&= B.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the corresponding elementary column operations are</p>\n<p>$\\qquad$ $C_2:=C_2-9C_3\\quad$ and $\\quad C_4:=C_4-4C_3.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries to the left and to the right of $a_{23}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary column operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 5 & \\color{blue}1& 3\\\\\n3 & 9 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & 4\\\\\n4 & 0 & \\color{blue}1 & 7\\\\\n2 & 3 & \\color{blue}0 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_1:=C_1-3C_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-2 & 5 & \\color{blue}1& 3\\\\\n\\color{red}0 & 9 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & 4\\\\\n1 & 0 & \\color{blue}1 & 7\\\\\n2 & 3 & \\color{blue}0 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_2:=C_2-9C_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-2 & -4 & \\color{blue}1& 3\\\\\n\\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & 4\\\\\n1 & -9 & \\color{blue}1 & 7\\\\\n2 & 3 & \\color{blue}0 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&& \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_4:=C_4-4C_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-2 & -4 & \\color{blue}1& -1\\\\\n\\color{red}0 & \\color{red}0 & \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{red}0\\\\\n1 & -9 & \\color{blue}1 & 3\\\\\n2 & 3 & \\color{blue}0 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&= B.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the corresponding elementary column operations are\n$\\qquad$ $C_2:=C_2-9C_3\\quad$ and $\\quad C_4:=C_4-4C_3.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$C_2:=C_2-9C_3\\quad$ and $\\quad C_4:=C_4-4C_3$",

            {

            },
            {
              "number": 4,

            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$C_2:=C_2+4C_3\\quad$ and $\\quad C_4:=C_4+9C_3$",
              "text": "$C_2:=C_2+4C_3\\quad$ and $\\quad C_4:=C_4+9C_3$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$C_2:=C_2-9C_3\\quad$ and $\\quad C_4:=C_4-4C_3$",
            "text": "$C_2:=C_2-9C_3\\quad$ and $\\quad C_4:=C_4-4C_3$",

      "question_count": 6,
      "questions": [
        {
          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 1092,
          "task_id": 2830904,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 233,
          "topic_name": "The Minors of a 3x3 Matrix",
          "example_id": 4439,
          "example_title": "Calculating a Minor in a 3x3 Matrix With Integer Entries",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T00:04:38.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 12:04 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 13717,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:14",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Calculate the minor of the entry $b_{22}$ in the matrix\n$\n   B= \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        3 & 13 & -6 \\\\\n        2 & 7 & 0 \\\\\n\t5 & 0 & 4\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack.\n$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Calculate the minor of the entry $b_{22}$ in the matrix\n$\nB= \\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 & 13 & -6 \\\\\n2 & 7 & 0 \\\\\n5 & 0 & 4\n}\n\\right\\rbrack.\n$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The minor of $b_{22} = 7$ (second row, second column) is the determinant of the remaining entries when the column and row containing $7$ are removed: </p>\n<p>\\[\n{B} = \n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n     3 & {\\color{gray}\\ast} & -6 \\\\\n        {\\color{gray}\\ast} & \\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}7}  & {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n\t5 & {\\color{gray}\\ast} & 4\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n\\] </p>\n<p>So, the minor of $b_{22}=7$ is\n\\begin{align*}\n\t\\text{det}\n\t\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{cc} \n\t\t3 & -6 \\\\\n\t\t5 & 4\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right]\n&=\n\n\\left|\n\\begin{array}{cc} \n       3 & -6 \\\\\n\t5 & 4\n\\end{array}\n\\right| \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&=\n\t3 \\cdot 4 - (-6) \\cdot 5 \\\\[5pt] \n        &= 12 - (-30) \\\\[5pt] \n        &= 42.\n\\end{align*} </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The minor of $b_{22} = 7$ (second row, second column) is the determinant of the remaining entries when the column and row containing $7$ are removed:\n\\[\n{B} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 & {\\color{gray}\\ast} & -6 \\\\\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} & \\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}7} & {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n5 & {\\color{gray}\\ast} & 4\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\]\nSo, the minor of $b_{22}=7$ is\n\\begin{align*}\n\\text{det}\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc}\n3 & -6 \\\\\n5 & 4\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n&=\n\\left|\n\\begin{array}{cc}\n3 & -6 \\\\\n5 & 4\n\\end{array}\n\\right| \\\\[5pt]\n&=\n3 \\cdot 4 - (-6) \\cdot 5 \\\\[5pt]\n&= 12 - (-30) \\\\[5pt]\n&= 42.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$42$",
              "text": "$42$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$-18$",
              "text": "$-18$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$28$",
              "text": "$28$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$-42$",
              "text": "$-42$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$3$",
              "text": "$3$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {

            "html": "$42$",
            "text": "$42$",
            "free_response": [],

        {
          "number": 2,
          "question_id": 69858,
          "task_id": 2830904,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 233,
          "topic_name": "The Minors of a 3x3 Matrix",
          "example_id": 4439,
          "example_title": "Calculating a Minor in a 3x3 Matrix With Integer Entries",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T00:05:19.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 12:05 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 37546,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:38",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Calculate the minor of the entry $d_{33}$ in the matrix\n$\n    {D} = \n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        3 & -2 & 4 \\\\\n        4 & -1 & 54 \\\\\n\t11 & 6 & 7\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack.\n$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Calculate the minor of the entry $d_{33}$ in the matrix\n$\n{D} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 & -2 & 4 \\\\\n4 & -1 & 54 \\\\\n11 & 6 & 7\n}\n\\right\\rbrack.\n$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The minor of $d_{33} = 7$ (third row, third column) is the determinant of the remaining entries when the column and row containing $7$ are removed: </p>\n<p>\\[\n    {D} = \n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        3 & -2 & {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n        4 & -1 & {\\color{gray}\\ast}  \\\\\n\t{\\color{gray}\\ast} & {\\color{gray}\\ast} & \\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}7}\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n\\]</p>\n<p>So, the minor of $d_{33}=7$ is</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\\text{det}\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc} \n\t3 & -2 \\\\\n\t4 & -1\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \n\n&=\n\\left|\n\\begin{array}{cc} \n        3 & -2 \\\\\n\t4 & -1\n\\end{array}\n\\right| \\\\[5pt]\n\n&= 3 \\cdot (-1) - (-2) \\cdot 4 \\\\[5pt]\n\n&= -3 - (-8) \\\\[5pt]\n\n&= 5.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The minor of $d_{33} = 7$ (third row, third column) is the determinant of the remaining entries when the column and row containing $7$ are removed:\n\\[\n{D} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 & -2 & {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n4 & -1 & {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} & {\\color{gray}\\ast} & \\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}7}\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\]\nSo, the minor of $d_{33}=7$ is\n\\begin{align*}\n\\text{det}\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc}\n3 & -2 \\\\\n4 & -1\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n&=\n\\left|\n\\begin{array}{cc}\n3 & -2 \\\\\n4 & -1\n\\end{array}\n\\right| \\\\[5pt]\n&= 3 \\cdot (-1) - (-2) \\cdot 4 \\\\[5pt]\n&= -3 - (-8) \\\\[5pt]\n&= 5.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$5$",
              "text": "$5$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$40$",
              "text": "$40$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$35$",
              "text": "$35$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$-23$",
              "text": "$-23$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$-38$",
              "text": "$-38$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$5$",
            "text": "$5$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null

          "hints": [],

        {
          "number": 3,
          "question_id": 69761,
          "task_id": 2830904,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 233,
          "topic_name": "The Minors of a 3x3 Matrix",
          "example_id": 4440,
          "example_title": "Calculating a Minor in a 3x3 Matrix With Variable Entries",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T00:06:25.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 12:06 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 19045,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:19",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>What is the minor of $k_{31}$ in the matrix\n$\n    {K} = \n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        w & u & u^2 \\\\\n        u & w & w^2 \\\\\n\t2 & 3 & uw\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack ?\n$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "What is the minor of $k_{31}$ in the matrix\n$\n{K} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\nw & u & u^2 \\\\\nu & w & w^2 \\\\\n2 & 3 & uw\n}\n\\right\\rbrack ?\n$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, we remove the third row and first column:</p>\n<p>\\[\n    {X} = \n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n          {\\color{gray}\\ast} & u & u^2 \\\\\n         {\\color{gray}\\ast} & w & w^2 \\\\\n        \\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}2} & {\\color{gray}\\ast}& {\\color{gray}\\ast} \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack \n\\]</p>\n<p>Next, we calculate the determinant:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\t\\text{det}\n\t\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{cc} \n\t\t u & u^2 \\\\\n                 w & w^2 \n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right]\n\n&=\n\\left|\\begin{array}{cc}\n\t u & u^2 \\\\\n         w & w^2 \n\\end{array}\\right| \\\\[5pt]\n\t&=\n\n\tu \\cdot w^2 - u^2 \\cdot w \\\\[5pt] \n  &= uw^2 - wu^2\\\\[5pt]\n &= uw(w-u)\n\\end{align*} </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, we remove the third row and first column:\n\\[\n{X} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} & u & u^2 \\\\\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} & w & w^2 \\\\\n\\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}2} & {\\color{gray}\\ast}& {\\color{gray}\\ast}\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\]\nNext, we calculate the determinant:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\text{det}\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc}\nu & u^2 \\\\\nw & w^2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n&=\n\\left|\\begin{array}{cc}\nu & u^2 \\\\\nw & w^2\n\\end{array}\\right| \\\\[5pt]\n&=\nu \\cdot w^2 - u^2 \\cdot w \\\\[5pt]\n&= uw^2 - wu^2\\\\[5pt]\n&= uw(w-u)\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$uw(w-u)$ ",
              "text": "$uw(w-u)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$uw(w+u)$",
              "text": "$uw(w+u)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$uw(u-w)$",
              "text": "$uw(u-w)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$u^2w(w-u)$",
              "text": "$u^2w(w-u)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$uw^2(u-w)$",
              "text": "$uw^2(u-w)$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$uw(w-u)$ ",
            "text": "$uw(w-u)$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12875831,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        },
        {
          "number": 4,
          "question_id": 77469,
          "task_id": 2830904,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 233,
          "topic_name": "The Minors of a 3x3 Matrix",
          "example_id": 4440,
          "example_title": "Calculating a Minor in a 3x3 Matrix With Variable Entries",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T00:06:59.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 12:06 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 30889,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:31",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>What is the minor of $u_{21}$ in the matrix\n$\n    {U} = \n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -4 & j &k-2j \\\\ \n       kj & j & k \\\\\n\t7 & -k & j\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack ?\n$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "What is the minor of $u_{21}$ in the matrix\n$\n{U} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-4 & j &k-2j \\\\\nkj & j & k \\\\\n7 & -k & j\n}\n\\right\\rbrack ?\n$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, we remove the second row and the first column: </p>\n<p>\\[\n    {U} = \n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix { \n  {\\color{gray}\\ast}  & j &k-2j \\\\\n      {\\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}kj}}  & {\\color{gray}\\ast}  &  {\\color{gray}\\ast}  \\\\\n\t\\color{gray}\\ast  & -k & j}\n    \\right\\rbrack  \n\\]</p>\n<p>Next, we calculate the determinant:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\\require{cancel}\n\t\\text{det}\n\t\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{cc} \n\t         j & k-2j \\\\\n               - k & j   \n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right]\n&=\n\\left|\\begin{array}{cc}\n\t j& k-2j \\\\\n      -k & j   \n\\end{array}\\right| \\\\[5pt] \n\t&=\nj \\cdot j- ( -k)\\cdot (k-2j)\\\\[5pt]   \n &= j^2+k(k-2j ) \\\\[5pt]\n  &= j^2+k^2 -k2j \\\\[5pt]\n  &= j^2-2jk + k^2\\\\[5pt]\n&= (j-k)^2\n\\end{align*} </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, we remove the second row and the first column:\n\\[\n{U} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} & j &k-2j \\\\\n{\\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}kj}} & {\\color{gray}\\ast} & {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n\\color{gray}\\ast & -k & j}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\]\nNext, we calculate the determinant:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\require{cancel}\n\\text{det}\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc}\nj & k-2j \\\\\n- k & j\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n&=\n\\left|\\begin{array}{cc}\nj& k-2j \\\\\n-k & j\n\\end{array}\\right| \\\\[5pt]\n&=\nj \\cdot j- ( -k)\\cdot (k-2j)\\\\[5pt]\n&= j^2+k(k-2j ) \\\\[5pt]\n&= j^2+k^2 -k2j \\\\[5pt]\n&= j^2-2jk + k^2\\\\[5pt]\n&= (j-k)^2\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$(j-k)^2$",
              "text": "$(j-k)^2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$j+k$",
              "text": "$j+k$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$j^2-k^2$",
              "text": "$j^2-k^2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$j^2-2k^2$",
              "text": "$j^2-2k^2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$2(k-j)$",
              "text": "$2(k-j)$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$(j-k)^2$",
            "text": "$(j-k)^2$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12875852,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        },
        {
          "number": 5,
          "question_id": 18239,
          "task_id": 2830904,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 233,
          "topic_name": "The Minors of a 3x3 Matrix",
          "example_id": 4441,
          "example_title": "Solving for an Unknown Given the Value of a Minor",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T00:08:03.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 12:08 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 28822,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:29",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that the minor of $c_{23}$ is $-86$, find all the possible values of $x$ in the matrix\n\\[\nC =\\left[\\begin{array}{ccc}\n\t-3 & 7&-1\\\\\n\t4 & -4& 5\\\\\n\t11 &x &6\n\t\\end{array}\\right].\n\\]</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that the minor of $c_{23}$ is $-86$, find all the possible values of $x$ in the matrix\n\\[\nC =\\left[\\begin{array}{ccc}\n-3 & 7&-1\\\\\n4 & -4& 5\\\\\n11 &x &6\n\\end{array}\\right].\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, we remove the second row and third column:</p>\n<p>\\[\nC=\n\\left[\\begin{array}{ccc}\n-3 & 7 & {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} & {\\color{gray}\\ast} & \\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}5} \\\\\n11 & x & {\\color{gray}\\ast}\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]</p>\n<p>The minor of $c_{23}$ is</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\\textrm{det}\\left[\\begin{array}{cc}\n-3 & 7\\\\\n11 & x \n\\end{array} \\right] \n&= \\left|\\begin{array}{cc}\n-3 & 7\\\\\n11 & x \n\\end{array}\\right| \\\\[5pt] \n&=(-3) \\cdot x - 7 \\cdot 11 \\\\[5pt] \n&=-3x-77.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>We&#39;re told that the minor of $c_{23}$ is $-86$, so we must have:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n-3x-77 &=-86 \\\\[3pt]\n-3x&=-9 \\\\[3pt]\nx &= 3\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the solution is $x=3.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, we remove the second row and third column:\n\\[\nC=\n\\left[\\begin{array}{ccc}\n-3 & 7 & {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} & {\\color{gray}\\ast} & \\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}5} \\\\\n11 & x & {\\color{gray}\\ast}\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]\nThe minor of $c_{23}$ is\n\\begin{align*}\n\\textrm{det}\\left[\\begin{array}{cc}\n-3 & 7\\\\\n11 & x\n\\end{array} \\right]\n&= \\left|\\begin{array}{cc}\n-3 & 7\\\\\n11 & x\n\\end{array}\\right| \\\\[5pt]\n&=(-3) \\cdot x - 7 \\cdot 11 \\\\[5pt]\n&=-3x-77.\n\\end{align*}\nWe're told that the minor of $c_{23}$ is $-86$, so we must have:\n\\begin{align*}\n-3x-77 &=-86 \\\\[3pt]\n-3x&=-9 \\\\[3pt]\nx &= 3\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the solution is $x=3.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$x=3$ ",
              "text": "$x=3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$x=9$ ",
              "text": "$x=9$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$x=-9$ ",
              "text": "$x=-9$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$x=-3$ ",
              "text": "$x=-3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$x=-12$ ",
              "text": "$x=-12$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$x=3$ ",
            "text": "$x=3$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },

        {
          "number": 6,
          "question_id": 42614,
          "task_id": 2830904,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 233,
          "topic_name": "The Minors of a 3x3 Matrix",
          "example_id": 4441,
          "example_title": "Solving for an Unknown Given the Value of a Minor",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-13T00:11:59.000Z",
          "created_display": "Mon, Jan 13th @ 12:11 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 233429,
          "time_elapsed_display": "3:53",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that the minor of $m_{22}$ is $3k^2-45$, find all the possible values of $k$ in the matrix\n\\[\nM=\\left[\\begin{array}{ccc}\n2 & 5&1\\\\\n3 & -4& -1\\\\\n9k-15 &9 &-3\n\\end{array}\\right].\n\\]</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that the minor of $m_{22}$ is $3k^2-45$, find all the possible values of $k$ in the matrix\n\\[\nM=\\left[\\begin{array}{ccc}\n2 & 5&1\\\\\n3 & -4& -1\\\\\n9k-15 &9 &-3\n\\end{array}\\right].\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, we remove the second row and second column:</p>\n<p>\\[\nM=\n\\left[\\begin{array}{ccc}\n2 & {\\color{gray}\\ast} & 1 \\\\\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} & \\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}-4} & {\\color{gray}\\ast}  \\\\\n9k-15 & {\\color{gray}\\ast} & -3\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]</p>\n<p>The minor of $m_{22}$ is</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\\textrm{det}\\left[\\begin{array}{cc}\n2 & 1\\\\\n9k-15 &-3 \n\\end{array} \\right] &= \\left|\\begin{array}{cc}\n2 & 1\\\\\n9k-15 &-3 \n\\end{array}\\right|\\\\[5pt]\n&= 2\\cdot (-3) - (9k-15)\\cdot 1 \\\\[5pt]\n&=-6 - (9k-15)\\\\[5pt]\n&= -9k+9.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>We&#39;re told that the minor of $m_{22}$ is $3k^2-45$, so we must have:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n-9k+9 &=3k^2-45\\\\[3pt] \n-3k+3 &=k^2-15\\\\[3pt]\nk^2+3k-18 &=0\\\\[3pt]\n(k-3)(k+6) &=0\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the solutions are $k=3$ and $k=-6.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, we remove the second row and second column:\n\\[\nM=\n\\left[\\begin{array}{ccc}\n2 & {\\color{gray}\\ast} & 1 \\\\\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} & \\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}-4} & {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n9k-15 & {\\color{gray}\\ast} & -3\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]\nThe minor of $m_{22}$ is\n\\begin{align*}\n\\textrm{det}\\left[\\begin{array}{cc}\n2 & 1\\\\\n9k-15 &-3\n\\end{array} \\right] &= \\left|\\begin{array}{cc}\n2 & 1\\\\\n9k-15 &-3\n\\end{array}\\right|\\\\[5pt]\n&= 2\\cdot (-3) - (9k-15)\\cdot 1 \\\\[5pt]\n&=-6 - (9k-15)\\\\[5pt]\n&= -9k+9.\n\\end{align*}\nWe're told that the minor of $m_{22}$ is $3k^2-45$, so we must have:\n\\begin{align*}\n-9k+9 &=3k^2-45\\\\[3pt]\n-3k+3 &=k^2-15\\\\[3pt]\nk^2+3k-18 &=0\\\\[3pt]\n(k-3)(k+6) &=0\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the solutions are $k=3$ and $k=-6.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$k=3$ and $k=-6$ only",
              "text": "$k=3$ and $k=-6$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$k=3$ and $k=6$ only",
              "text": "$k=3$ and $k=6$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$k=-6$ only",
              "text": "$k=-6$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$k=8$ and $k=-6$ only",
              "text": "$k=8$ and $k=-6$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$k=-3$ and $k=6$ only",
              "text": "$k=-3$ and $k=6$ only"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$k=3$ and $k=-6$ only",
            "text": "$k=3$ and $k=-6$ only",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12876053,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        }
      ]
    },

      "question_count": 9,
      "questions": [
        {
          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 129205,
          "task_id": 2831474,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 2083,
          "topic_name": "Row Echelon Form",
          "example_id": 6166,
          "example_title": "Identifying the Leading Entries of a Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:44:17.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:44 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 15832,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:16",
          "result": "incorrect",
          "correct": false,
          "question_html": "<p>What is the sum of the leading entries of the $1$st, $2$nd, and $4$th rows in the matrix $A,$ given below?\n\\[\nA=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t0 & 0 & 7 & -2 \\\\\n\t0 & -1 & 0 & 1 \\\\\n\t4 & 0 & -6 & 9 \\\\\n\t0 & 0 & 0 & -3\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "question_text": "What is the sum of the leading entries of the $1$st, $2$nd, and $4$th rows in the matrix $A,$ given below?\n\\[\nA=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 & 0 & 7 & -2 \\\\\n0 & -1 & 0 & 1 \\\\\n4 & 0 & -6 & 9 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & -3\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The first non-zero entry of each row (from left to right) is the leading entry. </p>\n<p>Therefore, the leading entries of the $1$st, $2$nd, and $4$th rows of $A$ are $7$, $-1$, and $-3$, respectively.</p>\n<p>\\[\nA\n=\\begin{bmatrix} \n\t0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{7} & -2 \\\\\n\t0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-1} & 0 & 1 \\\\\n\t4 & 0 & -6 & 9 \\\\\n\t0 & 0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-3}\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n<p>So, the answer is $7+(-1)+(-3)=3.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The first non-zero entry of each row (from left to right) is the leading entry.\nTherefore, the leading entries of the $1$st, $2$nd, and $4$th rows of $A$ are $7$, $-1$, and $-3$, respectively.\n\\[\nA\n=\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{7} & -2 \\\\\n0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-1} & 0 & 1 \\\\\n4 & 0 & -6 & 9 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-3}\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nSo, the answer is $7+(-1)+(-3)=3.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$3$",
              "text": "$3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$10$",
              "text": "$10$"

              "number": 3,

              "html": "$-6$",
              "text": "$-6$"
            },

              "number": 5,
              "html": "$-4$",
              "text": "$-4$"
            }
          ],

        {
          "number": 2,
          "question_id": 42364,
          "task_id": 2831474,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 2083,
          "topic_name": "Row Echelon Form",
          "example_id": 6166,
          "example_title": "Identifying the Leading Entries of a Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:45:35.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:45 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 72437,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:12",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>What is the sum of the leading entries of the $2$nd and $4$th rows in the matrix $A,$ given below?\n\\[\nA=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t3 & 2 & 5 & -9\\\\\n\t0 & 0 & -4 & 1 \\\\\n\t0 & 0 & 0 & 5 \\\\\n\t0 & 7 & 7 & -3\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "question_text": "What is the sum of the leading entries of the $2$nd and $4$th rows in the matrix $A,$ given below?\n\\[\nA=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n3 & 2 & 5 & -9\\\\\n0 & 0 & -4 & 1 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 5 \\\\\n0 & 7 & 7 & -3\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The first non-zero entry of each row (from left to right) is the leading entry. </p>\n<p>Therefore, the leading entries of the $2$nd and $4$th rows of  $A$ are $-4$ and $7$, respectively.</p>\n<p>\\[\nA\n=\\begin{bmatrix} \n\t3 & 2 & 5 & -9\\\\\n\t0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-4} & 1 \\\\\n\t0 & 0 & 0 & 5 \\\\\n\t0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{7} & 7 & -3\n\\end{bmatrix}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>So, the answer is $-4+7=3.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The first non-zero entry of each row (from left to right) is the leading entry.\nTherefore, the leading entries of the $2$nd and $4$th rows of $A$ are $-4$ and $7$, respectively.\n\\[\nA\n=\\begin{bmatrix}\n3 & 2 & 5 & -9\\\\\n0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-4} & 1 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 5 \\\\\n0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{7} & 7 & -3\n\\end{bmatrix}.\n\\]\nSo, the answer is $-4+7=3.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$3$",
              "text": "$3$"

        {
          "number": 3,
          "question_id": 73148,
          "task_id": 2831474,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 2083,
          "topic_name": "Row Echelon Form",
          "example_id": 6166,
          "example_title": "Identifying the Leading Entries of a Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:46:57.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:46 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 77379,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:17",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>What is the sum of the leading entries of the $1$st, $2$nd, and $3$rd rows in the matrix $A,$ given below?\n\\[\nA=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\t0 & -7 & 2 & 0\\\\\n\t0 & 0 & 5 & 6 \\\\\n\t3 & 0 & 2 & -1 \\\\\n\t-1 & 0 & 0 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "question_text": "What is the sum of the leading entries of the $1$st, $2$nd, and $3$rd rows in the matrix $A,$ given below?\n\\[\nA=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 & -7 & 2 & 0\\\\\n0 & 0 & 5 & 6 \\\\\n3 & 0 & 2 & -1 \\\\\n-1 & 0 & 0 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The first non-zero entry of each row (from left to right) is the leading entry. </p>\n<p>Therefore, the leading entries of the $1$st, $2$nd, and $3$rd rows of $A$ are $-7$, $5$, and $3$, respectively.</p>\n<p>\\[\nA\n=\\begin{bmatrix} \n\t0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-7} & 2 & 0 \\\\\n\t0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} & 6 \\\\\n\t\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} &  0 & 2 & -1 \\\\\n\t-1 & 0 & 0 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n<p>So, the answer is $-7+5+3=1.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The first non-zero entry of each row (from left to right) is the leading entry.\nTherefore, the leading entries of the $1$st, $2$nd, and $3$rd rows of $A$ are $-7$, $5$, and $3$, respectively.\n\\[\nA\n=\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-7} & 2 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} & 6 \\\\\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} & 0 & 2 & -1 \\\\\n-1 & 0 & 0 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nSo, the answer is $-7+5+3=1.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$1$",
              "text": "$1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$10$",
              "text": "$10$"

          ],
          "student_answer": {

          "hints": [],

        {
          "number": 4,
          "question_id": 72491,
          "task_id": 2831474,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 2083,
          "topic_name": "Row Echelon Form",
          "example_id": 4688,
          "example_title": "Identifying Matrices in Row Echelon Form",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:50:19.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:50 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 57893,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:58",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Which of the following matrices are <strong>not</strong> in row echelon form?</p>\n<p>\\[\n\tA= \\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{cccc}\n\t\t6 & 0 & 3 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 3 & 1 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 2 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 6 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 0 \\\\\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right],\n\t\\quad\n\tB= \\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{cccccc}\n\t\t1 & 0 & 5 & 1 & -5 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 2 & 1 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 0 & 4 \n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right],\n\t\\quad \n\tC= \\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc}\n\t5 & 5 & 3 & 4 \\\\\n\t0 & 1 & 5 & 3 \\\\\n\t0 & 2 & 2 & 2 \\\\\n\t0 & 0 & -7 & 4 \n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right].\n\\]</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Which of the following matrices are not in row echelon form?\n\\[\nA= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{cccc}\n6 & 0 & 3 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 3 & 1 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 2 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 6 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 0 \\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nB= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{cccccc}\n1 & 0 & 5 & 1 & -5 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 2 & 1 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 0 & 4\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nC= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc}\n5 & 5 & 3 & 4 \\\\\n0 & 1 & 5 & 3 \\\\\n0 & 2 & 2 & 2 \\\\\n0 & 0 & -7 & 4\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Remember that a matrix is in row echelon form if it satisfies the following conditions:</p>\n<ol class=\"questionStatements\">\n<li>  All the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the <em>bottom</em> of the matrix. </li>\n<li>  Each leading entry of a row is in a column <em>to the right</em> of the leading entry of the row above it. </li>\n</ol>\n\n<p>With that in mind, let&#39;s look at each matrix in turn.</p>\n<ul>\n<li><p>The matrix $A$ is not in row echelon form since the leading entry $a_{43} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated). \n\\[\nA= \\left[\\begin{array}{cccc}\n\t\t \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{6} & 0 & 3 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} & 1 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{{\\color{red}6}} & {\\color{blue}0} \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0}\n\t\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>The matrix $B$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nB= \\left[\\begin{array}{cccccc}\n\t\t\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 0 & 5 & 1 & -5 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} & 1 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{4} \n\t\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>The matrix $C$ is not in row echelon form since the leading entry $c_{32} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated). \n\\[\nC=\\begin{bmatrix} \n\t\t\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} & 5 & 3 & 4 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 5 & 3 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{{\\color{red}2}} & 2 & 2 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-7} & 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Therefore, only $A$ and $C$ are <strong>not</strong> in row echelon form.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Remember that a matrix is in row echelon form if it satisfies the following conditions:\nAll the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the bottom of the matrix.\nEach leading entry of a row is in a column to the right of the leading entry of the row above it.\nWith that in mind, let's look at each matrix in turn.\nThe matrix $A$ is not in row echelon form since the leading entry $a_{43} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated).\n\\[\nA= \\left[\\begin{array}{cccc}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{6} & 0 & 3 & 0 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} & 1 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{{\\color{red}6}} & {\\color{blue}0} \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0}\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $B$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nB= \\left[\\begin{array}{cccccc}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 0 & 5 & 1 & -5 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} & 1 & 0 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{4}\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $C$ is not in row echelon form since the leading entry $c_{32} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated).\n\\[\nC=\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} & 5 & 3 & 4 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 5 & 3 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{{\\color{red}2}} & 2 & 2 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-7} & 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nTherefore, only $A$ and $C$ are not in row echelon form.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",

            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$A$ and $C$ only",
              "text": "$A$ and $C$ only"

              "number": 2,

            {

              "html": "$B$ and $C$ only",
              "text": "$B$ and $C$ only"

          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$A$ and $C$ only",
            "text": "$A$ and $C$ only",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12875020,

          }
        },
        {
          "number": 5,
          "question_id": 72492,
          "task_id": 2831474,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 2083,
          "topic_name": "Row Echelon Form",
          "example_id": 4688,
          "example_title": "Identifying Matrices in Row Echelon Form",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:51:46.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:51 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 70993,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:11",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Which of the following matrices are <strong>not</strong> in row echelon form?</p>\n<p>\\[\n\tA= \\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc}\n\t\t1 & 3 & 5 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right],\n\t\\quad\n\tB= \\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccccc}\n\t\t1 & 0 & 0 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 5 & 0 & 4 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 2 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 0\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right],\n\t\\quad \n\tC= \\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc}\n\t0 & 2 & 3 \\\\\n\t0 & -1 & -2 \\\\\n\t0 & 0 & -1 \\\\\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right].\n\\]</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Which of the following matrices are not in row echelon form?\n\\[\nA= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc}\n1 & 3 & 5 \\\\\n0 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nB= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccccc}\n1 & 0 & 0 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 5 & 0 & 4 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 2 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nC= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc}\n0 & 2 & 3 \\\\\n0 & -1 & -2 \\\\\n0 & 0 & -1 \\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Remember that a matrix is in row echelon form if it satisfies the following conditions:</p>\n<ol class=\"questionStatements\">\n<li>  All the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the <em>bottom</em> of the matrix. </li>\n<li>  Each leading entry of a row is in a column <em>to the right</em> of the leading entry of the row above it. </li>\n</ol>\n\n<p>With that in mind, let&#39;s look at each matrix in turn.</p>\n<ul>\n<li><p>The matrix $A$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nA= \\left[\\begin{array}{cccc}\n\t\t\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 3 & 5 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 2 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0}\n\t\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>The matrix $B$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nB= \\left[\\begin{array}{cccccc}\n\t\t\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 0 & 0 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} & 0 & 4 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0}\n\t\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>The matrix $C$ is not in row echelon form since the leading entry $c_{22} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated). \n\\[\nC=\\begin{bmatrix} \n\t0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} & 3 \\\\\n\t0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{\\color{red}-1} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{ -2} \\\\\n\t0 & 0 & -1 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Therefore, only $C$ is <strong>not</strong> in row echelon form.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Remember that a matrix is in row echelon form if it satisfies the following conditions:\nAll the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the bottom of the matrix.\nEach leading entry of a row is in a column to the right of the leading entry of the row above it.\nWith that in mind, let's look at each matrix in turn.\nThe matrix $A$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nA= \\left[\\begin{array}{cccc}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 3 & 5 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 2 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0}\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $B$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nB= \\left[\\begin{array}{cccccc}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 0 & 0 & 0 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} & 0 & 4 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0}\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $C$ is not in row echelon form since the leading entry $c_{22} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated).\n\\[\nC=\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} & 3 \\\\\n0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{\\color{red}-1} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{ -2} \\\\\n0 & 0 & -1 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nTherefore, only $C$ is not in row echelon form.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$C$ only",
              "text": "$C$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$A$ only",
              "text": "$A$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$B$ only",
              "text": "$B$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$A$ and $C$ only",
              "text": "$A$ and $C$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$B$ and $C$ only",
              "text": "$B$ and $C$ only"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$C$ only",
            "text": "$C$ only",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],

        {
          "number": 6,
          "question_id": 129227,
          "task_id": 2831474,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 2083,
          "topic_name": "Row Echelon Form",
          "example_id": 4687,
          "example_title": "Identifying Augmented Matrices in Row Echelon Form",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:53:21.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:53 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 13287,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:13",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Which of the following augmented matrices are in row echelon form?\n\\[\n\tA=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t1 & 0 & 9 & 2 \\\\\n\t\t0 & -2 & 0 & 7 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 5 & 0 \\\\\n                0 & 0 & 0 & 0\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right],\n\t\\quad\n\tB=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t8 & 0 & 7 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & -9 & 0 & 0 \\\\\n                0 & 0 & 1 & 0\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right],\n\t\\quad \n\tC=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t0 & -5 & 0 & 3 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 6 & 7 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 9 \n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right].\n\\]</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Which of the following augmented matrices are in row echelon form?\n\\[\nA=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1 & 0 & 9 & 2 \\\\\n0 & -2 & 0 & 7 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 5 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nB=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n8 & 0 & 7 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 0 \\\\\n0 & -9 & 0 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 1 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nC=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n0 & -5 & 0 & 3 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 6 & 7 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 9\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Remember that an augmented matrix is in row echelon form if its coefficient matrix satisfies the following conditions:</p>\n<ol class=\"questionStatements\">\n<li>  All the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the bottom of the matrix. </li>\n<li>  Each leading entry of a row is in a column to the right of the leading entry of the row above it. </li>\n</ol>\n\n<p>With that in mind, let&#39;s look at each matrix in turn.</p>\n<ul>\n<li><p>The matrix $A$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\n\tA=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 0 & 9 & 2 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-2} & 0 & 7 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} & 0 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & 0\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>The matrix $B$ is not in row echelon form since the second row contains only zeros and it is not at the bottom of the matrix (condition I is violated).\n\\[\n\tB=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{9} & 0 & 7 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{red}0} & {\\color{red}0} & {\\color{red}0} & 0 \\\\\n                0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-9} & 0 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 0\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>The matrix $C$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\n\tC=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-5} & 0 & 3 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{6} & 7 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & 9 \n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Therefore, only $A$ and $C$ are in row echelon form.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Remember that an augmented matrix is in row echelon form if its coefficient matrix satisfies the following conditions:\nAll the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the bottom of the matrix.\nEach leading entry of a row is in a column to the right of the leading entry of the row above it.\nWith that in mind, let's look at each matrix in turn.\nThe matrix $A$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nA=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 0 & 9 & 2 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-2} & 0 & 7 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} & 0 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $B$ is not in row echelon form since the second row contains only zeros and it is not at the bottom of the matrix (condition I is violated).\n\\[\nB=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{9} & 0 & 7 & 0 \\\\\n{\\color{red}0} & {\\color{red}0} & {\\color{red}0} & 0 \\\\\n0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-9} & 0 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $C$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nC=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-5} & 0 & 3 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{6} & 7 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & 9\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\]\nTherefore, only $A$ and $C$ are in row echelon form.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$A$ and $C$ only",
              "text": "$A$ and $C$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$A$ and $B$ only",
              "text": "$A$ and $B$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$B$ and $C$ only",
              "text": "$B$ and $C$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$A,$ $B,$ and $C$ ",
              "text": "$A,$ $B,$ and $C$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$A$ only",
              "text": "$A$ only"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$A$ and $C$ only",
            "text": "$A$ and $C$ only",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",

        {
          "number": 7,
          "question_id": 129232,
          "task_id": 2831474,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 2083,
          "topic_name": "Row Echelon Form",
          "example_id": 4687,
          "example_title": "Identifying Augmented Matrices in Row Echelon Form",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:53:58.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:53 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 33735,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:34",
          "result": "incorrect",
          "correct": false,
          "question_html": "<p>Which of the following augmented matrices are <strong>not</strong> in row echelon form?\n\\[\n\tA=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t0 & -4 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 3 & -1 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 6 & 7 \n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right],\n\t\\quad\n\tB=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t-6 & 3 & 4 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 9 & 6 & -1 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 0 \\\\\n                0 & 0 & -4 & 6 \\\\\n                0 & 0 & 0 & 5\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right],\n\t\\quad \n\tC=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{cccc|c}\n\t\t0 & 1 & -4 & 5 & 9 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 3 & -7 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 2 & 0 & -1 \\\\\n                0 & 0 & 0 & 5 & 1\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right].\n\\]</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Which of the following augmented matrices are not in row echelon form?\n\\[\nA=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n0 & -4 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 3 & -1 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 6 & 7\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nB=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n-6 & 3 & 4 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 9 & 6 & -1 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & -4 & 6 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 5\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nC=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cccc|c}\n0 & 1 & -4 & 5 & 9 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 3 & -7 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 2 & 0 & -1 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 5 & 1\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Remember that an augmented matrix is in row echelon form if its coefficient matrix satisfies the following conditions:</p>\n<ol class=\"questionStatements\">\n<li>  All the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the <em>bottom</em> of the matrix. </li>\n<li>  Each leading entry of a row is in a column <em>to the right</em> of the leading entry of the row above it. </li>\n</ol>\n\n<p>With that in mind, let&#39;s look at each matrix in turn.</p>\n<ul>\n<li><p>The matrix $A$ is not in row echelon form since $a_{33} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated).\n\\[\n\tA=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-4} & 1 & 2 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} & -1 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{\\color{red}6} & 7\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>The matrix $B$ is not in row echelon form since the third row contains only zeros and it is not at the bottom of the matrix (condition I is violated).\n\\[\n\tB=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-6} & 3 & 4 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{9} & 6 & -1 \\\\\n                {\\color{red}0} & {\\color{red}0} & {\\color{red}0} & 0 \\\\\n                0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-4} & 6 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 5\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>The matrix $C$ is not in row echelon form since $c_{33} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated).\n\\[\n\tC=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{cccc|c}\n\t\t0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & -4 & 5 & 9 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} & -7 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{\\color{red}2} & 0 & -1 \\\\\n                0 & 0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} & 1\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Therefore, $A,$ $B,$ and $C$ are <strong>not</strong> in row echelon form.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Remember that an augmented matrix is in row echelon form if its coefficient matrix satisfies the following conditions:\nAll the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the bottom of the matrix.\nEach leading entry of a row is in a column to the right of the leading entry of the row above it.\nWith that in mind, let's look at each matrix in turn.\nThe matrix $A$ is not in row echelon form since $a_{33} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated).\n\\[\nA=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-4} & 1 & 2 \\\\\n0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} & -1 \\\\\n0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{\\color{red}6} & 7\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $B$ is not in row echelon form since the third row contains only zeros and it is not at the bottom of the matrix (condition I is violated).\n\\[\nB=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-6} & 3 & 4 & 0 \\\\\n0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{9} & 6 & -1 \\\\\n{\\color{red}0} & {\\color{red}0} & {\\color{red}0} & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-4} & 6 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 5\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $C$ is not in row echelon form since $c_{33} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated).\n\\[\nC=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cccc|c}\n0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & -4 & 5 & 9 \\\\\n0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} & -7 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{\\color{red}2} & 0 & -1 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} & 1\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\]\nTherefore, $A,$ $B,$ and $C$ are not in row echelon form.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$A,$ $B,$ and $C$",
              "text": "$A,$ $B,$ and $C$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$B$ and $C$ only",
              "text": "$B$ and $C$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$A$ and $C$ only",
              "text": "$A$ and $C$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$A$ and $B$ only",
              "text": "$A$ and $B$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$A$ only",
              "text": "$A$ only"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 2,
            "html": "$B$ and $C$ only",
            "text": "$B$ and $C$ only",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],

        },
        {
          "number": 8,
          "question_id": 129230,
          "task_id": 2831474,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 2083,
          "topic_name": "Row Echelon Form",
          "example_id": 4687,
          "example_title": "Identifying Augmented Matrices in Row Echelon Form",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:55:06.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:55 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 57345,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:57",
          "result": "incorrect",
          "correct": false,
          "question_html": "<p>Which of the following augmented matrices are in row echelon form?\n\\[\n\tA=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{cccc|c}\n\t\t-5 & 0 & 8 & 4 & 2 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 3 & -5 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & -2 & 0 & 1 \\\\\n                0 & 0 & 0 & 0 & -1\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right],\n\t\\quad\n\tB=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t4 & 0 & 6 & 8 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 3 & -1 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 2\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right],\n\t\\quad \n\tC=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t0 & -9 & 0 & -6 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 1 & -7 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 1 \\\\\n                0 & 0 & 0 & 0\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right].\n\\]</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Which of the following augmented matrices are in row echelon form?\n\\[\nA=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cccc|c}\n-5 & 0 & 8 & 4 & 2 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 3 & -5 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & -2 & 0 & 1 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 0 & -1\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nB=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n4 & 0 & 6 & 8 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 3 & -1 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nC=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n0 & -9 & 0 & -6 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 1 & -7 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 1 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Remember that an augmented matrix is in row echelon form if its coefficient matrix satisfies the following conditions:</p>\n<ol class=\"questionStatements\">\n<li>  All the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the bottom of the matrix. </li>\n<li>  Each leading entry of a row is in a column to the right of the leading entry of the row above it. </li>\n</ol>\n\n<p>With that in mind, let&#39;s look at each matrix in turn.</p>\n<ul>\n<li><p>The matrix $A$ is not in row echelon form since $a_{33} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated).\n\\[\n\tA=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{cccc|c}\n\t\t\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-5} & 0 & 8 & 4 & 2 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} & -5 & 0 \\\\\n                0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{{\\color{red}-2}} & 0 & 1 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 0 & -1\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>The matrix $B$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\n\tB=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{4} & 0 & 6 & 8 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} & -1 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & 2\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>The matrix $C$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\n\tC=\\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-9} & 0 & -6 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & -7 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & 1 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & 0\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Therefore, only $B$ and $C$ are in row echelon form.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Remember that an augmented matrix is in row echelon form if its coefficient matrix satisfies the following conditions:\nAll the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the bottom of the matrix.\nEach leading entry of a row is in a column to the right of the leading entry of the row above it.\nWith that in mind, let's look at each matrix in turn.\nThe matrix $A$ is not in row echelon form since $a_{33} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated).\n\\[\nA=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cccc|c}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-5} & 0 & 8 & 4 & 2 \\\\\n0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} & -5 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{{\\color{red}-2}} & 0 & 1 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 0 & -1\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $B$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nB=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{4} & 0 & 6 & 8 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} & -1 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $C$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nC=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-9} & 0 & -6 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & -7 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & 1 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\]\nTherefore, only $B$ and $C$ are in row echelon form.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$B$ and $C$ only",
              "text": "$B$ and $C$ only"

          "student_answer": {

        {
          "number": 9,
          "question_id": 42029,
          "task_id": 2831474,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 2083,
          "topic_name": "Row Echelon Form",
          "example_id": 4687,
          "example_title": "Identifying Augmented Matrices in Row Echelon Form",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:55:34.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:55 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 25948,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:26",
          "result": "incorrect",
          "correct": false,
          "question_html": "<p>Which of the following augmented matrices are <strong>not</strong> in row echelon form?</p>\n<p>\\[\n\tA= \\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t0 & 3 & 4 & 5 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 0\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right],\n\t\\quad\n\tB= \\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccccc|c}\n\t\t5 & 0 & 1 & 0 & 7 & 8 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 4 & 4 & 5 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 0 & 2 & 1\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right],\n\t\\quad \n\tC= \\left[\n\t\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t1 & 2 & 3 & 4 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 0 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & 2 & 4\n\t\\end{array}\n\t\\right].\n\\]</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Which of the following augmented matrices are not in row echelon form?\n\\[\nA= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n0 & 3 & 4 & 5 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 1 & 2 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nB= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccccc|c}\n5 & 0 & 1 & 0 & 7 & 8 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 4 & 4 & 5 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 0 & 2 & 1\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nC= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1 & 2 & 3 & 4 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 0 & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & 2 & 4\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Remember that an augmented matrix is in row echelon form if its coefficient matrix satisfies the following conditions:</p>\n<ol class=\"questionStatements\">\n<li>  All the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the <em>bottom</em> of the matrix. </li>\n<li>  Each leading entry of a row is in a column <em>to the right</em> of the leading entry of the row above it. </li>\n</ol>\n\n<p>With that in mind, let&#39;s look at each matrix in turn.</p>\n<ul>\n<li><p>The matrix $A$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nA= \\left[\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} & 4 & 5 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 2 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & 0\n\t\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>The matrix $B$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\n\tB= \\left[\\begin{array}{ccccc|c}\n\t\t\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} & 0 & 1 & 0 & 7 & 8 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{4} & 4 & 5 & 0 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} & 1\n\t\\end{array}\\right].\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>The matrix $C$ is not in row echelon form since the second row contains only zeros and it is not at the bottom of the matrix (condition I is violated).\n\\[\nC= \\left[\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\t\t\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 2 & 3 & 4 \\\\\n\t\t{\\color{red}0} & {\\color{red}0} & {\\color{red}0} & 0 \\\\\n\t\t0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} & 4\n\t\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]</p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Therefore, only $C$ is <strong>not</strong> in row echelon form.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Remember that an augmented matrix is in row echelon form if its coefficient matrix satisfies the following conditions:\nAll the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the bottom of the matrix.\nEach leading entry of a row is in a column to the right of the leading entry of the row above it.\nWith that in mind, let's look at each matrix in turn.\nThe matrix $A$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nA= \\left[\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n{\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} & 4 & 5 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 2 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & 0\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $B$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nB= \\left[\\begin{array}{ccccc|c}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} & 0 & 1 & 0 & 7 & 8 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{4} & 4 & 5 & 0 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}0} & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} & 1\n\\end{array}\\right].\n\\]\nThe matrix $C$ is not in row echelon form since the second row contains only zeros and it is not at the bottom of the matrix (condition I is violated).\n\\[\nC= \\left[\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} & 2 & 3 & 4 \\\\\n{\\color{red}0} & {\\color{red}0} & {\\color{red}0} & 0 \\\\\n0 & 0 & \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} & 4\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]\nTherefore, only $C$ is not in row echelon form.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$C$ only",
              "text": "$C$ only"
            },

              "number": 2,
              "html": "$A$ only",
              "text": "$A$ only"
            },
            {

            },
            {

              "html": "$A$ and $C$ only",
              "text": "$A$ and $C$ only"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$B$ and $C$ only",
              "text": "$B$ and $C$ only"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {

    {
      "task_id": 2830903,
      "task_type": "Lesson",
      "topic_id": 146,
      "topic_name": "Scalar Multiplication of Matrices",
      "course_id": null,
      "course_name": "Mathematical Foundations III",
      "completed_at": "2025-01-12T23:40:19.000Z",
      "completed_date": "Sun, Jan 12th, 2025",
      "completed_time": "11:40 PM",
      "started_at": "2025-01-12T23:34:49.000Z",
      "points_possible": 7,
      "points_awarded": 7,
      "progress": 1,
      "progress_display": "100%",
      "review_url": "/learn?taskId=2830903",

        }
      ],
      "question_count": 8,
      "questions": [
        {
          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 125881,
          "task_id": 2830903,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 146,
          "topic_name": "Scalar Multiplication of Matrices",
          "example_id": 4380,
          "example_title": "Multiplying a Matrix by a Scalar",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:35:55.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:35 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 45271,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:45",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Calculate $\\dfrac{1}{3}Q$, if \n$\n{Q} = \n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        3 & 6  \\\\\n        0 & -9 \\\\\n\t-3 & 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack.\n$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Calculate $\\dfrac{1}{3}Q$, if\n$\n{Q} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 & 6 \\\\\n0 & -9 \\\\\n-3 & 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack.\n$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>We multiply each entry of $Q$ by $\\dfrac{1}{3},$ as follows:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{1}{3} {Q}\n\n    &=\n\n\\dfrac{1}{3} \n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        3 & 6  \\\\\n        0 & -9 \\\\\n\t-3 & 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n\n   \n    &=\n\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 3 & \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 6  \\\\\n        \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 0 & \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-9) \\\\\n\t\\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-3) & \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n\n    &=\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        1 & 2  \\\\\n        0 & -3 \\\\\n\t-1 & 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "We multiply each entry of $Q$ by $\\dfrac{1}{3},$ as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{1}{3} {Q}\n&=\n\\dfrac{1}{3}\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 & 6 \\\\\n0 & -9 \\\\\n-3 & 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n\\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 3 & \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 6 \\\\\n\\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 0 & \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-9) \\\\\n\\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-3) & \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 & 2 \\\\\n0 & -3 \\\\\n-1 & 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        1 & 2  \\\\\n        0 & -3 \\\\\n\t-1 & 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 & 2 \\\\\n0 & -3 \\\\\n-1 & 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n         3 & 6  \\\\\n        0 & -9 \\\\\n\t-3 & 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 & 6 \\\\\n0 & -9 \\\\\n-3 & 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n         9 & 18  \\\\\n        0 & -27 \\\\\n\t-9 & 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n9 & 18 \\\\\n0 & -27 \\\\\n-9 & 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        1 & 2  \\\\\n        0 & 3 \\\\\n\t-1 & 1\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 & 2 \\\\\n0 & 3 \\\\\n-1 & 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        1 & 2  \\\\\n        0 & -3 \\\\\n\t-1 & \\dfrac{1}{3}\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 & 2 \\\\\n0 & -3 \\\\\n-1 & \\dfrac{1}{3}\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {

            "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        1 & 2  \\\\\n        0 & -3 \\\\\n\t-1 & 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
            "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 & 2 \\\\\n0 & -3 \\\\\n-1 & 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$",
