        {
          "number": 2,
          "question_id": 125878,
          "task_id": 2830903,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 146,
          "topic_name": "Scalar Multiplication of Matrices",
          "example_id": 4380,
          "example_title": "Multiplying a Matrix by a Scalar",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:36:08.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:36 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 10137,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:10",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>$5\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       4 & 1 & -2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n    =$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "$5\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n4 & 1 & -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n=$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>We multiply each entry of the matrix by $5$, as follows:</p>\n<p>\\begin{eqnarray}\n\\eqalign{\n5\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       4 & 1 & -2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack \n\n       &= \n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       5 \\cdot 4 & 5 \\cdot 1 & 5 \\cdot (-2)\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&=\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       20 & 5 & -10\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n}\n\\end{eqnarray}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "We multiply each entry of the matrix by $5$, as follows:\n\\begin{eqnarray}\n\\eqalign{\n5\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n4 & 1 & -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n5 \\cdot 4 & 5 \\cdot 1 & 5 \\cdot (-2)\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n20 & 5 & -10\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n}\n\\end{eqnarray}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       20 & 5 & -10\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n20 & 5 & -10\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       4 & 1 & -2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n4 & 1 & -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       -20 & -5 & 10\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-20 & -5 & 10\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       20 \\\\ 5 \\\\ -10\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n20 \\\\ 5 \\\\ -10\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       4 \\\\ 1 \\\\ -2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n4 \\\\ 1 \\\\ -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       20 & 5 & -10\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
            "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n20 & 5 & -10\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",

        {
          "number": 3,
          "question_id": 69675,
          "task_id": 2830903,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 146,
          "topic_name": "Scalar Multiplication of Matrices",
          "example_id": 4381,
          "example_title": "Calculating the Value of a Scalar",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:36:31.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:36 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 9738,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:10",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>If $k {M} =\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        0 & 6 & -3 \\\\\n        6 & -9 & 6 \\\\\n        15 & 9 & 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$,\nwhere ${M} =\n     \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        0 & 2 & -1 \\\\\n        2 & -3 & 2 \\\\\n        5 & 3 & 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$\nand $k$ is a scalar, calculate the value of $k.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "If $k {M} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n0 & 6 & -3 \\\\\n6 & -9 & 6 \\\\\n15 & 9 & 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$,\nwhere ${M} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n0 & 2 & -1 \\\\\n2 & -3 & 2 \\\\\n5 & 3 & 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$\nand $k$ is a scalar, calculate the value of $k.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To express $kM,$ we multiply each entry of $M$ by $k,$ as follows:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nk {M} &=\n    k\\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        0 & 2 & -1 \\\\\n        2 & -3 & 2 \\\\\n        5 & 3 & 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        0 & 2k & -k \\\\\n        2k & -3k & 2k \\\\\n         5k  & 3k & 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Now, we set the above result equal to the matrix given for $kM$ in the problem statement:</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        0 & 2k & -k \\\\\n        2k & -3k & 2k \\\\\n        5k  & 3k & 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n= \n   \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        0 & 6 & -3 \\\\\n        6 & -9 & 6 \\\\\n        15  & 9 & 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n    \\]</p>\n<p>We can solve for $k$ by equating any two corresponding (nonzero) elements. Let&#39;s choose the top-right elements, $-k$ and $-3.$ Equating these elements, we get</p>\n<p>\\begin{align}\n-k &= -3 \\\\[3pt]\nk &= 3.\n\\end{align}</p>\n<p><strong>Note:</strong> We would still get the same result if we chose any other two corresponding (nonzero) elements.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To express $kM,$ we multiply each entry of $M$ by $k,$ as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nk {M} &=\nk\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n0 & 2 & -1 \\\\\n2 & -3 & 2 \\\\\n5 & 3 & 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n0 & 2k & -k \\\\\n2k & -3k & 2k \\\\\n5k & 3k & 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we set the above result equal to the matrix given for $kM$ in the problem statement:\n\\[\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n0 & 2k & -k \\\\\n2k & -3k & 2k \\\\\n5k & 3k & 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n0 & 6 & -3 \\\\\n6 & -9 & 6 \\\\\n15 & 9 & 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\]\nWe can solve for $k$ by equating any two corresponding (nonzero) elements. Let's choose the top-right elements, $-k$ and $-3.$ Equating these elements, we get\n\\begin{align}\n-k &= -3 \\\\[3pt]\nk &= 3.\n\\end{align}\nNote: We would still get the same result if we chose any other two corresponding (nonzero) elements.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$k=3$",
              "text": "$k=3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$k=-3$",
              "text": "$k=-3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$k=2$",
              "text": "$k=2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$k=-2$",
              "text": "$k=-2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$k=0$",
              "text": "$k=0$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$k=3$",
            "text": "$k=3$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],

        },
        {
          "number": 4,
          "question_id": 125887,
          "task_id": 2830903,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 146,
          "topic_name": "Scalar Multiplication of Matrices",
          "example_id": 4381,
          "example_title": "Calculating the Value of a Scalar",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:36:41.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:36 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 7733,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:08",
          "result": "incorrect",
          "correct": false,
          "question_html": "<p>If $k {X} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        2 & 0 \\\\\n        -5 & -4 \\\\\n        -3 & 2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$,\nwhere ${X} =\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -4 & 0 \\\\\n        10 & 8 \\\\\n        6 & -4\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$\nand $k$ is a scalar, calculate the value of $k.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "If $k {X} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 & 0 \\\\\n-5 & -4 \\\\\n-3 & 2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$,\nwhere ${X} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-4 & 0 \\\\\n10 & 8 \\\\\n6 & -4\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$\nand $k$ is a scalar, calculate the value of $k.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To express $kX,$ we multiply each entry of $X$ by $k,$ as follows:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nk{X} &=\n    k    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -4 & 0 \\\\\n        10 & 8 \\\\\n        6 & -4\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\\\\[5pt]\n&=\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -4k & 0 \\\\\n        10k & 8k \\\\\n        6k & -4k\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Now, we set the above result equal to the matrix given for $kX$ in the problem statement:</p>\n<p>\\[    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -4k & 0 \\\\\n        10k & 8k \\\\\n        6k & -4k\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n= \n\\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        2 & 0 \\\\\n        -5 & -4 \\\\\n        -3 & 2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n    \\]</p>\n<p>We can solve for $k$ by equating any two corresponding (nonzero) elements. Let&#39;s choose the top-left elements, $-4k$ and $2.$ Equating these elements, we get</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n-4k &= 2 \\\\[5pt]\nk &= -\\dfrac{1}{2}.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p><strong>Note:</strong> We would still get the same result if we chose any other two corresponding (nonzero) elements.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To express $kX,$ we multiply each entry of $X$ by $k,$ as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nk{X} &=\nk \\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-4 & 0 \\\\\n10 & 8 \\\\\n6 & -4\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-4k & 0 \\\\\n10k & 8k \\\\\n6k & -4k\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we set the above result equal to the matrix given for $kX$ in the problem statement:\n\\[ \\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-4k & 0 \\\\\n10k & 8k \\\\\n6k & -4k\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 & 0 \\\\\n-5 & -4 \\\\\n-3 & 2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\]\nWe can solve for $k$ by equating any two corresponding (nonzero) elements. Let's choose the top-left elements, $-4k$ and $2.$ Equating these elements, we get\n\\begin{align*}\n-4k &= 2 \\\\[5pt]\nk &= -\\dfrac{1}{2}.\n\\end{align*}\nNote: We would still get the same result if we chose any other two corresponding (nonzero) elements.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$k=-\\dfrac{1}{2}$",
              "text": "$k=-\\dfrac{1}{2}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$k=\\dfrac{1}{2}$",
              "text": "$k=\\dfrac{1}{2}$"

              "text": "$k=-2$"

          ],

        {
          "number": 5,
          "question_id": 69672,
          "task_id": 2830903,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 146,
          "topic_name": "Scalar Multiplication of Matrices",
          "example_id": 4381,
          "example_title": "Calculating the Value of a Scalar",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:36:58.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:36 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 11358,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:11",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>If $k {L} =\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -4 & 0 & -10 \\\\\n        -6 & -6 & -14 \\\\\n         0 & -10 & -2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$,\nwhere ${L} =\n     \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        2 & 0 & 5 \\\\\n        3 & 3 & 7 \\\\\n        0 & 5 & 1\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$\nand $k$ is a scalar, calculate the value of $k.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "If $k {L} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-4 & 0 & -10 \\\\\n-6 & -6 & -14 \\\\\n0 & -10 & -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$,\nwhere ${L} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 & 0 & 5 \\\\\n3 & 3 & 7 \\\\\n0 & 5 & 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$\nand $k$ is a scalar, calculate the value of $k.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To express $kL,$ we multiply each entry of $L$ by $k,$ as follows:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nk {L} &=\n    k\\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        2 & 0 & 5 \\\\\n        3 & 3 & 7 \\\\\n        0 & 5 & 1\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        2k & 0 & 5k \\\\\n        3k & 3k & 7k \\\\\n         0  & 5k & k\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Now, we set the above result equal to the matrix given for $kL$ in the problem statement:</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        2k & 0 & 5k \\\\\n        3k & 3k & 7k \\\\\n         0  & 5k & k\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n= \n   \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -4 & 0 & -10 \\\\\n        -6 & -6 & -14 \\\\\n        0  & -10 & -2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n    \\]</p>\n<p>We can solve for $k$ by equating any two corresponding (nonzero) elements. Let&#39;s choose the bottom-right elements, $k$ and $-2.$ Equating these elements, we get</p>\n<p>\\begin{align}\nk &= -2.\n\\end{align}</p>\n<p><strong>Note:</strong> We would still get the same result if we chose any other two corresponding (nonzero) elements.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To express $kL,$ we multiply each entry of $L$ by $k,$ as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nk {L} &=\nk\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 & 0 & 5 \\\\\n3 & 3 & 7 \\\\\n0 & 5 & 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2k & 0 & 5k \\\\\n3k & 3k & 7k \\\\\n0 & 5k & k\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we set the above result equal to the matrix given for $kL$ in the problem statement:\n\\[\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2k & 0 & 5k \\\\\n3k & 3k & 7k \\\\\n0 & 5k & k\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-4 & 0 & -10 \\\\\n-6 & -6 & -14 \\\\\n0 & -10 & -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\]\nWe can solve for $k$ by equating any two corresponding (nonzero) elements. Let's choose the bottom-right elements, $k$ and $-2.$ Equating these elements, we get\n\\begin{align}\nk &= -2.\n\\end{align}\nNote: We would still get the same result if we chose any other two corresponding (nonzero) elements.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [

              "number": 1,
              "html": "$k=-2$",
              "text": "$k=-2$"

        {

          "time_elapsed_ms": 6931,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:07",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>If $k {R} =\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        2 & -3 \\\\\n        0 & 1\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$,\nwhere $ {R} =\n     \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -2 & 3 \\\\\n        0 & -1\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$\nand $k$ is a scalar, calculate the value of $k.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "If $k {R} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 & -3 \\\\\n0 & 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$,\nwhere $ {R} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-2 & 3 \\\\\n0 & -1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$\nand $k$ is a scalar, calculate the value of $k.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To express $kR,$ we multiply each entry of $R$ by $k,$ as follows:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nk {R} &=\n    k\\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -2 & 3 \\\\\n        0 & -1\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -2k & 3k \\\\\n        0 & -k\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Now, we set the above result equal to the matrix given for $kR$ in the problem statement:</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -2k & 3k \\\\\n        0 & -k\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n= \n   \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        2 & -3 \\\\\n        0 & 1\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack. \n    \\]</p>\n<p>We can solve for $k$ by equating any two corresponding (nonzero) elements. Let&#39;s choose the top-right elements, $3k$ and $-3.$ Equating these elements, we get</p>\n<p>\\begin{align}\n3k &= -3 \\\\[3pt]\nk &= -1.\n\\end{align}</p>\n<p><strong>Note:</strong> We would still get the same result if we chose any other two corresponding (nonzero) elements.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To express $kR,$ we multiply each entry of $R$ by $k,$ as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nk {R} &=\nk\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-2 & 3 \\\\\n0 & -1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-2k & 3k \\\\\n0 & -k\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we set the above result equal to the matrix given for $kR$ in the problem statement:\n\\[\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-2k & 3k \\\\\n0 & -k\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 & -3 \\\\\n0 & 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack.\n\\]\nWe can solve for $k$ by equating any two corresponding (nonzero) elements. Let's choose the top-right elements, $3k$ and $-3.$ Equating these elements, we get\n\\begin{align}\n3k &= -3 \\\\[3pt]\nk &= -1.\n\\end{align}\nNote: We would still get the same result if we chose any other two corresponding (nonzero) elements.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$k=-1$",
              "text": "$k=-1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$k=1$",
              "text": "$k=1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$k=-2$",
              "text": "$k=-2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$k=2$",
              "text": "$k=2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$k=0$",
              "text": "$k=0$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$k=-1$",
            "text": "$k=-1$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],

        {
          "number": 7,
          "question_id": 126003,
          "task_id": 2830903,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 146,
          "topic_name": "Scalar Multiplication of Matrices",
          "example_id": 4382,
          "example_title": "Calculating a Linear Combination of Two Matrices",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:39:29.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:39 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 63899,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:04",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Calculate $-{W} - 2{A}$, where\n${W}=\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -3 \\\\ 1 \\\\ 1 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$\nand\n$\n{A}=\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       5 \\\\ -2 \\\\ 2 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Calculate $-{W} - 2{A}$, where\n${W}=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 \\\\ 1 \\\\ 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$\nand\n$\n{A}=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n5 \\\\ -2 \\\\ 2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>We multiply each entry of $W$ by $-1$ and each entry of $A$ by $2,$ and then subtract the resulting matrices, as follows:</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\begin{align}\n-{W} - 2{A}\n&=\n(-1) \n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -3 \\\\ 1 \\\\ 1 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n\n   \n    -\n\n2\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       5 \\\\ -2 \\\\ 2 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n\n&=\n\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n         (-1) \\cdot (-3) \\\\ (-1) \\cdot 1 \\\\ (-1) \\cdot 1 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n\n   \n    -\n\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       2 \\cdot 5 \\\\ 2 \\cdot (-2) \\\\ 2 \\cdot 2 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n\n&=\n\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       3 \\\\ -1 \\\\ -1 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n   \n    -\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       10 \\\\ -4 \\\\ 4 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n\n\n&=\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       3-10 \\\\ -1-(-4) \\\\ -1-4 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&=\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       -7 \\\\ 3 \\\\ -5 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "We multiply each entry of $W$ by $-1$ and each entry of $A$ by $2,$ and then subtract the resulting matrices, as follows:\n\\[\n\\begin{align}\n-{W} - 2{A}\n&=\n(-1)\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 \\\\ 1 \\\\ 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n-\n2\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n5 \\\\ -2 \\\\ 2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n(-1) \\cdot (-3) \\\\ (-1) \\cdot 1 \\\\ (-1) \\cdot 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n-\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 \\cdot 5 \\\\ 2 \\cdot (-2) \\\\ 2 \\cdot 2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 \\\\ -1 \\\\ -1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n-\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n10 \\\\ -4 \\\\ 4\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3-10 \\\\ -1-(-4) \\\\ -1-4\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-7 \\\\ 3 \\\\ -5\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       -7 \\\\ 3 \\\\ -5 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-7 \\\\ 3 \\\\ -5\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       2 \\\\ 3 \\\\ -1 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 \\\\ 3 \\\\ -1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       -7 \\\\ 0 \\\\ -1 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-7 \\\\ 0 \\\\ -1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       -2 \\\\ 1 \\\\ -5 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-2 \\\\ 1 \\\\ -5\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       -7 \\\\ -1 \\\\ 2 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-7 \\\\ -1 \\\\ 2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n       -7 \\\\ 3 \\\\ -5 \n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
            "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-7 \\\\ 3 \\\\ -5\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],

        {
          "number": 8,
          "question_id": 126011,
          "task_id": 2830903,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 146,
          "topic_name": "Scalar Multiplication of Matrices",
          "example_id": 4382,
          "example_title": "Calculating a Linear Combination of Two Matrices",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:40:19.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:40 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 47068,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:47",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Calculate $\\dfrac{1}{3}{S} - 2{T}$, where\n${S}=\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -3 & -3 \\\\\n        -3 & 6 \\\\\n        0 & 0 \\\\\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$\nand\n$\n{T}=\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        1 & -2 \\\\\n        1 & 0 \\\\\n        1 & 1 \\\\\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Calculate $\\dfrac{1}{3}{S} - 2{T}$, where\n${S}=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 & -3 \\\\\n-3 & 6 \\\\\n0 & 0 \\\\\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$\nand\n$\n{T}=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 & -2 \\\\\n1 & 0 \\\\\n1 & 1 \\\\\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>We multiply each entry of $S$ by $\\dfrac{1}{3}$ and each entry of $T$ by $2,$ and then subtract the resulting matrices, as follows:</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{1}{3}{S} - 2{T}\n&=\n\\dfrac{1}{3} \n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -3 & -3 \\\\\n        -3 & 6 \\\\\n        0 & 0 \\\\\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n\n   \n    -\n\n2\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        1 & -2 \\\\\n        1 & 0 \\\\\n        1 & 1 \\\\\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n\n&=\n\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-3) & \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-3) \\\\\n        \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-3) & \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 6 \\\\\n        \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 0 & \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 0 \\\\\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n\n   \n    -\n\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        2 \\cdot 1 & 2 \\cdot (-2) \\\\\n        2 \\cdot 1 & 2 \\cdot 0 \\\\\n        2 \\cdot 1 & 2 \\cdot 1 \\\\\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n\n&=\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -1 & -1 \\\\\n        -1 & 2 \\\\\n        0 & 0\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n\n   \n    -\n\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        2 & -4 \\\\\n        2 & 0 \\\\\n        2 & 2 \\\\\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&=\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -1-2 & -1-(-4) \\\\\n        -1-2 & 2-0 \\\\\n        0-2 & 0-2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n\n\t&=\n\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -3 & 3 \\\\\n        -3 & 2 \\\\\n        -2 & -2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "We multiply each entry of $S$ by $\\dfrac{1}{3}$ and each entry of $T$ by $2,$ and then subtract the resulting matrices, as follows:\n\\[\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{1}{3}{S} - 2{T}\n&=\n\\dfrac{1}{3}\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 & -3 \\\\\n-3 & 6 \\\\\n0 & 0 \\\\\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n-\n2\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 & -2 \\\\\n1 & 0 \\\\\n1 & 1 \\\\\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n\\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-3) & \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-3) \\\\\n\\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-3) & \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 6 \\\\\n\\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 0 & \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 0 \\\\\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n-\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 \\cdot 1 & 2 \\cdot (-2) \\\\\n2 \\cdot 1 & 2 \\cdot 0 \\\\\n2 \\cdot 1 & 2 \\cdot 1 \\\\\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-1 & -1 \\\\\n-1 & 2 \\\\\n0 & 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n-\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 & -4 \\\\\n2 & 0 \\\\\n2 & 2 \\\\\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-1-2 & -1-(-4) \\\\\n-1-2 & 2-0 \\\\\n0-2 & 0-2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 & 3 \\\\\n-3 & 2 \\\\\n-2 & -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -3 & 3 \\\\\n        -3 & 2 \\\\\n        -2 & -2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 & 3 \\\\\n-3 & 2 \\\\\n-2 & -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -2 & 3 \\\\\n        -3 & 2 \\\\\n        -1 & -2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-2 & 3 \\\\\n-3 & 2 \\\\\n-1 & -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        1 & -5 \\\\\n        1 & 2 \\\\\n        2 & 2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 & -5 \\\\\n1 & 2 \\\\\n2 & 2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        1 & -3 \\\\\n        -1 & 2 \\\\\n        2 & 2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 & -3 \\\\\n-1 & 2 \\\\\n2 & 2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -3 & 3 \\\\\n        2 & 2 \\\\\n        -2 & -1\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
              "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 & 3 \\\\\n2 & 2 \\\\\n-2 & -1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$\n    \\left\\lbrack\n    \\matrix {\n        -3 & 3 \\\\\n        -3 & 2 \\\\\n        -2 & -2\n    }\n    \\right\\rbrack\n$",
            "text": "$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 & 3 \\\\\n-3 & 2 \\\\\n-2 & -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$",

    {
      "task_id": 2830902,
      "task_type": "Lesson",
      "topic_id": 149,
      "topic_name": "Elementary Row Operations",
      "course_id": null,
      "course_name": "Mathematics for Machine Learning",
      "completed_at": "2025-01-12T23:33:28.000Z",
      "completed_date": "Sun, Jan 12th, 2025",
      "completed_time": "11:33 PM",
      "started_at": "2025-01-12T22:43:14.000Z",
      "points_possible": 7,
      "points_awarded": 7,
      "progress": 1,
      "progress_display": "100%",
      "review_url": "/learn?taskId=2830902",
      "topic_url": "/topics/149",
      "prerequisites": [
        {
          "id": 148,
          "name": "Systems of Equations as Augmented Matrices",
          "url_slug": null,

        {
          "id": 1167,
          "name": "Index Notation for Matrices",

        }
      ],
      "question_count": 10,
      "questions": [
        {
          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 130065,
          "task_id": 2830902,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 149,
          "topic_name": "Elementary Row Operations",
          "example_id": 4693,
          "example_title": "Applying Row Operations To a Coefficients Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:14:06.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:14 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 253759,
          "time_elapsed_display": "4:14",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Consider the matrix $C,$ given by\n\\[\nC = \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1&   2&\t1 \\\\\n-9&\t0&\t-4\\\\\n2&\t0 &\t-1 \n\\end{bmatrix}.\t\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the following row operations on $C?$\n\\[\n\\begin{align*}\nR_1 &:= R_1 + 3R_3\\\\\nR_2 &:= R_2 + 5R_3\n\\end{align*}\n\\] </p>\n",
          "question_text": "Consider the matrix $C,$ given by\n\\[\nC =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1& 2& 1 \\\\\n-9& 0& -4\\\\\n2& 0 & -1\n\\end{bmatrix}.\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the following row operations on $C?$\n\\[\n\\begin{align*}\nR_1 &:= R_1 + 3R_3\\\\\nR_2 &:= R_2 + 5R_3\n\\end{align*}\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Let&#39;s list the required operations:</p>\n<ul>\n<li><p>The operation $R_1 :=R_1 + 3R_3$ means that we need to add $3$ times the third row to the first row. </p>\n</li>\n<li><p>The operation $R_2 :=R_2 + 5R_3$ means that we need to add $5$ times the third row to the second row. </p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Applying the operations, we obtain the following:</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\begin{align*}\nC&= \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 2 & 1 \\\\\n-9 & 0 & -4 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2}&\t{\\color{blue}0}&\t{\\color{blue}-1}\n\\end{bmatrix} \\qquad R_1 := R_1 + {\\color{red}3} \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_3}\\\\[5pt]\n\n&\\sim \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 + ({\\color{red}3})({\\color{blue}2}) &\t2 + ({\\color{red}3})({\\color{blue}0}) &\t1 + ({\\color{red}3})({\\color{blue}-1}) \\\\\n-9 & 0 & -4 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2} &\t{\\color{blue}0}&\t{\\color{blue}-1}\n\\end{bmatrix}\\\\[5pt]\n\n&\\sim \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 &\t 2 & -2 \\\\\n-9 & 0 & -4 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2}&\t{\\color{blue}0}&\t{\\color{blue}-1}\n\\end{bmatrix} \\qquad R_2 := R_2 + {\\color{red}5} \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_3}\\\\[5pt]\n\n&\\sim \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n7&\t2 &\t-2 \\\\\n-9 + ({\\color{red}5})({\\color{blue}2}) &\t 0 + ({\\color{red}5})({\\color{blue}0}) &\t-4 + ({\\color{red}5})({\\color{blue}-1}) \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2} &\t{\\color{blue}0} &\t{\\color{blue}-1} \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\\\\[5pt]\n\n&\\sim \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 &\t2 &\t-2 \\\\\n{\\color{red}1} &\t {\\color{red}0} &\t{\\color{red}-9} \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2}&\t{\\color{blue}0}&\t{\\color{blue}-1}\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\end{align*}\n\\] </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Let's list the required operations:\nThe operation $R_1 :=R_1 + 3R_3$ means that we need to add $3$ times the third row to the first row.\nThe operation $R_2 :=R_2 + 5R_3$ means that we need to add $5$ times the third row to the second row.\nApplying the operations, we obtain the following:\n\\[\n\\begin{align*}\nC&=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 2 & 1 \\\\\n-9 & 0 & -4 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2}& {\\color{blue}0}& {\\color{blue}-1}\n\\end{bmatrix} \\qquad R_1 := R_1 + {\\color{red}3} \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_3}\\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 + ({\\color{red}3})({\\color{blue}2}) & 2 + ({\\color{red}3})({\\color{blue}0}) & 1 + ({\\color{red}3})({\\color{blue}-1}) \\\\\n-9 & 0 & -4 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2} & {\\color{blue}0}& {\\color{blue}-1}\n\\end{bmatrix}\\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 & 2 & -2 \\\\\n-9 & 0 & -4 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2}& {\\color{blue}0}& {\\color{blue}-1}\n\\end{bmatrix} \\qquad R_2 := R_2 + {\\color{red}5} \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_3}\\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n7& 2 & -2 \\\\\n-9 + ({\\color{red}5})({\\color{blue}2}) & 0 + ({\\color{red}5})({\\color{blue}0}) & -4 + ({\\color{red}5})({\\color{blue}-1}) \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2} & {\\color{blue}0} & {\\color{blue}-1} \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 & 2 & -2 \\\\\n{\\color{red}1} & {\\color{red}0} & {\\color{red}-9} \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2}& {\\color{blue}0}& {\\color{blue}-1}\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\end{align*}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 &\t2 &\t-2 \\\\\n1 &\t 0 &\t-9 \\\\\n2&\t0&\t-1\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}$",
              "text": "$\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 & 2 & -2 \\\\\n1 & 0 & -9 \\\\\n2& 0& -1\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}$"
            },

              "html": "$\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 &\t2 &\t1 \\\\\n7 &\t 2 &\t-2 \\\\\n2&\t0&\t-1\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}$",
              "text": "$\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 2 & 1 \\\\\n7 & 2 & -2 \\\\\n2& 0& -1\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}$"
            },
            {

              "html": "$\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 &\t2 &\t-2 \\\\\n-9 &\t 0 &\t-4 \\\\\n1&\t0&\t-9\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}$",

              "html": "$\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 &\t2 &\t1 \\\\\n7 &\t2 &\t-2 \\\\\n1&\t0&\t-9\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}$",

          ],
          "student_answer": {

        {
          "number": 2,
          "question_id": 130009,
          "task_id": 2830902,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 149,
          "topic_name": "Elementary Row Operations",
          "example_id": 4693,
          "example_title": "Applying Row Operations To a Coefficients Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:15:47.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:15 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 97644,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:38",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Consider the matrix $M,$ given by\n\\[\nM = \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 &\t-1 &\t2 \\\\\n-3 &\t2 &\t-1 \\\\\n6 &\t0 &\t4\n\\end{bmatrix}.\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the row operation $R_1:=R_1 + 4 R_2$ on $M?$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Consider the matrix $M,$ given by\n\\[\nM =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 & -1 & 2 \\\\\n-3 & 2 & -1 \\\\\n6 & 0 & 4\n\\end{bmatrix}.\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the row operation $R_1:=R_1 + 4 R_2$ on $M?$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The operation $R_1:=R_1 + 4 R_2$ means that we need to add $4$ times the second row to the first row.</p>\n<p>Applying this row operation, we obtain the following:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nM &= \n\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 & -1 & 2 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}-3} &\t{\\color{blue}2} &\t{\\color{blue}-1} \\\\\n6 &\t0 &\t4 \n\\end{bmatrix} \\qquad  R_1:=R_1 + {\\color{red}4} \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_2} \\\\[5pt]\n\n&\\sim \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 + ({\\color{red}4}) ({\\color{blue}-3})&\t-1 + ({\\color{red}4}) ({\\color{blue}2}) &\t2 +({\\color{red}4}) ({\\color{blue}-1}) \\\\\n{\\color{blue}-3}&\t{\\color{blue}2}&\t{\\color{blue}-1} \\\\\n6 & 0 & 4\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{red}-12 &\t\\color{red}7 &\t\\color{red}-2 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}-3}&\t{\\color{blue}2}&\t{\\color{blue}-1} \\\\\n6 & 0 & 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The operation $R_1:=R_1 + 4 R_2$ means that we need to add $4$ times the second row to the first row.\nApplying this row operation, we obtain the following:\n\\begin{align*}\nM &=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 & -1 & 2 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}-3} & {\\color{blue}2} & {\\color{blue}-1} \\\\\n6 & 0 & 4\n\\end{bmatrix} \\qquad R_1:=R_1 + {\\color{red}4} \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_2} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 + ({\\color{red}4}) ({\\color{blue}-3})& -1 + ({\\color{red}4}) ({\\color{blue}2}) & 2 +({\\color{red}4}) ({\\color{blue}-1}) \\\\\n{\\color{blue}-3}& {\\color{blue}2}& {\\color{blue}-1} \\\\\n6 & 0 & 4\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{red}-12 & \\color{red}7 & \\color{red}-2 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}-3}& {\\color{blue}2}& {\\color{blue}-1} \\\\\n6 & 0 & 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\begin{bmatrix}\n-12 & 7 &\t-2 \\\\\n-3&\t2 &\t-1 \\\\\n6 & 0 & 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$",
              "text": "$\\begin{bmatrix}\n-12 & 7 & -2 \\\\\n-3& 2 & -1 \\\\\n6 & 0 & 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 & -1 &\t2 \\\\\n-3&\t-2 &\t7 \\\\\n6 & 0 & 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$",

            },
            {

            },
            {

              "text": "$\\begin{bmatrix}\n-3 & 1 & 1 \\\\\n-3& 2 & -1 \\\\\n6 & 0 & 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\begin{bmatrix}\n-12 & 9 &\t-6 \\\\\n-3&\t2 &\t-1 \\\\\n6 & 0 & 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$",
              "text": "$\\begin{bmatrix}\n-12 & 9 & -6 \\\\\n-3& 2 & -1 \\\\\n6 & 0 & 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$\\begin{bmatrix}\n-12 & 7 &\t-2 \\\\\n-3&\t2 &\t-1 \\\\\n6 & 0 & 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$",
            "text": "$\\begin{bmatrix}\n-12 & 7 & -2 \\\\\n-3& 2 & -1 \\\\\n6 & 0 & 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$",

        {
          "number": 3,
          "question_id": 72763,
          "task_id": 2830902,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 149,
          "topic_name": "Elementary Row Operations",
          "example_id": 4694,
          "example_title": "Applying Row Operations To an Augmented Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:16:30.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:16 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 29964,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:30",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Consider the augmented matrix $M,$ given by\n\\[\nM = \n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t0&\t2 & 3 \\\\\n0&\t-2&\t1 & 4 \\\\\n1&\t-1&\t1 & 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the row operation $R_3 := R_3 + (-1) \\cdot R_1$ on $M?$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Consider the augmented matrix $M,$ given by\n\\[\nM =\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& 0& 2 & 3 \\\\\n0& -2& 1 & 4 \\\\\n1& -1& 1 & 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the row operation $R_3 := R_3 + (-1) \\cdot R_1$ on $M?$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The operation $R_3 := R_3 + (-1) \\cdot R_1$ means that we need to add $-1$ times the first row to the third row.</p>\n<p>Applying this row operation, we obtain the following:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nM &= \n\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n{\\color{blue}1}&\t{\\color{blue}0}&\t{\\color{blue}2} & {\\color{blue}3} \\\\\n0&\t-2&\t1 & 4 \\\\\n1&\t-1&\t1 & 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]  \\qquad R_3 := R_3 + ({\\color{red}-1}) \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_1} \\\\[5pt]\n\n&\\sim \n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n{\\color{blue}1}&\t{\\color{blue}0}&\t{\\color{blue}2} & {\\color{blue}3} \\\\\n0&\t-2&\t1 & 4 \\\\\n1 + ({\\color{red}-1})({\\color{blue}1})&\t-1 + ({\\color{red}-1})({\\color{blue}0})&\t1 + ({\\color{red}-1})({\\color{blue}2}) & 2 + ({\\color{red}-1})({\\color{blue}3})\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\\\[5pt]\n\n&\\sim \n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n{\\color{blue}1}&\t{\\color{blue}0}&\t{\\color{blue}2} & {\\color{blue}3} \\\\\n0&\t-2&\t1 & 4 \\\\\n\\color{red}0&\t\\color{red}-1&\t\\color{red}-1 & \\color{red}-1\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The operation $R_3 := R_3 + (-1) \\cdot R_1$ means that we need to add $-1$ times the first row to the third row.\nApplying this row operation, we obtain the following:\n\\begin{align*}\nM &=\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n{\\color{blue}1}& {\\color{blue}0}& {\\color{blue}2} & {\\color{blue}3} \\\\\n0& -2& 1 & 4 \\\\\n1& -1& 1 & 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\qquad R_3 := R_3 + ({\\color{red}-1}) \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_1} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n{\\color{blue}1}& {\\color{blue}0}& {\\color{blue}2} & {\\color{blue}3} \\\\\n0& -2& 1 & 4 \\\\\n1 + ({\\color{red}-1})({\\color{blue}1})& -1 + ({\\color{red}-1})({\\color{blue}0})& 1 + ({\\color{red}-1})({\\color{blue}2}) & 2 + ({\\color{red}-1})({\\color{blue}3})\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n{\\color{blue}1}& {\\color{blue}0}& {\\color{blue}2} & {\\color{blue}3} \\\\\n0& -2& 1 & 4 \\\\\n\\color{red}0& \\color{red}-1& \\color{red}-1 & \\color{red}-1\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t0&\t2 & 3 \\\\\n0&\t-2&\t1 & 4 \\\\\n0&\t-1&\t-1 & -1  \n\\end{array}\n\\right] $ ",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& 0& 2 & 3 \\\\\n0& -2& 1 & 4 \\\\\n0& -1& -1 & -1\n\\end{array}\n\\right] $"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t0&\t2 & 3 \\\\\n0&\t-2&\t1 & 4 \\\\\n1&\t-1&\t1 & 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right] $  ",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& 0& 2 & 3 \\\\\n0& -2& 1 & 4 \\\\\n1& -1& 1 & 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right] $"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n0&\t-2&\t1 & 4 \\\\\n1&\t0&\t2 & 3 \\\\\n1&\t1&\t0 & 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n0& -2& 1 & 4 \\\\\n1& 0& 2 & 3 \\\\\n1& 1& 0 & 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n0&\t-2&\t1 & 4 \\\\\n1&\t0&\t2 & 3 \\\\\n1&\t1&\t0 & -2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n0& -2& 1 & 4 \\\\\n1& 0& 2 & 3 \\\\\n1& 1& 0 & -2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t3&\t9 & 1 \\\\\n0&\t-3&\t3 & 0 \\\\\n0&\t1&\t1 & 1  \n\\end{array}\n\\right] $ ",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& 3& 9 & 1 \\\\\n0& -3& 3 & 0 \\\\\n0& 1& 1 & 1\n\\end{array}\n\\right] $"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t0&\t2 & 3 \\\\\n0&\t-2&\t1 & 4 \\\\\n0&\t-1&\t-1 & -1  \n\\end{array}\n\\right] $ ",
            "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& 0& 2 & 3 \\\\\n0& -2& 1 & 4 \\\\\n0& -1& -1 & -1\n\\end{array}\n\\right] $",
            "free_response": [],

          "hints": [],

        {
          "number": 4,
          "question_id": 130073,
          "task_id": 2830902,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 149,
          "topic_name": "Elementary Row Operations",
          "example_id": 4694,
          "example_title": "Applying Row Operations To an Augmented Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:22:33.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:22 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 358101,
          "time_elapsed_display": "5:58",
          "result": "incorrect",
          "correct": false,
          "question_html": "<p>Consider the augmented matrix $U,$ given by\n\\[\nU = \n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t-3&\t5 & -2 \\\\\n3&\t9&\t-8 & 7 \\\\\n8&\t6&\t4 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\t\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the following row operations on $U?$</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\begin{align*}\nR_2 &:= R_2 + 2R_1 \\\\[3pt]\nR_3 &:= -\\dfrac12 R_3 \n\\end{align*} \n\\] </p>\n",
          "question_text": "Consider the augmented matrix $U,$ given by\n\\[\nU =\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& -3& 5 & -2 \\\\\n3& 9& -8 & 7 \\\\\n8& 6& 4 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the following row operations on $U?$\n\\[\n\\begin{align*}\nR_2 &:= R_2 + 2R_1 \\\\[3pt]\nR_3 &:= -\\dfrac12 R_3\n\\end{align*}\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Let&#39;s list the required operations:</p>\n<ul>\n<li><p>The operation $R_2 := R_2 + 2R_1$ means that we need to add $2$ times the first row to the second row.</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>The operation $R_3 := -\\dfrac{1}{2}R_3$ means that we need to multiply the third row of the matrix by $-\\dfrac{1}{2}.$</p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Applying the operations, we obtain the following:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nU &= \n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t-3&\t5 & -2 \\\\\n3 &\t9 &\t-8 & 7 \\\\\n8&\t6&\t4 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]   \\qquad R_2 := R_2 + 2 R_1 \\\\[5pt]  \n\n&\\sim \n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t-3&\t5 & -2 \\\\\n3 + (2)(1) &\t9 + (2)(-3) &\t-8 + (2)(5)  & 7 + (2)(-2) \\\\\n8&\t6&\t4 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\\\[5pt]\n\n&\\sim \n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t-3&\t5 & -2 \\\\\n5 &\t3 &\t2  & 3 \\\\\n8&\t6&\t4 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]  \\qquad R_3 := -\\dfrac 1 2 \\cdot R_3 \\\\[5pt]\n\n&\\sim   \n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t-3&\t5 & -2 \\\\\n5 &\t3 &\t2 & 3 \\\\\n-4 &\t-3 &\t-2 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Let's list the required operations:\nThe operation $R_2 := R_2 + 2R_1$ means that we need to add $2$ times the first row to the second row.\nThe operation $R_3 := -\\dfrac{1}{2}R_3$ means that we need to multiply the third row of the matrix by $-\\dfrac{1}{2}.$\nApplying the operations, we obtain the following:\n\\begin{align*}\nU &=\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& -3& 5 & -2 \\\\\n3 & 9 & -8 & 7 \\\\\n8& 6& 4 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\qquad R_2 := R_2 + 2 R_1 \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& -3& 5 & -2 \\\\\n3 + (2)(1) & 9 + (2)(-3) & -8 + (2)(5) & 7 + (2)(-2) \\\\\n8& 6& 4 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& -3& 5 & -2 \\\\\n5 & 3 & 2 & 3 \\\\\n8& 6& 4 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\qquad R_3 := -\\dfrac 1 2 \\cdot R_3 \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& -3& 5 & -2 \\\\\n5 & 3 & 2 & 3 \\\\\n-4 & -3 & -2 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t-3&\t5 & -2 \\\\\n5 &\t3 &\t2 & 3 \\\\\n-4 &\t-3 &\t-2 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& -3& 5 & -2 \\\\\n5 & 3 & 2 & 3 \\\\\n-4 & -3 & -2 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t-3&\t5 & -2 \\\\\n5 &\t3 &\t2 & 3 \\\\\n4 &\t3 &\t2 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& -3& 5 & -2 \\\\\n5 & 3 & 2 & 3 \\\\\n4 & 3 & 2 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t-3&\t5 & -2 \\\\\n5 &\t3 &\t2 & 3 \\\\\n8 &\t6 &\t4 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& -3& 5 & -2 \\\\\n5 & 3 & 2 & 3 \\\\\n8 & 6 & 4 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n5&\t3&\t2 & 3 \\\\\n3 &\t9 &\t-8 & 7 \\\\\n-4 &\t-3 &\t-2 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n5& 3& 2 & 3 \\\\\n3 & 9 & -8 & 7 \\\\\n-4 & -3 & -2 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t-3&\t5 & -2 \\\\\n5 &\t3 &\t2 & 3 \\\\\n-4 &\t6 &\t4 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& -3& 5 & -2 \\\\\n5 & 3 & 2 & 3 \\\\\n-4 & 6 & 4 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {

            "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&\t-3&\t5 & -2 \\\\\n5 &\t3 &\t2 & 3 \\\\\n4 &\t3 &\t2 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
            "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1& -3& 5 & -2 \\\\\n5 & 3 & 2 & 3 \\\\\n4 & 3 & 2 & 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
            "free_response": [],

          "hints": [],

        {
          "number": 5,
          "question_id": 72757,
          "task_id": 2830902,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 149,
          "topic_name": "Elementary Row Operations",
          "example_id": 4694,
          "example_title": "Applying Row Operations To an Augmented Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:24:12.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:24 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 94804,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:35",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Consider the augmented matrix $T,$ given by\n\\[\nT = \n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 &\t3&\t2\\\\\n-2 &\t-2&\t1\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\t\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the row operation $R_2 := R_2+2R_1$ on $T?$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Consider the augmented matrix $T,$ given by\n\\[\nT =\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & 3& 2\\\\\n-2 & -2& 1\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the row operation $R_2 := R_2+2R_1$ on $T?$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The operation $R_2 :=R_2 + 2 R_1$ means that we need to add $2$ times the first row to the second row. </p>\n<p>Applying this row operation, we obtain the following:  </p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nT & =\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c} \n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue}3& \\color{blue}2\\\\\n-2 &\t-2&\t1\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\qquad R_2 := R_2 + {\\color{red}2} \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_1} \\\\[5pt] \n&\\sim \n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue}3 & \\color{blue}2\\\\  \n-2 + ({\\color{red}2})({\\color{blue}1}) &\t-2 + ({\\color{red}2})({\\color{blue}3})&\t1 + ({\\color{red}2})({\\color{blue}2})\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\\\[5pt] \n \n&\\sim \n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue} 3& \\color{blue}2\\\\ \n\\color{red}0     &  \\color{red} 4&\t\\color{red} 5\\\\ \n\\end{array} \n\\right]\n\\end{align*} </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The operation $R_2 :=R_2 + 2 R_1$ means that we need to add $2$ times the first row to the second row.\nApplying this row operation, we obtain the following:\n\\begin{align*}\nT & =\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue}3& \\color{blue}2\\\\\n-2 & -2& 1\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\qquad R_2 := R_2 + {\\color{red}2} \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_1} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue}3 & \\color{blue}2\\\\\n-2 + ({\\color{red}2})({\\color{blue}1}) & -2 + ({\\color{red}2})({\\color{blue}3})& 1 + ({\\color{red}2})({\\color{blue}2})\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue} 3& \\color{blue}2\\\\\n\\color{red}0 & \\color{red} 4& \\color{red} 5\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & 3& 2\\\\ \n0 &   4 &5\\\\  \n\\end{array} \n\\right]$ ",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & 3& 2\\\\\n0 & 4 &5\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & 3& 2\\\\ \n0 &   4 &-5\\\\  \n\\end{array} \n\\right]$ ",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & 3& 2\\\\\n0 & 4 &-5\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & 3& 2\\\\ \n0 &   -4 &5\\\\  \n\\end{array} \n\\right]$ ",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & 3& 2\\\\\n0 & -4 &5\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & 3& 2\\\\ \n0 &   5 &4\\\\  \n\\end{array}  \n\\right]$ ",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & 3& 2\\\\\n0 & 5 &4\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & 3& 2\\\\ \n0 &   -5&-4\\\\  \n\\end{array}  \n\\right]$ ",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & 3& 2\\\\\n0 & -5&-4\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & 3& 2\\\\ \n0 &   4 &5\\\\  \n\\end{array} \n\\right]$ ",
            "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & 3& 2\\\\\n0 & 4 &5\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
            "free_response": [],

          },
          "hints": [],

        {
          "number": 6,
          "question_id": 72747,
          "task_id": 2830902,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 149,
          "topic_name": "Elementary Row Operations",
          "example_id": 4694,
          "example_title": "Applying Row Operations To an Augmented Matrix",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:29:10.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:29 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 294287,
          "time_elapsed_display": "4:54",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Consider the augmented matrix $B,$ given by\n\\[\nB = \n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 &\t-1&\t2\\\\\n3 &\t-4&\t4\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the row operation $R_2 := R_2+(-3)\\cdot R_1$ on $B?$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Consider the augmented matrix $B,$ given by\n\\[\nB =\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & -1& 2\\\\\n3 & -4& 4\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the row operation $R_2 := R_2+(-3)\\cdot R_1$ on $B?$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The operation $R_2:=R_2 + (-3)\\cdot R_1$ means that we need to add $-3$ times the first row to the second row. </p>\n<p>Applying this row operation, we obtain the following:\n\\begin{align*}\nB & =\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue}-1& \\color{blue}2\\\\\n3 &\t-4&\t4\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\qquad R_2 := R_2 + ({\\color{red}-3}) \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_1} \\\\[5pt] \n&\\sim \n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue}-1 & \\color{blue}2\\\\\n3+ ({\\color{red}-3})({\\color{blue}1}) &\t-4+ ({\\color{red}-3})({\\color{blue}-1})&\t4+ ({\\color{red}-3})({\\color{blue}2})\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\\\[5pt]\n\n&\\sim \n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue}-1 & \\color{blue}2\\\\ \n\\color{red}0 & \\color{red}-1&\t\\color{red}-2\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\end{align*} </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The operation $R_2:=R_2 + (-3)\\cdot R_1$ means that we need to add $-3$ times the first row to the second row.\nApplying this row operation, we obtain the following:\n\\begin{align*}\nB & =\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue}-1& \\color{blue}2\\\\\n3 & -4& 4\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\qquad R_2 := R_2 + ({\\color{red}-3}) \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_1} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue}-1 & \\color{blue}2\\\\\n3+ ({\\color{red}-3})({\\color{blue}1}) & -4+ ({\\color{red}-3})({\\color{blue}-1})& 4+ ({\\color{red}-3})({\\color{blue}2})\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue}-1 & \\color{blue}2\\\\\n\\color{red}0 & \\color{red}-1& \\color{red}-2\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\left[\\begin{array}{cc|c} \n1 &\t-1&\t2\\\\\n0 &\t-1&\t-2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
              "text": "$\\left[\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & -1& 2\\\\\n0 & -1& -2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\\left[\\begin{array}{cc|c} \n1 &\t-1&\t2\\\\\n0 &\t-1&\t2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
              "text": "$\\left[\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & -1& 2\\\\\n0 & -1& 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\\left[\\begin{array}{cc|c} \n1 &\t-1&\t2\\\\\n0 &\t1&\t-3\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
              "text": "$\\left[\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & -1& 2\\\\\n0 & 1& -3\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\\left[\\begin{array}{cc|c} \n1 &\t-1&\t2\\\\\n0 &\t1& -10\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
              "text": "$\\left[\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & -1& 2\\\\\n0 & 1& -10\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n-8 &\t-13& -10\\\\\n3 &\t-4&\t4\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
              "text": "$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n-8 & -13& -10\\\\\n3 & -4& 4\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$\\left[\\begin{array}{cc|c} \n1 &\t-1&\t2\\\\\n0 &\t-1&\t-2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
            "text": "$\\left[\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 & -1& 2\\\\\n0 & -1& -2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],

        {
          "number": 7,
          "question_id": 72853,
          "task_id": 2830902,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 149,
          "topic_name": "Elementary Row Operations",
          "example_id": 4695,
          "example_title": "Identifying the Appropriate Row Operation To Transform One Matrix Into Another",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:30:30.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:30 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 16091,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:16",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Which of the following elementary row operations transforms the matrix $B$ into the matrix $C$, where\n\\[\nB=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n4& 3 & 2\\\\ \n1 & 2 & 1\\\\  \n0 & -2 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\quad\nC=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 2 & 1\\\\\n4 & 3& 2\\\\\n0 & -2 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix}? \n\\]</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Which of the following elementary row operations transforms the matrix $B$ into the matrix $C$, where\n\\[\nB=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n4& 3 & 2\\\\\n1 & 2 & 1\\\\\n0 & -2 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\quad\nC=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 2 & 1\\\\\n4 & 3& 2\\\\\n0 & -2 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix}?\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, notice that the $3$rd row is the same in both matrices. The only rows that have changed are the $1$st row and the $2$nd row. </p>\n<p>By considering the given row operations that change both the $1$st and the $2$nd rows, we notice that</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nB &=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}4& \\color{blue}3 &\\color{blue} 2\\\\ \n\\color{red}1 & \\color{red}2 & \\color{red}1\\\\    \n0 & -2 & 1 \n\\end{bmatrix} & {\\color{blue}R_1} \\leftrightarrow {\\color{red}R_2} \\\\[5pt]\n \n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{red}1 & \\color{red}2 & \\color{red}1\\\\ \n\\color{blue}4& \\color{blue}3 &\\color{blue} 2\\\\ \n0 & -2 & 1 \n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n\n&= C.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the required row operation is</p>\n<p>\\[ \nR_1 \\leftrightarrow R_2.\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, notice that the $3$rd row is the same in both matrices. The only rows that have changed are the $1$st row and the $2$nd row.\nBy considering the given row operations that change both the $1$st and the $2$nd rows, we notice that\n\\begin{align*}\nB &=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}4& \\color{blue}3 &\\color{blue} 2\\\\\n\\color{red}1 & \\color{red}2 & \\color{red}1\\\\\n0 & -2 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix} & {\\color{blue}R_1} \\leftrightarrow {\\color{red}R_2} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{red}1 & \\color{red}2 & \\color{red}1\\\\\n\\color{blue}4& \\color{blue}3 &\\color{blue} 2\\\\\n0 & -2 & 1\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&= C.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the required row operation is\n\\[\nR_1 \\leftrightarrow R_2.\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$R_1 \\leftrightarrow R_2$ ",
              "text": "$R_1 \\leftrightarrow R_2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$R_1 :=  \\dfrac {1}{ 4} R_1$  ",
              "text": "$R_1 := \\dfrac {1}{ 4} R_1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$R_1 \\leftrightarrow R_3$   ",
              "text": "$R_1 \\leftrightarrow R_3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$R_2 := R_2 +   \\dfrac {3}{ 4}R_1$   ",
              "text": "$R_2 := R_2 + \\dfrac {3}{ 4}R_1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$R_2 \\leftrightarrow R_3$   ",
              "text": "$R_2 \\leftrightarrow R_3$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$R_1 \\leftrightarrow R_2$ ",
            "text": "$R_1 \\leftrightarrow R_2$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12873736,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        },
        {

          "example_id": 4695,
          "example_title": "Identifying the Appropriate Row Operation To Transform One Matrix Into Another",

          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:31:11.000Z",

          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Which of the following elementary row operations transforms the matrix $U$ into the matrix $V$, where\n\\[\nU=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 3 & 2\\\\ \n6 & 4 & 5\\\\ \n1 & 2 & -1\n\\end{bmatrix}, \n\\quad\nV=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 2 & -1\\\\\n6 & 4 & 5\\\\ \n1 & 3 & 2 \n\\end{bmatrix}? \n\\]</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Which of the following elementary row operations transforms the matrix $U$ into the matrix $V$, where\n\\[\nU=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 3 & 2\\\\\n6 & 4 & 5\\\\\n1 & 2 & -1\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\quad\nV=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 2 & -1\\\\\n6 & 4 & 5\\\\\n1 & 3 & 2\n\\end{bmatrix}?\n\\]",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, notice that the $2$nd row is the same in both matrices. The only rows that have changed are the $1$st row and the $3$rd row.   </p>\n<p>By considering the given row operations that change both the $1$st and the $3$rd row, we notice that</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nU &= \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n \\color{blue}1 &  \\color{blue}3 &  \\color{blue}2\\\\ \n6 & 4 & 5\\\\ \n\\color{red}1 & \\color{red}2 & \\color{red}-1 \n\\end{bmatrix} & \\hspace{-0.2cm} {\\color{blue}R_1} \\leftrightarrow {\\color{red}R_3} \\\\[5pt]\n\n&\\sim \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{red}1 & \\color{red}2 &\\color{red} -1\\\\ \n6 & 4 & 5\\\\ \n \\color{blue}1 &  \\color{blue}3 &  \\color{blue}2 \n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n\n&= V.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the required row operation is</p>\n<p>\\[\nR_1 \\leftrightarrow R_3.\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, notice that the $2$nd row is the same in both matrices. The only rows that have changed are the $1$st row and the $3$rd row.\nBy considering the given row operations that change both the $1$st and the $3$rd row, we notice that\n\\begin{align*}\nU &=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue}3 & \\color{blue}2\\\\\n6 & 4 & 5\\\\\n\\color{red}1 & \\color{red}2 & \\color{red}-1\n\\end{bmatrix} & \\hspace{-0.2cm} {\\color{blue}R_1} \\leftrightarrow {\\color{red}R_3} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{red}1 & \\color{red}2 &\\color{red} -1\\\\\n6 & 4 & 5\\\\\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue}3 & \\color{blue}2\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&= V.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the required row operation is\n\\[\nR_1 \\leftrightarrow R_3.\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": " $\nR_1 \\leftrightarrow R_3 \n$",
              "text": "$\nR_1 \\leftrightarrow R_3\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\nR_2 \\leftrightarrow R_3 \n$",
              "text": "$\nR_2 \\leftrightarrow R_3\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\nR_3 := R_2 + (-5) R_1  \n$",
              "text": "$\nR_3 := R_2 + (-5) R_1\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\nR_2 := 2R_3+4R_1\n$",
              "text": "$\nR_2 := 2R_3+4R_1\n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\nR_2 \\leftrightarrow R_1 \n$",
              "text": "$\nR_2 \\leftrightarrow R_1\n$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": " $\nR_1 \\leftrightarrow R_3 \n$",
            "text": "$\nR_1 \\leftrightarrow R_3\n$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12873794,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        },
        {
          "number": 9,
          "question_id": 20386,
          "task_id": 2830902,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 149,
          "topic_name": "Elementary Row Operations",
          "example_id": 4696,
          "example_title": "Identifying the Appropriate Row Operation To Transform a Given Entry to a Desired Value",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:32:17.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:32 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 42524,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:43",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given \n$\nD = \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 &  3 &\t -1\\\\\n 0 & 4 &\t3\\\\\n3 & 2 &\t 2\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$,\n which of the following elementary row operations makes $a_{31}=0?$ </p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given\n$\nD =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 3 & -1\\\\\n0 & 4 & 3\\\\\n3 & 2 & 2\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$,\nwhich of the following elementary row operations makes $a_{31}=0?$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Of the given options, we consider the row operation </p>\n<p>\\[\nR_3 := R_3 + (-3) R_1.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Applying the above row operation, we obtain the following:</p>\n<p>\\[\\begin{align*}\nD &= \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}1&\t\\color{blue}3&\t\\color{blue}-1\\\\\n 0 & 4 &\t3\\\\\n3 & 2 &\t 2\n\\end{bmatrix} \n\\qquad R_3 := R_3 + ({\\color{red}-3}) \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_1} \\\\[5pt] \n\n&\\sim \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}&\t\\color{blue}3&\t\\color{blue}-1\\\\\n0 & 4 &\t3\\\\\n\\color{red}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{0}&\t\\color{red}-7&\t\\color{red}5\\\\ \n\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\end{align*}\\] </p>\n<p>Therefore, the required row operation is</p>\n<p>\\[\n R_3 := R_3 + (-3)R_1.  \n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Of the given options, we consider the row operation\n\\[\nR_3 := R_3 + (-3) R_1.\n\\]\nApplying the above row operation, we obtain the following:\n\\[\\begin{align*}\nD &=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}1& \\color{blue}3& \\color{blue}-1\\\\\n0 & 4 & 3\\\\\n3 & 2 & 2\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\qquad R_3 := R_3 + ({\\color{red}-3}) \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_1} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}& \\color{blue}3& \\color{blue}-1\\\\\n0 & 4 & 3\\\\\n\\color{red}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{0}& \\color{red}-7& \\color{red}5\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\end{align*}\\]\nTherefore, the required row operation is\n\\[\nR_3 := R_3 + (-3)R_1.\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$ R_3 := R_3 + (-3)R_1$",
              "text": "$ R_3 := R_3 + (-3)R_1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$ R_3 := R_3 + \\dfrac 1 2 R_1$",
              "text": "$ R_3 := R_3 + \\dfrac 1 2 R_1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$ R_3 := \\dfrac13 R_3 $",
              "text": "$ R_3 := \\dfrac13 R_3 $"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$ R_3 := R_3 + (-2)R_1$",
              "text": "$ R_3 := R_3 + (-2)R_1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$ R_3 \\leftrightarrow R_1$",
              "text": "$ R_3 \\leftrightarrow R_1$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$ R_3 := R_3 + (-3)R_1$",
            "text": "$ R_3 := R_3 + (-3)R_1$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12873840,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        },
        {
          "number": 10,
          "question_id": 72874,
          "task_id": 2830902,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 149,
          "topic_name": "Elementary Row Operations",
          "example_id": 4696,
          "example_title": "Identifying the Appropriate Row Operation To Transform a Given Entry to a Desired Value",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T23:33:28.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:33 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 68254,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:08",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that\n$\nW = \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 2  &\t-1 \\\\\n-2 & 3  &\t-4 \\\\\n-1 & 2  & -3 \n\\end{bmatrix}\n$,\nwhich of the following elementary row operations makes $a_{31}=0?$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that\n$\nW =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 & 2 & -1 \\\\\n-2 & 3 & -4 \\\\\n-1 & 2 & -3\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$,\nwhich of the following elementary row operations makes $a_{31}=0?$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Of the given options, we consider the row operation </p>\n<p>\\[\nR_3 := R_3 + R_1 = R_3 + 1\\cdot R_1\n\\]</p>\n<p>Applying the above row operation, we obtain the following:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nW &= \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue}2  & \\color{blue}-1 \\\\\n-2 & 3  &\t-4 \\\\\n-1 & 2  & -3 \n\\end{bmatrix} \\qquad R_3 := R_3 + {\\color{red}1} \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_1} \\\\[5pt] \n\n&\\sim \n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}  & \\color{blue}2  & \\color{blue}-1 \\\\ \n-2 & 3  &\t-4 \\\\\n\\color{red}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{0} & \\color{red}4  &\t\\color{red}-4 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix} \n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the required row operation is</p>\n<p>\\[\nR_3 := R_3 + 1 \\cdot R_1\n\\]</p>\n<p>or simply</p>\n<p>\\[\nR_3 := R_3 + R_1.\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Of the given options, we consider the row operation\n\\[\nR_3 := R_3 + R_1 = R_3 + 1\\cdot R_1\n\\]\nApplying the above row operation, we obtain the following:\n\\begin{align*}\nW &=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}1 & \\color{blue}2 & \\color{blue}-1 \\\\\n-2 & 3 & -4 \\\\\n-1 & 2 & -3\n\\end{bmatrix} \\qquad R_3 := R_3 + {\\color{red}1} \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_1} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} & \\color{blue}2 & \\color{blue}-1 \\\\\n-2 & 3 & -4 \\\\\n\\color{red}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{0} & \\color{red}4 & \\color{red}-4 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the required row operation is\n\\[\nR_3 := R_3 + 1 \\cdot R_1\n\\]\nor simply\n\\[\nR_3 := R_3 + R_1.\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$R_3 := R_3 + R_1$",
              "text": "$R_3 := R_3 + R_1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$R_3\\leftrightarrow R_1$",
              "text": "$R_3\\leftrightarrow R_1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$R_3\\leftrightarrow R_2$",
              "text": "$R_3\\leftrightarrow R_2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$R_3 := R_3 -  R_1$",
              "text": "$R_3 := R_3 - R_1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$R_3 := -R_3$",
              "text": "$R_3 := -R_3$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$R_3 := R_3 + R_1$",
            "text": "$R_3 := R_3 + R_1$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12873957,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "task_id": 2830773,
