            {
              "id": 455873,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n%\\usetikzlibrary{arrows} %% Can be useful, not essential\n%\\usetikzlibrary{intersections} %% Can be useful, not essential\n%\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\begin{tikzpicture}\n\\begin{axis}[\n%trig format plots=rad, %% Ensures radians for trig functions\ntick style={draw=none},\ntick label style={font=\\footnotesize},\nlabel style={font=\\small},\nlegend style={font=\\footnotesize},\ntick style={draw=none},\naxis line style = thick,\naxis y line = center,\naxis x line = middle,\n%axis equal,\n%grid = both,\n%scale= 0.8,\nxmin = -8,\nxmax = 8,\nymin = -4,\nymax = 16,\nyticklabels={,,},\nxticklabels={,,},\nxlabel = $x$,\nylabel = {$y$},\nxlabel style={below right},\nylabel style={above left}\n]\n\n%Graphic:\n\\addplot [domain=-8:8,samples=200,color=red, line width=0.8pt]{x^2+7};\n\n% Arrows\n%\\draw[<                   -> , blue, line width=2.3pt] (axis cs:-7.08,0) -- (axis cs:1.92,0);\n%\\draw[<              -> ,  blue, line width=1.5pt] (axis cs:-8,0) -- (axis cs:8,0);\n%\\draw[ ->, red, line width=1.5pt] (axis cs:2.08,0) -- (axis cs:10.8,0);\n\n\n%\\node[circle,draw=blue,fill=blue,inner sep=1.5pt] (A1) at (axis cs:-4,0) {}; \n%\\coordinate [label={above left:{\\small $-4$}}] (A1) at (axis cs:-4,0);\n%\\node[circle,draw=blue,fill=blue,inner sep=1.5pt] (A2) at (axis cs:4,0) {};\n%\\coordinate [label={above right:{\\small $4$}}] (A2) at (axis cs:4,0); \n\n\\end{axis}\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",

        {
          "number": 10,
          "question_id": 30807,
          "task_id": 2829175,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1495,
          "topic_name": "Solving Elementary Quadratic Inequalities",
          "example_id": 5058,
          "example_title": "Elementary Quadratic Inequalities with No Solutions or All Real Solutions",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T20:39:14.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 8:39 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 24183,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:24",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Solve the inequality $3x^2-6\\leq 5x^2.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Solve the inequality $3x^2-6\\leq 5x^2.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, let&#39;s simplify the inequality:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n3x^2-6&\\leq 5x^2 \\\\\n3x^2-5x^2 &\\leq6\\\\\n-2x^2 &\\leq 6\\\\\nx^2&\\geq -\\dfrac{ 6}{2}\\\\\nx^2&\\geq -3.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Now, we can use either of two methods to solve the inequality.</p>\n<p><strong>Method 1 - Solving an Absolute Value Inequality</strong></p>\n<p>We know that the square of <em>any</em> real number is greater than or equal to zero.</p>\n<p>So, the inequality is true for every real number. Therefore the solution is $x \\in (-\\infty,\\infty).$ </p>\n<p><strong>Method 2 - Picturing a Quadratic Curve</strong></p>\n<p>Starting from $-2x^2 \\leq 6$ and moving all terms to the left-hand side, we reach</p>\n<p>\\[\n-2x^2-6 \\leq 0 .\n\\]</p>\n<p>Now, we consider the corresponding parabola given by $y = -2x^2-6$:</p>\n<p>Since the leading coefficient of $ -2x^2-6$   is negative, the parabola opens downward (like $\\frown$). The desired inequality $(y\\leq 0)$ is always satisfied. Therefore, the solution set to the given inequality includes all real numbers. That is, $x \\in (-\\infty,\\infty).$</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-dfddbbf679013266d5e4c09f8b40f421\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/dfddbbf679013266d5e4c09f8b40f421\" /></div>\n\n\n\n\n\n\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, let's simplify the inequality:\n\\begin{align*}\n3x^2-6&\\leq 5x^2 \\\\\n3x^2-5x^2 &\\leq6\\\\\n-2x^2 &\\leq 6\\\\\nx^2&\\geq -\\dfrac{ 6}{2}\\\\\nx^2&\\geq -3.\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we can use either of two methods to solve the inequality.\nMethod 1 - Solving an Absolute Value Inequality\nWe know that the square of any real number is greater than or equal to zero.\nSo, the inequality is true for every real number. Therefore the solution is $x \\in (-\\infty,\\infty).$\nMethod 2 - Picturing a Quadratic Curve\nStarting from $-2x^2 \\leq 6$ and moving all terms to the left-hand side, we reach\n\\[\n-2x^2-6 \\leq 0 .\n\\]\nNow, we consider the corresponding parabola given by $y = -2x^2-6$:\nSince the leading coefficient of $ -2x^2-6$ is negative, the parabola opens downward (like $\\frown$). The desired inequality $(y\\leq 0)$ is always satisfied. Therefore, the solution set to the given inequality includes all real numbers. That is, $x \\in (-\\infty,\\infty).$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\, x\\in (-\\infty, \\infty)$",
              "text": "$\\, x\\in (-\\infty, \\infty)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\\, x\\in [3 ,   \\infty)$",
              "text": "$\\, x\\in [3 , \\infty)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\\, x \\in (-\\infty  ,  -3]\\cup [3,\\infty)$",
              "text": "$\\, x \\in (-\\infty , -3]\\cup [3,\\infty)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\\, x \\in (-\\infty  ,  -3]$",
              "text": "$\\, x \\in (-\\infty , -3]$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\, x \\in (-\\infty  ,  -5] \\cup [5, \\infty)$",
              "text": "$\\, x \\in (-\\infty , -5] \\cup [5, \\infty)$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$\\, x\\in (-\\infty, \\infty)$",
            "text": "$\\, x\\in (-\\infty, \\infty)$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [
            {
              "id": 342613,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n%\\usetikzlibrary{arrows} %% Can be useful, not essential\n%\\usetikzlibrary{intersections} %% Can be useful, not essential\n%\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\begin{tikzpicture}\n\\begin{axis}[\n%trig format plots=rad, %% Ensures radians for trig functions\ntick style={draw=none},\ntick label style={font=\\footnotesize},\nlabel style={font=\\small},\nlegend style={font=\\footnotesize},\ntick style={draw=none},\naxis line style = thick,\naxis y line = center,\naxis x line = middle,\n%axis equal,\n%grid = both,\n%scale= 0.8,\nxmin = -6,\nxmax = 6,\nymin = -25,\nymax = 6,\nyticklabels={,,},\nxticklabels={,,},\nxlabel = $x$,\nylabel = {$y$},\nxlabel style={below right},\nylabel style={above left}\n]\n \n\n%Graphic:\n\\addplot [domain=-6:6,samples=200,color=red, line width=0.6pt]{-2*x^2-6 };  \n\n\n\n% Arrows\n%\\draw[<             -> , blue, line width=2.3pt] (axis cs:-6.08,0) -- (axis cs: -2.02,0);\n\\draw[<           ->, blue, line width=1.5pt] (axis cs:-6.0,0) -- (axis cs:6.02,0); \n%\\draw[ ->, red, line width=1.5pt] (axis cs:2.08,0) -- (axis cs:10.8,0);\n\n\n\\end{axis} \n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",

              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n%\\usetikzlibrary{arrows} %% Can be useful, not essential\n%\\usetikzlibrary{intersections} %% Can be useful, not essential\n%\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\begin{tikzpicture}\n\\begin{axis}[\n%trig format plots=rad, %% Ensures radians for trig functions\ntick style={draw=none},\ntick label style={font=\\footnotesize},\nlabel style={font=\\small},\nlegend style={font=\\footnotesize},\ntick style={draw=none},\naxis line style = thick,\naxis y line = center,\naxis x line = middle,\n%axis equal,\n%grid = both,\n%scale= 0.8,\nxmin = -6,\nxmax = 6,\nymin = -25,\nymax = 6,\nyticklabels={,,},\nxticklabels={,,},\nxlabel = $x$,\nylabel = {$y$},\nxlabel style={below right},\nylabel style={above left}\n]\n \n\n%Graphic:\n\\addplot [domain=-6:6,samples=200,color=red, line width=0.6pt]{-2*x^2-6 };  \n\n\n\n% Arrows\n%\\draw[<             -> , blue, line width=2.3pt] (axis cs:-6.08,0) -- (axis cs: -2.02,0);\n\\draw[<           ->, blue, line width=1.5pt] (axis cs:-6.0,0) -- (axis cs:6.02,0); \n%\\draw[ ->, red, line width=1.5pt] (axis cs:2.08,0) -- (axis cs:10.8,0);\n\n\n\\end{axis} \n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",

          "question_html": "<p>Solve the inequality $x^2 \\lt -{2}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Solve the inequality $x^2 \\lt -{2}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>We can solve this inequality using either of the two methods described below.</p>\n<p><strong>Method 1 - Solving an Absolute Value Inequality</strong></p>\n<p>We know that the square of <em>any</em> real number is greater than or equal to zero. Therefore, it is impossible for the square of a real number to be smaller than $-2.$</p>\n<p>So, the inequality $x^2 \\lt  -2$ has no solutions.</p>\n<p><strong>Method 2 - Picturing a Quadratic Curve</strong></p>\n<p>Let&#39;s bring all the terms to the left-hand side,\n\\[\nx^2+ 2 <      0,\n\\]\nand then plot the parabola $y=x^2+ 2.$ The desired inequality ($y<     0$) is never satisfied, so there are no solutions.</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-c841572ab4058b673d8c43cbf79dd4fd\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/c841572ab4058b673d8c43cbf79dd4fd\" /></div> \n",
          "explanation_text": "We can solve this inequality using either of the two methods described below.\nMethod 1 - Solving an Absolute Value Inequality\nWe know that the square of any real number is greater than or equal to zero. Therefore, it is impossible for the square of a real number to be smaller than $-2.$\nSo, the inequality $x^2 \\lt -2$ has no solutions.\nMethod 2 - Picturing a Quadratic Curve\nLet's bring all the terms to the left-hand side,\n\\[\nx^2+ 2 < 0,\n\\]\nand then plot the parabola $y=x^2+ 2.$ The desired inequality ($y< 0$) is never satisfied, so there are no solutions.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "The inequality has no solution",
              "text": "The inequality has no solution"

              "text": "$- 2 \\lt x \\lt 2$"
            },

          "graphics": [
            {
              "id": 342588,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n%\\usetikzlibrary{arrows} %% Can be useful, not essential\n%\\usetikzlibrary{intersections} %% Can be useful, not essential\n%\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\begin{tikzpicture}\n\\begin{axis}[\n%trig format plots=rad, %% Ensures radians for trig functions\ntick style={draw=none},\ntick label style={font=\\footnotesize},\nlabel style={font=\\small},\nlegend style={font=\\footnotesize},\ntick style={draw=none},\naxis line style = thick,\naxis y line = center,\naxis x line = middle,\n%axis equal,\n%grid = both,\n%scale= 0.8,\nxmin = -5,\nxmax = 5,\nymin = -5,\nymax = 15,\nyticklabels={,,},\nxticklabels={,,},\nxlabel = $x$,\nylabel = {$y$},\nxlabel style={below right},\nylabel style={above left}\n]\n\n%Graphic:\n\\addplot [domain=-8:8,samples=200,color=red, line width=0.8pt]{x^2+2};\n\n% Arrows\n%\\draw[<                   -> , blue, line width=2.3pt] (axis cs:-7.08,0) -- (axis cs:1.92,0);\n%\\draw[<              -> ,  blue, line width=1.5pt] (axis cs:-5,0) -- (axis cs:5,0);\n%\\draw[ ->, red, line width=1.5pt] (axis cs:2.08,0) -- (axis cs:10.8,0);\n\n\n%\\node[circle,draw=blue,fill=blue,inner sep=1.5pt] (A1) at (axis cs:-4,0) {}; \n%\\coordinate [label={above left:{\\small $-4$}}] (A1) at (axis cs:-4,0);\n%\\node[circle,draw=blue,fill=blue,inner sep=1.5pt] (A2) at (axis cs:4,0) {};\n%\\coordinate [label={above right:{\\small $4$}}] (A2) at (axis cs:4,0); \n\n\\end{axis}\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",

              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n%\\usetikzlibrary{arrows} %% Can be useful, not essential\n%\\usetikzlibrary{intersections} %% Can be useful, not essential\n%\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\begin{tikzpicture}\n\\begin{axis}[\n%trig format plots=rad, %% Ensures radians for trig functions\ntick style={draw=none},\ntick label style={font=\\footnotesize},\nlabel style={font=\\small},\nlegend style={font=\\footnotesize},\ntick style={draw=none},\naxis line style = thick,\naxis y line = center,\naxis x line = middle,\n%axis equal,\n%grid = both,\n%scale= 0.8,\nxmin = -5,\nxmax = 5,\nymin = -5,\nymax = 15,\nyticklabels={,,},\nxticklabels={,,},\nxlabel = $x$,\nylabel = {$y$},\nxlabel style={below right},\nylabel style={above left}\n]\n\n%Graphic:\n\\addplot [domain=-8:8,samples=200,color=red, line width=0.8pt]{x^2+2};\n\n% Arrows\n%\\draw[<                   -> , blue, line width=2.3pt] (axis cs:-7.08,0) -- (axis cs:1.92,0);\n%\\draw[<              -> ,  blue, line width=1.5pt] (axis cs:-5,0) -- (axis cs:5,0);\n%\\draw[ ->, red, line width=1.5pt] (axis cs:2.08,0) -- (axis cs:10.8,0);\n\n\n%\\node[circle,draw=blue,fill=blue,inner sep=1.5pt] (A1) at (axis cs:-4,0) {}; \n%\\coordinate [label={above left:{\\small $-4$}}] (A1) at (axis cs:-4,0);\n%\\node[circle,draw=blue,fill=blue,inner sep=1.5pt] (A2) at (axis cs:4,0) {};\n%\\coordinate [label={above right:{\\small $4$}}] (A2) at (axis cs:4,0); \n\n\\end{axis}\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",

      "questions": [
        {
          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 70868,
          "task_id": 2828637,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1183,
          "topic_name": "Circles in the Coordinate Plane",
          "example_id": 3207,
          "example_title": "Determining the Center of a Circle on the Coordinate Plane Using the Midpoint Formula",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T20:23:11.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 8:23 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 6753,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:07",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find the coordinates $(h,k)$ of the center of the circle above given that the points $(0,2)$ and $(8,4)$ lie on a diameter of the circle.</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find the coordinates $(h,k)$ of the center of the circle above given that the points $(0,2)$ and $(8,4)$ lie on a diameter of the circle.",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The center of the circle is located at the midpoint of the diameter. Therefore, we can calculate it using the midpoint formula:</p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\t(h,k) &= \\left( \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}, \\dfrac {y_1+y_2}{2}\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&= \\left(\\dfrac{0+8}2,\\dfrac{2+4}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&= \\left(\\dfrac{8}2,\\dfrac{6}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&= (4, 3). \n\t}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The center of the circle is located at the midpoint of the diameter. Therefore, we can calculate it using the midpoint formula:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n(h,k) &= \\left( \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}, \\dfrac {y_1+y_2}{2}\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\left(\\dfrac{0+8}2,\\dfrac{2+4}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\left(\\dfrac{8}2,\\dfrac{6}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&= (4, 3).\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$(4,3)$",
              "text": "$(4,3)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$(3,1)$ ",
              "text": "$(3,1)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$(3,3)$ ",
              "text": "$(3,3)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$(3,4)$ ",
              "text": "$(3,4)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$(4,1)$",
              "text": "$(4,1)$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$(4,3)$",
            "text": "$(4,3)$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [
            {
              "id": 364801,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{arrows} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usetikzlibrary{intersections} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\n\\begin{document}\n\t\n\t\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\t\n\t\\begin{tikzpicture} [scale = 0.5]\n\t\n\\draw[-stealth, thick] (-6.5,0)--(9.5,0) node[right]{$x$};\n\\draw[-stealth, thick] (0,-2.5)--(0,7.5) node[above]{$y$};\n\n\\draw[very thick, red] (4,3) circle (4.12cm);\n\n\\draw[dashed, blue] (0,2) -- (8,4);\n\n\\node[fill,circle,inner sep=1.5pt, red] at (0,2) {};\n\\draw[shift={(0,2)},color=black] node[left] {$\\small \\left(0,2\\right)$};\n\n\\node[fill,circle,inner sep=1.5pt, red] at (8,4) {};\n\\draw[shift={(8,4)},color=black] node[right] {$\\small \\left(8,4\\right)$};\n\n\n\\node[fill,circle,inner sep=1.5pt, red] at (4,3) {};\n\\draw[shift={(3.4,3.3)},color=black] node[above right] {$\\small (h,k)$}; \n\t\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-70868"

        {
          "number": 2,

          "task_id": 2828637,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1183,
          "topic_name": "Circles in the Coordinate Plane",

          "example_title": "Determining the Center of a Circle on the Coordinate Plane Using the Midpoint Formula",

          "time_elapsed_ms": 14154,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:14",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find the coordinates $(h,k)$ of the center of the circle above given that the points $(-4,3)$ and $(2,1)$ lie on a diameter of the circle.</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find the coordinates $(h,k)$ of the center of the circle above given that the points $(-4,3)$ and $(2,1)$ lie on a diameter of the circle.",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The center of the circle is located at the midpoint of the diameter. Therefore, we can calculate it using the midpoint formula:</p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\t(h,k) &= \\left( \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}, \\dfrac {y_1+y_2}{2}\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&= \\left(\\dfrac{-4+2}2,\\dfrac{3+1}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&= \\left(\\dfrac{-2}2,\\dfrac{4}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&= (-1, 2). \n\t}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The center of the circle is located at the midpoint of the diameter. Therefore, we can calculate it using the midpoint formula:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n(h,k) &= \\left( \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}, \\dfrac {y_1+y_2}{2}\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\left(\\dfrac{-4+2}2,\\dfrac{3+1}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\left(\\dfrac{-2}2,\\dfrac{4}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&= (-1, 2).\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",

              "html": " $(-1,2)$",

            {
              "id": 364791,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{arrows} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usetikzlibrary{intersections} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\n\\begin{document}\n\t\n\t\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\t\n\t\\begin{tikzpicture} [scale = 0.8]\n\t\n\\draw[-stealth, thick] (-5.5,0)--(4.5,0) node[right]{$x$};\n\\draw[-stealth, thick] (0,-3.5)--(0,5.5) node[above]{$y$};\n\n\\draw[very thick, red] (-1,2) circle (3.16cm);\n\n\\draw[dashed, blue] (-4,3) -- (2,1);\n\n\\node[fill,circle,inner sep=1.5pt, red] at (-4,3) {};\n\\draw[shift={(-4,3)},color=black] node[above left] {$\\small \\left(-4,3\\right)$};\n\n\\node[fill,circle,inner sep=1.5pt, red] at (2,1) {};\n\\draw[shift={(2,1)},color=black] node[below right] {$\\small \\left(2,1\\right)$};\n\n\n\\node[fill,circle,inner sep=1.5pt, red] at (-1,2) {};\n\\draw[shift={(-1.3,2.1)},color=black] node[above right] {$\\small (h,k)$}; \n\t\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-45689"

        {
          "number": 3,
          "question_id": 70871,
          "task_id": 2828637,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1183,
          "topic_name": "Circles in the Coordinate Plane",
          "example_id": 3208,
          "example_title": "Finding the Radius of a Circle on the Coordinate Plane Using the Distance Formula",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T20:25:10.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 8:25 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 25214,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:25",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>The points $A$ and $B$ have coordinates $(-6,3)$ and $(0,1)$, respectively, and the segment $\\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of a circle. Find the radius of the circle.</p>\n",
          "question_text": "The points $A$ and $B$ have coordinates $(-6,3)$ and $(0,1)$, respectively, and the segment $\\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of a circle. Find the radius of the circle.",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The diameter can be calculated using the distance formula: \n\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\td &= \\sqrt{{(x_1 - x_2)}^2+{(y_1-y_2)}^2} \\\\[3pt]\n           &= \\sqrt{\\left(-6 - 0\\right)^2 + \\left(3 -1\\right)^2} \\\\[3pt]\n                    &= \\sqrt{(-6)^2+(2)^2} \\\\[3pt]\t\t   \n                    &= \\sqrt{36+4} \\\\[3pt]\n                    &= \\sqrt{40} \\\\[3pt]\n\t\t    &= 2\\sqrt{10}. \n\t\t \n\t}\n\\]\nThe radius is half the diameter:\n\\[\nr = \\dfrac{d}{2} = \\dfrac{2\\sqrt{10}}{2} =\\sqrt{10}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The diameter can be calculated using the distance formula:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\nd &= \\sqrt{{(x_1 - x_2)}^2+{(y_1-y_2)}^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{\\left(-6 - 0\\right)^2 + \\left(3 -1\\right)^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{(-6)^2+(2)^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{36+4} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{40} \\\\[3pt]\n&= 2\\sqrt{10}.\n}\n\\]\nThe radius is half the diameter:\n\\[\nr = \\dfrac{d}{2} = \\dfrac{2\\sqrt{10}}{2} =\\sqrt{10}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\sqrt{10}$ ",
              "text": "$\\sqrt{10}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$4\\sqrt{10}$  ",
              "text": "$4\\sqrt{10}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{\\sqrt{10}}{2}$ ",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{\\sqrt{10}}{2}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\\sqrt{5}$  ",
              "text": "$\\sqrt{5}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$2\\sqrt{10}$  ",
              "text": "$2\\sqrt{10}$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$\\sqrt{10}$ ",
            "text": "$\\sqrt{10}$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {

            "select_id": null,

          }
        },
        {
          "number": 4,
          "question_id": 45690,
          "task_id": 2828637,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1183,
          "topic_name": "Circles in the Coordinate Plane",
          "example_id": 3208,
          "example_title": "Finding the Radius of a Circle on the Coordinate Plane Using the Distance Formula",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T20:25:29.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 8:25 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 16396,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:16",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>The points $A$ and $B$ have coordinates $(6,-4)$ and $(0,-2)$, respectively, and the segment $\\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of a circle. Find the radius of the circle.</p>\n",
          "question_text": "The points $A$ and $B$ have coordinates $(6,-4)$ and $(0,-2)$, respectively, and the segment $\\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of a circle. Find the radius of the circle.",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The diameter can be calculated using the distance formula: </p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\td &= \\sqrt{{(x_1 - x_2)}^2+{(y_1-y_2)}^2} \\\\[3pt]\n                                   &= \\sqrt{(6-0)^2 + (-4 - (-2))^2}\\\\[3pt]\n\t\t   &= \\sqrt{(6)^2+(-2)^2} \\\\[3pt]\n\t\t   &= \\sqrt{36 +4} \\\\[3pt]\n\t\t   &= \\sqrt{40}\\\\[3pt]\n\t\t   &= 2\\sqrt{10}\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>The radius is half the diameter:\n\\[\nr = \\dfrac{d}{2} = 2\\dfrac{\\sqrt{10}}{2} = \\sqrt{10}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The diameter can be calculated using the distance formula:\n\\begin{align*}\nd &= \\sqrt{{(x_1 - x_2)}^2+{(y_1-y_2)}^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{(6-0)^2 + (-4 - (-2))^2}\\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{(6)^2+(-2)^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{36 +4} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{40}\\\\[3pt]\n&= 2\\sqrt{10}\n\\end{align*}\nThe radius is half the diameter:\n\\[\nr = \\dfrac{d}{2} = 2\\dfrac{\\sqrt{10}}{2} = \\sqrt{10}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": " $\\sqrt{10}$",
              "text": "$\\sqrt{10}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": " $2\\sqrt{10}$",
              "text": "$2\\sqrt{10}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$3\\sqrt{2}$",
              "text": "$3\\sqrt{2}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": " $\\sqrt{8}$",
              "text": "$\\sqrt{8}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$3\\sqrt{3}$",
              "text": "$3\\sqrt{3}$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": " $\\sqrt{10}$",
            "text": "$\\sqrt{10}$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],

        {
          "number": 5,
          "question_id": 45686,
          "task_id": 2828637,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1183,
          "topic_name": "Circles in the Coordinate Plane",
          "example_id": 3209,
          "example_title": "Determining the Center and Radius of a Circle on the Coordinate Plane",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T20:26:48.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 8:26 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 67309,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:07",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find the coordinates $(h,k)$ of the center of the circle above and its radius given that the points $(2,-3)$ and $(10,5)$ lie on a diameter of the circle. </p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find the coordinates $(h,k)$ of the center of the circle above and its radius given that the points $(2,-3)$ and $(10,5)$ lie on a diameter of the circle.",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The center of the circle is located at the midpoint of the diameter. So, we can calculate the coordinates of the center using the midpoint formula:</p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\t(h,k) &= \\left( \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}, \\dfrac {y_1+y_2}{2}\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t   &= \\left(\\dfrac{2+10}2,\\dfrac{-3+5}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t  &= \\left(\\dfrac{12}2,\\dfrac{2}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t &= (6, 1). \n\t}\n\\]\nThe radius can be found by calculating the distance between the center $\\left(6,1\\right)$ and a point on the circle, such as $A(2,-3)\\mathbin{:}$</p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\nr&=\\sqrt{{(x_1-x_2)}^2+{(y_1-y_2)}^2}\\\\[3pt]  \n          &= \\sqrt{(6 - 2)^2 + (1 - (-3))^2} \\\\[3pt]\n\t\t   &= \\sqrt{(4)^2+(4)^2} \\\\[3pt]\n\t\t   &= \\sqrt{16+16} \\\\[3pt]\n\t\t  &=  \\sqrt{32}\\\\[3pt]\n\t\t&= 4\\sqrt{2}. \n\t\t \n\t}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The center of the circle is located at the midpoint of the diameter. So, we can calculate the coordinates of the center using the midpoint formula:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n(h,k) &= \\left( \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}, \\dfrac {y_1+y_2}{2}\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\left(\\dfrac{2+10}2,\\dfrac{-3+5}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\left(\\dfrac{12}2,\\dfrac{2}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&= (6, 1).\n}\n\\]\nThe radius can be found by calculating the distance between the center $\\left(6,1\\right)$ and a point on the circle, such as $A(2,-3)\\mathbin{:}$\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\nr&=\\sqrt{{(x_1-x_2)}^2+{(y_1-y_2)}^2}\\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{(6 - 2)^2 + (1 - (-3))^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{(4)^2+(4)^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{16+16} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{32}\\\\[3pt]\n&= 4\\sqrt{2}.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "The center is $(6,1)$ and the radius is $4\\sqrt{2}$",

              "html": "The center is $(6,1)$ and the radius is $3\\sqrt{2}$",

              "text": "The center is $(5,1)$ and the radius is $4\\sqrt{2}$"

          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "The center is $(6,1)$ and the radius is $4\\sqrt{2}$",

            "free_response": [],

          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [
            {
              "id": 364790,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{arrows} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usetikzlibrary{intersections} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\t\n\\begin{document}\n\t\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\n\\begin{tikzpicture} [scale = 0.4]\n\n\n\\draw[-stealth, thick] (-1,0)--(14,0) node[right]{$x$};\n\\draw[-stealth, thick] (0,-7)--(0,7) node[above]{$y$};\n\n\\draw[shift={(0,5)},color=black] node[above left] {};\n\\draw[shift={(5,0)},color=black] node[below right] {};\n\\draw[shift={(0,-5)},color=black] node[below left] {};\n\\draw[shift={(-5,0)},color=black] node[below left] {};\n\n\\draw[very thick, red] (6,1) circle (5.65683cm);\n\n\\draw[dashed, blue] (10,5) -- (2,-3);\n\n\\node[fill,circle,inner sep=1.5pt, red] at (10,5) {};\n\\draw[shift={(10,5)},color=black] node[above right] {$\\displaystyle\\left(10,5\\right)$};\n\n\\node[fill,circle,inner sep=1.5pt, red] at (2,-3) {};\n\\draw[shift={(1.5,-3.5)},color=black] node[below] {$(2,-3)$};\n\n\\node[fill,circle,inner sep=1.5pt, red] at (6,1) {};\n\\draw[shift={(6,0.7)},color=black] node[right] {$(h,k)$}; \n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-45686"

        {
          "number": 6,
          "question_id": 54052,
          "task_id": 2828637,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1183,
          "topic_name": "Circles in the Coordinate Plane",
          "example_id": 3209,
          "example_title": "Determining the Center and Radius of a Circle on the Coordinate Plane",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T20:27:55.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 8:27 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 63596,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:04",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>The segment $\\overline{AB}$ is the diameter of a circle where the points $A$ and $B$ have coordinates $(3b,6b)$ and $(-3b,-2b)$, respectively, with $b>0.$ Find the coordinates of the center and the radius of the circle.</p>\n",
          "question_text": "The segment $\\overline{AB}$ is the diameter of a circle where the points $A$ and $B$ have coordinates $(3b,6b)$ and $(-3b,-2b)$, respectively, with $b>0.$ Find the coordinates of the center and the radius of the circle.",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The center of the circle is located at the midpoint of the diameter. So, we can calculate the coordinates of the center using the midpoint formula:\n\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\t(h,k) &= \\left( \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}, \\dfrac {y_1+y_2}{2}\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&= \\left(\\dfrac{3b+(-3b)}2,\\dfrac{6b+(-2b)}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t  &= \\left(\\dfrac{0}2,\\dfrac{4b}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t &= (0, 2b). \n\t}\n\\]</p>\n<p>The radius can be found by calculating the distance between the center $\\left(0,2b\\right)$ and a point on the circle, such as $A(3b,6b)\\mathbin{:}$</p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\tr&=\\sqrt{{(x_1-x_2)}^2+{(y_1-y_2)}^2}\\\\[3pt]\n  &= \\sqrt{(0 - 3b)^2 + (2b - 6b)^2}\\\\[3pt]\n\t\t   &= \\sqrt{(-3b)^2+(-4b)^2} \\\\[3pt]\n\t\t   &= \\sqrt{9b^2 +16b^2} \\\\[3pt]\n\t\t   &= \\sqrt{25b^2}\\\\[3pt]\n\t\t   &= 5|b|.\n\t\t \n\t}\n\\]</p>\n<p>Finally, since $b > 0,$ we have that \n\\[\nr = 5\\vert b \\vert = 5b.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Therefore, the center of the circle is at $(0, 2b)$ and the radius is $5b.$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The center of the circle is located at the midpoint of the diameter. So, we can calculate the coordinates of the center using the midpoint formula:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n(h,k) &= \\left( \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}, \\dfrac {y_1+y_2}{2}\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\left(\\dfrac{3b+(-3b)}2,\\dfrac{6b+(-2b)}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\left(\\dfrac{0}2,\\dfrac{4b}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&= (0, 2b).\n}\n\\]\nThe radius can be found by calculating the distance between the center $\\left(0,2b\\right)$ and a point on the circle, such as $A(3b,6b)\\mathbin{:}$\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\nr&=\\sqrt{{(x_1-x_2)}^2+{(y_1-y_2)}^2}\\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{(0 - 3b)^2 + (2b - 6b)^2}\\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{(-3b)^2+(-4b)^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{9b^2 +16b^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\sqrt{25b^2}\\\\[3pt]\n&= 5|b|.\n}\n\\]\nFinally, since $b > 0,$ we have that\n\\[\nr = 5\\vert b \\vert = 5b.\n\\]\nTherefore, the center of the circle is at $(0, 2b)$ and the radius is $5b.$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "The center is $(0,2b)$ and the radius is $5b$",
              "text": "The center is $(0,2b)$ and the radius is $5b$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "The center is $(b,b)$ and the radius is $5|b|$",
              "text": "The center is $(b,b)$ and the radius is $5|b|$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "The center is $(b,2b)$ and the radius is $4b$",
              "text": "The center is $(b,2b)$ and the radius is $4b$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "The center is $(0,b)$ and the radius is $3b$",
              "text": "The center is $(0,b)$ and the radius is $3b$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "The center is $(b,3b)$ and the radius is $3|b|$",
              "text": "The center is $(b,3b)$ and the radius is $3|b|$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "The center is $(0,2b)$ and the radius is $5b$",
            "text": "The center is $(0,2b)$ and the radius is $5b$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",

            "answer_id": 12862924,

      ]

    {
      "task_id": 2828111,

      "points_awarded": 4,
      "progress": 1,

          "id": 3282,
          "name": "The Poisson Distribution",

      "question_count": 3,
      "questions": [
        {
          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 85319,
          "task_id": 2828111,
          "task_type": "Review",
          "topic_id": 2838,
          "topic_name": "Modeling With the Poisson Distribution",
          "example_id": 7493,
          "example_title": "Identifying Random Variables that Follow a Poisson Distribution",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T20:04:54.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 8:04 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 260672,
          "time_elapsed_display": "4:21",
          "result": "incorrect",
          "correct": false,
          "question_html": "<p>Which of the following random variables could be modeled using a Poisson distribution?</p>\n<ol class=\"questionStatements\">\n<li>  The number of defective cell phones in a manufactured batch </li>\n<li>  The average price of a car </li>\n<li>  The number of patients admitted to a hospital emergency room in a day </li>\n</ol>",
          "question_text": "Which of the following random variables could be modeled using a Poisson distribution?\nThe number of defective cell phones in a manufactured batch\nThe average price of a car\nThe number of patients admitted to a hospital emergency room in a day",
          "explanation_html": "<p>A Poisson distribution models the number of events that occur in an interval of time or space if events occur independently and at a constant average rate.</p>\n<p>With that in mind, let&#39;s consider each of the given random variables.</p>\n<ul>\n<li><p>Random variable I follows a Poisson distribution. The number of defective cell phones can be modeled as independent events with a constant average rate, and we wish to know how many events occur in an interval of space (a batch).</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>Random variable II does <em>not</em> follow a Poisson distribution. The average price of a car is not an event that occurs at a constant average rate.</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>Random variable III follows a Poisson distribution. The number of patients admitted can be modeled as independent events with a constant average rate, and we wish to know how many events occur in an interval of time (a day).</p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Therefore, the correct answer is &quot;I and III only.&quot;</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "A Poisson distribution models the number of events that occur in an interval of time or space if events occur independently and at a constant average rate.\nWith that in mind, let's consider each of the given random variables.\nRandom variable I follows a Poisson distribution. The number of defective cell phones can be modeled as independent events with a constant average rate, and we wish to know how many events occur in an interval of space (a batch).\nRandom variable II does not follow a Poisson distribution. The average price of a car is not an event that occurs at a constant average rate.\nRandom variable III follows a Poisson distribution. The number of patients admitted can be modeled as independent events with a constant average rate, and we wish to know how many events occur in an interval of time (a day).\nTherefore, the correct answer is \"I and III only.\"",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "I and III only",
              "text": "I and III only"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "I only",
              "text": "I only"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "II and III only",
              "text": "II and III only"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "I, II, and III",
              "text": "I, II, and III"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "III only",
              "text": "III only"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 5,
            "html": "III only",
            "text": "III only",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],

            "answer_id": 12861610,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        },
        {
          "number": 2,
          "question_id": 85329,
          "task_id": 2828111,
          "task_type": "Review",
          "topic_id": 2838,
          "topic_name": "Modeling With the Poisson Distribution",
          "example_id": 7495,
          "example_title": "Computing the Probability of a Poisson Random Variable at Some Value",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T20:16:33.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 8:16 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 681768,
          "time_elapsed_display": "11:22",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>It is known that, on average, $6$ cell phones per batch produced by a cellphone factory have defects. What is the probability that the next batch contains exactly $4$ phones with defects? Round your answer to $3$ decimal places. You may assume that defective phones occur independently and at a constant average rate.</p>\n",
          "question_text": "It is known that, on average, $6$ cell phones per batch produced by a cellphone factory have defects. What is the probability that the next batch contains exactly $4$ phones with defects? Round your answer to $3$ decimal places. You may assume that defective phones occur independently and at a constant average rate.",
          "explanation_html": "<p>A Poisson random variable $X$ with rate $\\lambda$ has the following probability mass function:</p>\n<p>\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{\\lambda^x e^{-\\lambda}}{x!}\n\\]</p>\n<p>Let $X$ be the number of cell phones with defects. Since the average rate is $6$ cell phones per batch, and events occur independently and at a constant average rate, $X$ can be modeled as a Poisson random variable with rate $\\lambda = 6,$ and we can write $X\\sim \\textrm{Po}(6).$ </p>\n<p>So, $X$ has the following probability mass function:</p>\n<p>\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{6^x e^{-6}}{x!}\n\\]</p>\n<p>Therefore, the probability of having $4$ cell phones with defects in the next batch is</p>\n<p>\\[\\begin{align*}\nP(X=4) &= f(4) \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{6^{4} e^{-6}}{4!} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\approx 0.134\n\\end{align*}\\]</p>\n<p>rounded to $3$ decimal places.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "A Poisson random variable $X$ with rate $\\lambda$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{\\lambda^x e^{-\\lambda}}{x!}\n\\]\nLet $X$ be the number of cell phones with defects. Since the average rate is $6$ cell phones per batch, and events occur independently and at a constant average rate, $X$ can be modeled as a Poisson random variable with rate $\\lambda = 6,$ and we can write $X\\sim \\textrm{Po}(6).$\nSo, $X$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{6^x e^{-6}}{x!}\n\\]\nTherefore, the probability of having $4$ cell phones with defects in the next batch is\n\\[\\begin{align*}\nP(X=4) &= f(4) \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{6^{4} e^{-6}}{4!} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\approx 0.134\n\\end{align*}\\]\nrounded to $3$ decimal places.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$0.134$",
              "text": "$0.134$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$0.141$",
              "text": "$0.141$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$0.099$",
              "text": "$0.099$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$0.118$",
              "text": "$0.118$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$0.165$",
              "text": "$0.165$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$0.134$",
            "text": "$0.134$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",

        {
          "number": 3,
          "question_id": 85330,
          "task_id": 2828111,
          "task_type": "Review",
          "topic_id": 2838,
          "topic_name": "Modeling With the Poisson Distribution",
          "example_id": 7495,
          "example_title": "Computing the Probability of a Poisson Random Variable at Some Value",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T20:17:37.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 8:17 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 59105,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:59",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>An office secretary notes that, on average, $5$ calls are received per minute. What is the probability that she will receive $7$ calls in the next minute? Round your answer to $3$ decimal places. You may assume that calls occur independently and at a constant average rate.</p>\n",
          "question_text": "An office secretary notes that, on average, $5$ calls are received per minute. What is the probability that she will receive $7$ calls in the next minute? Round your answer to $3$ decimal places. You may assume that calls occur independently and at a constant average rate.",
          "explanation_html": "<p>A Poisson random variable $X$ with rate $\\lambda$ has the following probability mass function:</p>\n<p>\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{\\lambda^x e^{-\\lambda}}{x!}\n\\]</p>\n<p>Let $X$ be the number of calls per minute. Since the average rate is $5$ calls per minute, and events occur independently and at a constant average rate, the number of calls can be modeled as a Poisson random variable with rate $\\lambda = 5,$ and we can write $X\\sim \\textrm{Po}(5).$ </p>\n<p>So, $X$ has the following probability mass function:</p>\n<p>\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{5^x e^{-5}}{x!}\n\\]</p>\n<p>Therefore, the probability of having $7$ calls in the next minute is</p>\n<p>\\[\\begin{align*}\nP(X=7) &= f(7) \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{5^{7} e^{-5}}{7!} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\approx 0.104\n\\end{align*}\\]</p>\n<p>rounded to $3$ decimal places.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "A Poisson random variable $X$ with rate $\\lambda$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{\\lambda^x e^{-\\lambda}}{x!}\n\\]\nLet $X$ be the number of calls per minute. Since the average rate is $5$ calls per minute, and events occur independently and at a constant average rate, the number of calls can be modeled as a Poisson random variable with rate $\\lambda = 5,$ and we can write $X\\sim \\textrm{Po}(5).$\nSo, $X$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{5^x e^{-5}}{x!}\n\\]\nTherefore, the probability of having $7$ calls in the next minute is\n\\[\\begin{align*}\nP(X=7) &= f(7) \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{5^{7} e^{-5}}{7!} \\\\[5pt]\n&\\approx 0.104\n\\end{align*}\\]\nrounded to $3$ decimal places.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$0.104$",
              "text": "$0.104$"

          "id": 669,
          "name": "Finding the Common Difference of an Arithmetic Sequence",

      "question_count": 6,
      "questions": [
        {
          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 5433,
          "task_id": 2828675,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1346,
          "topic_name": "Finding the Nth Term of an Arithmetic Sequence Given Two Terms",
          "example_id": 12924,
          "example_title": "Finding the Nth Term Given the First Term and Another Term",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:44:22.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:44 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 511142,
          "time_elapsed_display": "8:31",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find the general formula for the $n$th term of an arithmetic sequence if the $15$th term is $40$ and the first term is $-2.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find the general formula for the $n$th term of an arithmetic sequence if the $15$th term is $40$ and the first term is $-2.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The formula for the $n$th term of an arithmetic sequence is given by </p>\n<p>\\[ a_n = a_1 + (n-1)d. \\]</p>\n<p>We already know that the first term is $a_1=-2,$ so we just need to figure out the common difference $d.$ </p>\n<p>To find the common difference, we can find the total difference between the terms $a_1=-2$ and $a_{15}=40$ and divide it by the number of jumps, as follows:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nd &= \\dfrac{a_{15} - a_1}{15-1} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{40 - (-2)}{14} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{42}{14} \\\\[5pt]\n&= 3\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>So,  the formula for the $n$th term of our arithmetic sequence is<br>\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\ta_n&= a_{1}+(n-1)d\\\\[3pt]\n \t\t & = -2 + (n-1) (3) \\\\[3pt]\n                & = -2 + 3n - 3\\\\[3pt]\n& = 3n - 5.\n  \t\n\t}\n\\]  </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The formula for the $n$th term of an arithmetic sequence is given by\n\\[ a_n = a_1 + (n-1)d. \\]\nWe already know that the first term is $a_1=-2,$ so we just need to figure out the common difference $d.$\nTo find the common difference, we can find the total difference between the terms $a_1=-2$ and $a_{15}=40$ and divide it by the number of jumps, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nd &= \\dfrac{a_{15} - a_1}{15-1} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{40 - (-2)}{14} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{42}{14} \\\\[5pt]\n&= 3\n\\end{align*}\nSo, the formula for the $n$th term of our arithmetic sequence is\\[\n\\eqalign{\na_n&= a_{1}+(n-1)d\\\\[3pt]\n& = -2 + (n-1) (3) \\\\[3pt]\n& = -2 + 3n - 3\\\\[3pt]\n& = 3n - 5.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$ a_n  = 3n - 5$",
              "text": "$ a_n = 3n - 5$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$ a_n  = 2n + 10$",
              "text": "$ a_n = 2n + 10$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$ a_n  = 4n - 20$",
              "text": "$ a_n = 4n - 20$"

              "number": 4,

              "text": "$ a_n = 4n - 10$"
            },

              "number": 5,
              "html": "$ a_n  = 3n + 15$",
              "text": "$ a_n = 3n + 15$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$ a_n  = 3n - 5$",
            "text": "$ a_n = 3n - 5$",
            "free_response": [],

        {
          "number": 2,
          "question_id": 19989,
          "task_id": 2828675,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1346,
          "topic_name": "Finding the Nth Term of an Arithmetic Sequence Given Two Terms",
          "example_id": 12924,
          "example_title": "Finding the Nth Term Given the First Term and Another Term",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:47:43.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:47 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 41366,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:41",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find the formula for the $n$th term of an arithmetic sequence if the $7$th term is $45$ and the first term is $3.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find the formula for the $n$th term of an arithmetic sequence if the $7$th term is $45$ and the first term is $3.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The formula for the $n$th term of an arithmetic sequence is given by</p>\n<p>\\[ a_n = a_1 + (n-1)d. \\]</p>\n<p>We already know that the first term is $a_1=3,$ so we just need to figure out the common difference $d.$ </p>\n<p>To find the common difference, we can find the total difference between the terms $a_1=3$ and $a_{7}=45$ and divide it by the number of jumps, as follows:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nd &= \\dfrac{a_{7} - a_1}{7-1} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{45 - 3}{6} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{42}{6} \\\\[5pt]\n&= 7\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>So,  the formula for the $n$th term of our arithmetic sequence is</p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\ta_n&= a_{1}+(n-1)d\\\\[3pt]\n \t\t & = 3 + (n-1) (7) \\\\[3pt]\n& = 3 + 7n - 7\\\\[3pt]\n                 & = 7n - 4.\n\t}\n\\]  </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The formula for the $n$th term of an arithmetic sequence is given by\n\\[ a_n = a_1 + (n-1)d. \\]\nWe already know that the first term is $a_1=3,$ so we just need to figure out the common difference $d.$\nTo find the common difference, we can find the total difference between the terms $a_1=3$ and $a_{7}=45$ and divide it by the number of jumps, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nd &= \\dfrac{a_{7} - a_1}{7-1} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{45 - 3}{6} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{42}{6} \\\\[5pt]\n&= 7\n\\end{align*}\nSo, the formula for the $n$th term of our arithmetic sequence is\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\na_n&= a_{1}+(n-1)d\\\\[3pt]\n& = 3 + (n-1) (7) \\\\[3pt]\n& = 3 + 7n - 7\\\\[3pt]\n& = 7n - 4.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$a_n  =  7n - 4$",
              "text": "$a_n = 7n - 4$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$a_n  =  6n + 3$",
              "text": "$a_n = 6n + 3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$a_n  =  5n + 10$",
              "text": "$a_n = 5n + 10$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$a_n  =  7n + 1$",
              "text": "$a_n = 7n + 1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$a_n  =  6n + 5$",
              "text": "$a_n = 6n + 5$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$a_n  =  7n - 4$",
            "text": "$a_n = 7n - 4$",

        {
          "number": 3,
          "question_id": 67315,
          "task_id": 2828675,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1346,
          "topic_name": "Finding the Nth Term of an Arithmetic Sequence Given Two Terms",
          "example_id": 12925,
          "example_title": "Finding the Nth Term Given Two Consecutive Terms",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:49:20.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:49 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 44011,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:44",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>The second and third terms of an arithmetic sequence are $45$ and $80,$ respectively. Determine the formula for the $n$th term of the sequence. </p>\n",
          "question_text": "The second and third terms of an arithmetic sequence are $45$ and $80,$ respectively. Determine the formula for the $n$th term of the sequence.",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The formula for the $n$th term is given by  </p>\n<p>\\[ a_n = a_1 + (n-1)d, \\]</p>\n<p>so we need to figure out the first term $a_1$ and the common difference $d.$ </p>\n<p>Since the terms $a_2=45$ and $a_3=80$ are consecutive, we can subtract them to find the common difference:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nd &= a_3-a_2 \\\\[3pt]\n&= 80- 45\\\\[3pt]\n&= 35\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Now, we can use one of our known values, say, $a_2=45,$ to determine the first term of the sequence. Since $a_2$ is $1$ jump after $a_1,$ we have:</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\eqalign{\na_2 &= a_1 + 1\\cdot d \\\\[3pt]\n45 &= a_1 +35\\\\[3pt]\n45-35 &= a_1\\\\[3pt]\n      a_1&= 10\n\t}\n\\]</p>\n<p>So the formula for the $n$th term of this arithmetic sequence is:  </p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\ta_n&= a_{1}+(n-1)d \\\\[3pt]\n \t\t& = 10 + (n-1) \\cdot  35\\\\[3pt]\n \t\t& =10 + 35n -35 \\\\[3pt]\n\t\t& = 35n -25\n\t}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The formula for the $n$th term is given by\n\\[ a_n = a_1 + (n-1)d, \\]\nso we need to figure out the first term $a_1$ and the common difference $d.$\nSince the terms $a_2=45$ and $a_3=80$ are consecutive, we can subtract them to find the common difference:\n\\begin{align*}\nd &= a_3-a_2 \\\\[3pt]\n&= 80- 45\\\\[3pt]\n&= 35\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we can use one of our known values, say, $a_2=45,$ to determine the first term of the sequence. Since $a_2$ is $1$ jump after $a_1,$ we have:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\na_2 &= a_1 + 1\\cdot d \\\\[3pt]\n45 &= a_1 +35\\\\[3pt]\n45-35 &= a_1\\\\[3pt]\na_1&= 10\n}\n\\]\nSo the formula for the $n$th term of this arithmetic sequence is:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\na_n&= a_{1}+(n-1)d \\\\[3pt]\n& = 10 + (n-1) \\cdot 35\\\\[3pt]\n& =10 + 35n -35 \\\\[3pt]\n& = 35n -25\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$a_n= 35n -25$",
              "text": "$a_n= 35n -25$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$a_n= 5n +25$",
              "text": "$a_n= 5n +25$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$a_n= 9n +27$",
              "text": "$a_n= 9n +27$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$a_n= 15n +15$",
              "text": "$a_n= 15n +15$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$a_n= 20n +5$",
              "text": "$a_n= 20n +5$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$a_n= 35n -25$",
            "text": "$a_n= 35n -25$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",

        {
          "number": 4,
          "question_id": 177701,
          "task_id": 2828675,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1346,
          "topic_name": "Finding the Nth Term of an Arithmetic Sequence Given Two Terms",
          "example_id": 12925,
          "example_title": "Finding the Nth Term Given Two Consecutive Terms",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:52:42.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:52 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 199758,
          "time_elapsed_display": "3:20",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>The $4$th and $5$th terms of an arithmetic sequence are $3$ and $-6,$ respectively. Determine the formula for the $n$th term of the sequence.</p>\n",
          "question_text": "The $4$th and $5$th terms of an arithmetic sequence are $3$ and $-6,$ respectively. Determine the formula for the $n$th term of the sequence.",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The formula for the $n$th term is given by \n\\[\na_n = a_1 + (n-1)d,\n\\]\nso we need to figure out the first term $a_1$ and the common difference $d.$ </p>\n<p>Since the terms $a_{4} = 3$ and $a_{5} = -6$ are consecutive, we can subtract them to find the common difference:\n\\begin{align*}\nd & = a_{5} - a_{4}\\\\[5pt]\n& = -6 - 3\\\\[5pt]\n& = -9\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Now, we can use one of our known values, say, $a_{4} = 3,$ to determine the first term of the sequence. Since $a_{4}$ is $3$ jumps after $a_1,$ we have\n\\begin{align*}\na_{4} & = a_1 + 3d \\\\[5pt]\n3 & = a_1 + 3(-9) \\\\[5pt]\n3 & = a_1 - 27\\\\[5pt]\n3 + 27 & = a_1\\\\[5pt]\na_1 & = 30.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>So, the formula for the $n$th term of this arithmetic sequence is\n\\begin{align*}\na_n & = a_{1} + (n-1)d \\\\[5pt]\n& = 30 + (n-1)(-9) \\\\[5pt]\n& = 30 - 9n + 9\\\\[5pt]\n& = 39 - 9n.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The formula for the $n$th term is given by\n\\[\na_n = a_1 + (n-1)d,\n\\]\nso we need to figure out the first term $a_1$ and the common difference $d.$\nSince the terms $a_{4} = 3$ and $a_{5} = -6$ are consecutive, we can subtract them to find the common difference:\n\\begin{align*}\nd & = a_{5} - a_{4}\\\\[5pt]\n& = -6 - 3\\\\[5pt]\n& = -9\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we can use one of our known values, say, $a_{4} = 3,$ to determine the first term of the sequence. Since $a_{4}$ is $3$ jumps after $a_1,$ we have\n\\begin{align*}\na_{4} & = a_1 + 3d \\\\[5pt]\n3 & = a_1 + 3(-9) \\\\[5pt]\n3 & = a_1 - 27\\\\[5pt]\n3 + 27 & = a_1\\\\[5pt]\na_1 & = 30.\n\\end{align*}\nSo, the formula for the $n$th term of this arithmetic sequence is\n\\begin{align*}\na_n & = a_{1} + (n-1)d \\\\[5pt]\n& = 30 + (n-1)(-9) \\\\[5pt]\n& = 30 - 9n + 9\\\\[5pt]\n& = 39 - 9n.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$a_n = 39 - 9n$",
              "text": "$a_n = 39 - 9n$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$a_n = 30 - 9n$",
              "text": "$a_n = 30 - 9n$"

            },

              "text": "$a_n = 31 - 7n$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$a_n = 39 - 9n$",
            "text": "$a_n = 39 - 9n$",
            "free_response": [],

        {
          "number": 5,
          "question_id": 177715,
          "task_id": 2828675,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1346,
          "topic_name": "Finding the Nth Term of an Arithmetic Sequence Given Two Terms",
          "example_id": 12927,
          "example_title": "Finding the Nth Term Given Two Non-Consecutive Terms",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:57:28.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:57 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 168601,
          "time_elapsed_display": "2:49",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find the $14$th term of an arithmetic sequence if the $3$rd term is $40$ and the $23$rd term is $-20.$ </p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find the $14$th term of an arithmetic sequence if the $3$rd term is $40$ and the $23$rd term is $-20.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The formula for the $n$th term is given by<br>\\[\na_n = a_1 + (n-1)d,\n\\] \nso we need to figure out the first term $a_1$ and the common difference $d.$</p>\n<p>To find the common difference, we can find the total difference between the terms $a_3 = 40$ and $a_{23} = -20$ and divide it by the number of jumps, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nd & = \\dfrac{a_{23} - a_3}{23 - 3} \\\\[5pt]\n& = \\dfrac{-20 - 40}{23 - 3} \\\\[5pt]\n& = \\dfrac{(-60)}{20} \\\\[5pt]\n& = -3\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Now, we can use one of our known values, say, $a_3 = 40,$ to determine the first term of the sequence. Since $a_3$ is $2$ jumps after $a_1,$ we have\n\\begin{align*}\na_3 & = a_1 + 2d \\\\[5pt]\n40 & = a_1 + 2(-3) \\\\[5pt]\n40 & = a_1 - 6 \\\\[5pt]\n40 + 6 & = a_1\\\\[5pt]\na_1 & = 46.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>So the formula for the $n$th term is:\n\\[\n\\begin{align}\na_n  &= 46 + (n-1)(-3)\\\\[5pt]\n        &= 46-3n+3\\\\[5pt]\n        &= 49-3n\n\\end{align}\n\\]</p>\n<p>Finally, we can find the $14$th term by substituting $n = 14$ into the above:\n\\begin{align*}\na_{14} & = 49-3(14)\\\\[5pt]\n& = 49-42 \\\\[5pt]\n& = 7\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The formula for the $n$th term is given by\\[\na_n = a_1 + (n-1)d,\n\\]\nso we need to figure out the first term $a_1$ and the common difference $d.$\nTo find the common difference, we can find the total difference between the terms $a_3 = 40$ and $a_{23} = -20$ and divide it by the number of jumps, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nd & = \\dfrac{a_{23} - a_3}{23 - 3} \\\\[5pt]\n& = \\dfrac{-20 - 40}{23 - 3} \\\\[5pt]\n& = \\dfrac{(-60)}{20} \\\\[5pt]\n& = -3\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we can use one of our known values, say, $a_3 = 40,$ to determine the first term of the sequence. Since $a_3$ is $2$ jumps after $a_1,$ we have\n\\begin{align*}\na_3 & = a_1 + 2d \\\\[5pt]\n40 & = a_1 + 2(-3) \\\\[5pt]\n40 & = a_1 - 6 \\\\[5pt]\n40 + 6 & = a_1\\\\[5pt]\na_1 & = 46.\n\\end{align*}\nSo the formula for the $n$th term is:\n\\[\n\\begin{align}\na_n &= 46 + (n-1)(-3)\\\\[5pt]\n&= 46-3n+3\\\\[5pt]\n&= 49-3n\n\\end{align}\n\\]\nFinally, we can find the $14$th term by substituting $n = 14$ into the above:\n\\begin{align*}\na_{14} & = 49-3(14)\\\\[5pt]\n& = 49-42 \\\\[5pt]\n& = 7\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$7$",
              "text": "$7$"
            },

              "number": 2,

              "text": "$-2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$-5$",
              "text": "$-5$"
            }
          ],

        {
          "number": 6,

          "topic_name": "Finding the Nth Term of an Arithmetic Sequence Given Two Terms",

          "format": "MC",

          "created_at": "2025-01-12T20:00:02.000Z",

          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find the  $11$th term of an arithmetic sequence if the $19$th term is $99$ and the $4$th term is $-6.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find the $11$th term of an arithmetic sequence if the $19$th term is $99$ and the $4$th term is $-6.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The formula for the $n$th term is given by  </p>\n<p>\\[ a_n = a_1 + (n-1)d, \\]</p>\n<p>so we need to figure out the first term $a_1$ and the common difference $d.$</p>\n<p>To find the common difference, we can find the total difference between the terms $a_{19}=99$ and $a_{4}=-6$ and divide it by the number of jumps, as follows:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nd &= \\dfrac{a_{19} -a_{4}}{19-4} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {99-(-6)}{19-4} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {105}{15} \\\\[5pt]\n&= 7\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Now, we can use one of our known values, say, $a_{4}=-6,$ to determine the first term of the sequence. Since $a_{4}$ is $3$ jumps after $a_1,$ we have:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\na_{4} &= a_1 + 3d \\\\[3pt]\n-6 &= a_1 + 3\\cdot 7 \\\\[3pt]\n-6 &= a_1 + 21 \\\\[3pt]\na_1 &= -27\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>So the formula for the $n$th term is:</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\begin{align}\na_n  &= -27 + (n-1)\\cdot 7\\\\[5pt]\n         &= 7n-34\n\\end{align}\n\\]</p>\n<p>Finally, we can find the $11$th term by substituting $n=11$ into the above:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\na_{11} \n&= 7(11)-34 \\\\[3pt]\n&= 77-34 \\\\[3pt]\n&= 43\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The formula for the $n$th term is given by\n\\[ a_n = a_1 + (n-1)d, \\]\nso we need to figure out the first term $a_1$ and the common difference $d.$\nTo find the common difference, we can find the total difference between the terms $a_{19}=99$ and $a_{4}=-6$ and divide it by the number of jumps, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nd &= \\dfrac{a_{19} -a_{4}}{19-4} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {99-(-6)}{19-4} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {105}{15} \\\\[5pt]\n&= 7\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we can use one of our known values, say, $a_{4}=-6,$ to determine the first term of the sequence. Since $a_{4}$ is $3$ jumps after $a_1,$ we have:\n\\begin{align*}\na_{4} &= a_1 + 3d \\\\[3pt]\n-6 &= a_1 + 3\\cdot 7 \\\\[3pt]\n-6 &= a_1 + 21 \\\\[3pt]\na_1 &= -27\n\\end{align*}\nSo the formula for the $n$th term is:\n\\[\n\\begin{align}\na_n &= -27 + (n-1)\\cdot 7\\\\[5pt]\n&= 7n-34\n\\end{align}\n\\]\nFinally, we can find the $11$th term by substituting $n=11$ into the above:\n\\begin{align*}\na_{11}\n&= 7(11)-34 \\\\[3pt]\n&= 77-34 \\\\[3pt]\n&= 43\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$43$",
              "text": "$43$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$37$",
              "text": "$37$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$39$",
              "text": "$39$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$47$",
              "text": "$47$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$49$ ",
              "text": "$49$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$43$",
            "text": "$43$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],

            "answer_id": 12861393,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "task_id": 2828112,
      "task_type": "Review",
      "topic_id": 1425,

      ],
      "question_count": 3,
      "questions": [
        {
          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 111795,
          "task_id": 2828112,
          "task_type": "Review",
          "topic_id": 1425,
          "topic_name": "Perfect Square Quadratic Equations with One or No Solutions",
          "example_id": 14820,
          "example_title": "Finding an Unknown Parameter",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:33:05.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:33 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 103093,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:43",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Which value of $q$ gives precisely one solution to the equation $x^2+q+3=7?$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Which value of $q$ gives precisely one solution to the equation $x^2+q+3=7?$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, we solve for $x$ as follows:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\tx^2+q+3 &= 7 \\\\[3pt]\n\tx^2\t\t&= 7-q-3 \\\\[3pt]\n     x^2\t\t&= 4-q \\\\[3pt]  \n\t\\sqrt{x^2}\t\t&= \\sqrt{4-q} \\\\[3pt]\n\t\\vert x\\vert\t&= \\sqrt{4-q} \n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>The equation has only one solution if the right-hand side is equal to zero, and the right-hand side is zero only if the expression underneath the radical is zero. So, we solve for the value of $q$ that makes the expression under the radical equal to zero:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\t4-q\t\t&= 0 \\\\[3pt]\n\tq\t&= 4\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, $q=4$ will give only one solution to the equation.  </p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, we solve for $x$ as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2+q+3 &= 7 \\\\[3pt]\nx^2 &= 7-q-3 \\\\[3pt]\nx^2 &= 4-q \\\\[3pt]\n\\sqrt{x^2} &= \\sqrt{4-q} \\\\[3pt]\n\\vert x\\vert &= \\sqrt{4-q}\n\\end{align*}\nThe equation has only one solution if the right-hand side is equal to zero, and the right-hand side is zero only if the expression underneath the radical is zero. So, we solve for the value of $q$ that makes the expression under the radical equal to zero:\n\\begin{align*}\n4-q &= 0 \\\\[3pt]\nq &= 4\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, $q=4$ will give only one solution to the equation.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$4$",
              "text": "$4$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$-3$",
              "text": "$-3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$7$",
              "text": "$7$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$-4$",
              "text": "$-4$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$10$",
              "text": "$10$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$4$",
            "text": "$4$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12860005,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        },
        {
          "number": 2,
          "question_id": 222406,
          "task_id": 2828112,
          "task_type": "Review",
          "topic_id": 1425,
          "topic_name": "Perfect Square Quadratic Equations with One or No Solutions",
          "example_id": 14817,
          "example_title": "Cases When the Perfect Square is Isolated",
          "format": "FR",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:33:52.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:33 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 44101,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:44",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "The solution to $-6(3u-8)^2 = 0$ is $u=$",
          "question_text": "The solution to $-6(3u-8)^2 = 0$ is $u=$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>We can solve this equation using the square root method:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n-6(3u-8)^2&= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n(3u-8)^2 &= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n\\sqrt{(3u-8)^2} &= \\sqrt{0} \\\\[5pt]\n\\vert 3u-8 \\vert  &= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n3u-8 &= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n3u &= 8 \\\\[5pt]\nu &= \\bbox[4pt,border: 1px solid lightgray]{\\dfrac{8}{3}}\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "We can solve this equation using the square root method:\n\\begin{align*}\n-6(3u-8)^2&= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n(3u-8)^2 &= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n\\sqrt{(3u-8)^2} &= \\sqrt{0} \\\\[5pt]\n\\vert 3u-8 \\vert &= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n3u-8 &= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n3u &= 8 \\\\[5pt]\nu &= \\bbox[4pt,border: 1px solid lightgray]{\\dfrac{8}{3}}\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": "[\n    answer:\\dfrac{8}{3};\n    type: number;\n    width: 30;\n    margin-left: 4;\n    margin-right: 1;\n    buttons: frac;\n].",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "[\nanswer:\\dfrac{8}{3};\ntype: number;\nwidth: 30;\nmargin-left: 4;\nmargin-right: 1;\nbuttons: frac;\n].",
          "choices": [],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": null,
            "html": null,
            "text": "\\frac{8}{3}",
            "free_response": [
              "\\frac{8}{3}"
            ],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12860053,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "FR"
          }
        },
        {
          "number": 3,
          "question_id": 222407,
          "task_id": 2828112,
          "task_type": "Review",
          "topic_id": 1425,
          "topic_name": "Perfect Square Quadratic Equations with One or No Solutions",
          "example_id": 14817,
          "example_title": "Cases When the Perfect Square is Isolated",
          "format": "FR",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:34:29.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:34 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 34722,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:35",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "The solution to $-3\\Big(2v+\\dfrac45\\Big)^2 = 0$ is $v=$",
          "question_text": "The solution to $-3\\Big(2v+\\dfrac45\\Big)^2 = 0$ is $v=$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>We can solve this equation using the square root method:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n-3\\Big(2v+\\dfrac45\\Big)^2&= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n\\Big(2v+\\dfrac45\\Big)^2 &= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n\\sqrt{\\Big(2v+\\dfrac45\\Big)^2} &= \\sqrt{0} \\\\[5pt]\n\\Big\\vert 2v+\\dfrac45 \\Big\\vert  &= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n2v+\\dfrac45 &= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n2v &= -\\dfrac45 \\\\[5pt]\nv &= \\bbox[4pt,border: 1px solid lightgray]{-\\dfrac{2}{5}}\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "We can solve this equation using the square root method:\n\\begin{align*}\n-3\\Big(2v+\\dfrac45\\Big)^2&= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n\\Big(2v+\\dfrac45\\Big)^2 &= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n\\sqrt{\\Big(2v+\\dfrac45\\Big)^2} &= \\sqrt{0} \\\\[5pt]\n\\Big\\vert 2v+\\dfrac45 \\Big\\vert &= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n2v+\\dfrac45 &= 0 \\\\[5pt]\n2v &= -\\dfrac45 \\\\[5pt]\nv &= \\bbox[4pt,border: 1px solid lightgray]{-\\dfrac{2}{5}}\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": "[\n    answer:-\\dfrac{2}{5};\n    type: number;\n    width: 40;\n    margin-left: 4;\n    margin-right: 1;\n    buttons: frac;\n].",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "[\nanswer:-\\dfrac{2}{5};\ntype: number;\nwidth: 40;\nmargin-left: 4;\nmargin-right: 1;\nbuttons: frac;\n].",
          "choices": [],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": null,
            "html": null,
            "text": "-\\frac{2}{5}",
            "free_response": [
              "-\\frac{2}{5}"
            ],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
