        {
          "number": 6,
          "question_id": 118982,
          "task_id": 2828473,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1102,
          "topic_name": "Problem Solving Using Vector Diagrams",
          "example_id": 5015,
          "example_title": "Identifying Vector Equalities",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:22:27.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:22 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 35270,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:35",
          "result": "incorrect",
          "correct": false,
          "question_html": "<p>In figure $ABCDE$ shown above, which of the following equalities are true?</p>\n<ol class=\"questionStatements\">\n<li>  $\\overrightarrow{AE}-\\overrightarrow{DE} = \\overrightarrow{AD}$ </li>\n<li>  $\\overrightarrow{AB}-\\overrightarrow{CB}+\\overrightarrow{CD}=\\overrightarrow{DA}$ </li>\n<li>  $\\overrightarrow{BC}-\\overrightarrow{DC}+\\overrightarrow{DE}=\\overrightarrow{BE}$ </li>\n</ol>\n\n",
          "question_text": "In figure $ABCDE$ shown above, which of the following equalities are true?\n$\\overrightarrow{AE}-\\overrightarrow{DE} = \\overrightarrow{AD}$\n$\\overrightarrow{AB}-\\overrightarrow{CB}+\\overrightarrow{CD}=\\overrightarrow{DA}$\n$\\overrightarrow{BC}-\\overrightarrow{DC}+\\overrightarrow{DE}=\\overrightarrow{BE}$",
          "explanation_html": "<ul>\n<li><p>Equality I is true. Indeed, \n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{AE}-\\overrightarrow{DE} &= \\overrightarrow{AE}+\\overrightarrow{ED}\\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{AD}.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>Equality II is false. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\t\\overrightarrow{AB}-\\overrightarrow{CB}+\\overrightarrow{CD}&=(\\overrightarrow{AB}+\\overrightarrow{BC})+\\overrightarrow{CD} \\\\[3pt]\n &= \\overrightarrow{AC}+\\overrightarrow{CD} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{AD}\\\\\n&\\ne \\overrightarrow{DA}.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>Equality III is true. Indeed, \n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{BC}-\\overrightarrow{DC}+\\overrightarrow{DE} &= \n(\\overrightarrow{BC}+\\overrightarrow{CD})+\\overrightarrow{DE}\\\\[3pt]\n&=\\overrightarrow{BD}+\\overrightarrow{DE}\\\\[3pt]\n& =\\overrightarrow{BE}.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Therefore, the correct answer is &quot;I and III only&quot;.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Equality I is true. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{AE}-\\overrightarrow{DE} &= \\overrightarrow{AE}+\\overrightarrow{ED}\\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{AD}.\n\\end{align*}\nEquality II is false. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{AB}-\\overrightarrow{CB}+\\overrightarrow{CD}&=(\\overrightarrow{AB}+\\overrightarrow{BC})+\\overrightarrow{CD} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{AC}+\\overrightarrow{CD} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{AD}\\\\\n&\\ne \\overrightarrow{DA}.\n\\end{align*}\nEquality III is true. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{BC}-\\overrightarrow{DC}+\\overrightarrow{DE} &=\n(\\overrightarrow{BC}+\\overrightarrow{CD})+\\overrightarrow{DE}\\\\[3pt]\n&=\\overrightarrow{BD}+\\overrightarrow{DE}\\\\[3pt]\n& =\\overrightarrow{BE}.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the correct answer is \"I and III only\".",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "I and III only",
              "text": "I and III only"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "II and III only",
              "text": "II and III only"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "I and II only",
              "text": "I and II only"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "I, II, and III",
              "text": "I, II, and III"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "I only",
              "text": "I only"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 4,
            "html": "I, II, and III",
            "text": "I, II, and III",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [
            {
              "id": 440636,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{arrows,shapes,positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings}\n\\tikzstyle arrowstyle=[scale=1]\n\\tikzstyle directed=[postaction={decorate,decoration={markings,\n    mark=at position .50 with {\\arrow[arrowstyle]{stealth}}}}]\n\\tikzstyle reverse directed=[postaction={decorate,decoration={markings,\n    mark=at position .50 with {\\arrowreversed[arrowstyle]{stealth};}}}]\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}\n\n\t% Grid\n\t\\begin{scope}\n\t\t\\draw [black!30, step=0.5] (-1,-1) grid (5,5);\n\t\\end{scope}\n\n\t% label coordinates\n\t\\coordinate [label={below left:$A$}] (A) at (0.5,0.5);\n\t\\coordinate [label={below right:$B$}] (B) at (3,0.5);\n\t\\coordinate [label={left:$C$}] (C) at (2.5,1.5);\n\t\\coordinate [label={above right:$D$}] (D) at (3.5,4);\n\\coordinate [label={above left:$E$}] (E) at (1.5,3);\n%\\coordinate [label={below right :$F$}] (F) at (1,1);\n\n\t% Figure\n\t\\draw[black, line width=1.2pt] \n\t\t(A) -- (B) -- (C) -- (D) -- (E) -- cycle;\n%\t\\draw[->, blue, line width=1.2pt, opacity=0.4] \n\t\t(A) -- (E) ; \n\n%\t\\draw[blue, thick,directed, opacity=0.4] (A) -- (E); node[midway,above=0.1cm]\n%\t\\draw[black, line width=1.2pt] \n\t\t(C) -- (E);\n   \n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118982"

        {
          "number": 7,
          "question_id": 118985,
          "task_id": 2828473,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1102,
          "topic_name": "Problem Solving Using Vector Diagrams",
          "example_id": 5015,
          "example_title": "Identifying Vector Equalities",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:23:46.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:23 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 49585,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:50",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>In figure $ABCDE$ shown above, which of the following equalities are true?</p>\n<ol class=\"questionStatements\">\n<li>  $\\overrightarrow{ED}-\\overrightarrow{CD} = \\overrightarrow{CE}$ </li>\n<li>  $\\overrightarrow{AE}-\\overrightarrow{DE}+\\overrightarrow{DC}=\\overrightarrow{AC}$ </li>\n<li>  $\\overrightarrow{CB}-\\overrightarrow{AB}-\\overrightarrow{EA}=\\overrightarrow{CE}$ </li>\n</ol>\n\n",
          "question_text": "In figure $ABCDE$ shown above, which of the following equalities are true?\n$\\overrightarrow{ED}-\\overrightarrow{CD} = \\overrightarrow{CE}$\n$\\overrightarrow{AE}-\\overrightarrow{DE}+\\overrightarrow{DC}=\\overrightarrow{AC}$\n$\\overrightarrow{CB}-\\overrightarrow{AB}-\\overrightarrow{EA}=\\overrightarrow{CE}$",
          "explanation_html": "<ul>\n<li><p>Equality I is false. Indeed, \n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{ED}-\\overrightarrow{CD} &= \\overrightarrow{ED}+\\overrightarrow{DC}\\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{EC}\\\\[3pt]\n&\\ne \\overrightarrow{CE}.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>Equality II is true. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\t\\overrightarrow{AE}-\\overrightarrow{DE}+\\overrightarrow{DC}&=(\\overrightarrow{AE}+\\overrightarrow{ED})+\\overrightarrow{DC} \\\\[3pt]\n &= \\overrightarrow{AD}+\\overrightarrow{DC} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{AC}.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>Equality III is true. Indeed, \n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{CB}-\\overrightarrow{AB}-\\overrightarrow{EA} &= \n(\\overrightarrow{CB}+\\overrightarrow{BA})-\\overrightarrow{EA}\\\\[3pt]\n&=\\overrightarrow{CA}+\\overrightarrow{AE}\\\\[3pt]\n& =\\overrightarrow{CE}.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Therefore, the correct answer is &quot;II and III only&quot;.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Equality I is false. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{ED}-\\overrightarrow{CD} &= \\overrightarrow{ED}+\\overrightarrow{DC}\\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{EC}\\\\[3pt]\n&\\ne \\overrightarrow{CE}.\n\\end{align*}\nEquality II is true. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{AE}-\\overrightarrow{DE}+\\overrightarrow{DC}&=(\\overrightarrow{AE}+\\overrightarrow{ED})+\\overrightarrow{DC} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{AD}+\\overrightarrow{DC} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{AC}.\n\\end{align*}\nEquality III is true. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{CB}-\\overrightarrow{AB}-\\overrightarrow{EA} &=\n(\\overrightarrow{CB}+\\overrightarrow{BA})-\\overrightarrow{EA}\\\\[3pt]\n&=\\overrightarrow{CA}+\\overrightarrow{AE}\\\\[3pt]\n& =\\overrightarrow{CE}.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the correct answer is \"II and III only\".",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "II and III only",
              "text": "II and III only"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "I and III only",
              "text": "I and III only"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "I and II only",
              "text": "I and II only"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "I, II, and III",
              "text": "I, II, and III"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "II only",
              "text": "II only"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "II and III only",
            "text": "II and III only",
            "free_response": [],

          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [
            {
              "id": 440637,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{arrows,shapes,positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings}\n\\tikzstyle arrowstyle=[scale=1]\n\\tikzstyle directed=[postaction={decorate,decoration={markings,\n    mark=at position .50 with {\\arrow[arrowstyle]{stealth}}}}]\n\\tikzstyle reverse directed=[postaction={decorate,decoration={markings,\n    mark=at position .50 with {\\arrowreversed[arrowstyle]{stealth};}}}]\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}\n\n\t% Grid\n\t\\begin{scope}\n\t\t\\draw [black!30, step=0.5] (-1,-1) grid (5.5,6);\n\t\\end{scope}\n\n\t% label coordinates\n\t\\coordinate [label={below right:$A$}] (A) at (3,0.5);\n\t\\coordinate [label={right:$B$}] (B) at (4,2.5);\n\t\\coordinate [label={above left:$C$}] (C) at (1.5,4.5);\n\t\\coordinate [label={right:$D$}] (D) at (2.5,2);\n\\coordinate [label={left:$E$}] (E) at (0.5,0.5);\n%\\coordinate [label={below right :$F$}] (F) at (1,1);\n\n\t% Figure\n\t\\draw[black, line width=1.2pt] \n\t\t(A) -- (B) -- (C) -- (D) -- (E) -- cycle;\n%\t\\draw[->, blue, line width=1.2pt, opacity=0.4] \n\t\t(A) -- (E) ; \n\n%\t\\draw[blue, thick,directed, opacity=0.4] (A) -- (E); node[midway,above=0.1cm]\n%\t\\draw[black, line width=1.2pt] \n\t\t(C) -- (E);\n   \n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",

        {
          "number": 8,
          "question_id": 118986,
          "task_id": 2828473,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1102,
          "topic_name": "Problem Solving Using Vector Diagrams",
          "example_id": 5015,
          "example_title": "Identifying Vector Equalities",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:25:47.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:25 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 117745,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:58",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>In figure $ABCDEFG$ shown above, which of the following equalities are true?</p>\n<ol class=\"questionStatements\">\n<li>  $\\overrightarrow{DC}-\\overrightarrow{BC} = \\overrightarrow{BD}$ </li>\n<li>  $\\overrightarrow{GF}-\\overrightarrow{EF}+\\overrightarrow{ED}=\\overrightarrow{GD}$ </li>\n<li>  $\\overrightarrow{BA}-\\overrightarrow{GA}-\\overrightarrow{FG}=\\overrightarrow{FB}$ </li>\n</ol>\n\n",
          "question_text": "In figure $ABCDEFG$ shown above, which of the following equalities are true?\n$\\overrightarrow{DC}-\\overrightarrow{BC} = \\overrightarrow{BD}$\n$\\overrightarrow{GF}-\\overrightarrow{EF}+\\overrightarrow{ED}=\\overrightarrow{GD}$\n$\\overrightarrow{BA}-\\overrightarrow{GA}-\\overrightarrow{FG}=\\overrightarrow{FB}$",
          "explanation_html": "<ul>\n<li><p>Equality I is false. Indeed, \n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{DC}-\\overrightarrow{BC} &= \\overrightarrow{DC}+\\overrightarrow{CB}\\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{DB}\\\\[3pt]\n&\\ne \\overrightarrow{BD}.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>Equality II is true. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\t\\overrightarrow{GF}-\\overrightarrow{EF}+\\overrightarrow{ED}&=(\\overrightarrow{GF}+\\overrightarrow{FE})+\\overrightarrow{ED} \\\\[3pt]\n &= \\overrightarrow{GE}+\\overrightarrow{ED} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{GD}.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n</li>\n<li><p>Equality III is false. Indeed, \n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{BA}-\\overrightarrow{GA}-\\overrightarrow{FG} &= \n(\\overrightarrow{BA}+\\overrightarrow{AG})-\\overrightarrow{FG}\\\\[3pt]\n&=\\overrightarrow{BG}+\\overrightarrow{GF}\\\\[3pt]\n& =\\overrightarrow{BF}\\\\[3pt]\n&\\ne \\overrightarrow{FB}.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Therefore, the correct answer is &quot;II only&quot;.</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Equality I is false. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{DC}-\\overrightarrow{BC} &= \\overrightarrow{DC}+\\overrightarrow{CB}\\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{DB}\\\\[3pt]\n&\\ne \\overrightarrow{BD}.\n\\end{align*}\nEquality II is true. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{GF}-\\overrightarrow{EF}+\\overrightarrow{ED}&=(\\overrightarrow{GF}+\\overrightarrow{FE})+\\overrightarrow{ED} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{GE}+\\overrightarrow{ED} \\\\[3pt]\n&= \\overrightarrow{GD}.\n\\end{align*}\nEquality III is false. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{BA}-\\overrightarrow{GA}-\\overrightarrow{FG} &=\n(\\overrightarrow{BA}+\\overrightarrow{AG})-\\overrightarrow{FG}\\\\[3pt]\n&=\\overrightarrow{BG}+\\overrightarrow{GF}\\\\[3pt]\n& =\\overrightarrow{BF}\\\\[3pt]\n&\\ne \\overrightarrow{FB}.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the correct answer is \"II only\".",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "II only",
              "text": "II only"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "I only",
              "text": "I only"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "III only",
              "text": "III only"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "I and II only",
              "text": "I and II only"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "II and III only",
              "text": "II and III only"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "II only",
            "text": "II only",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },

          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 70882,

          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1116,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Logarithmic Functions",
          "example_id": 3627,
          "example_title": "Differentiating the Product of a Constant and the Natural Logarithm Function",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:06:20.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:06 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 8305,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:08",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $y=4\\pi\\ln x$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $y=4\\pi\\ln x$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The derivative of the natural logarithm is the reciprocal function:</p>\n<p>\\[ \n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\ln x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x}\n\\]</p>\n<p>We apply our formula and get</p>\n<p>\\begin{align}\n\t\t\t\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(4\\pi\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&=4\\pi\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&=4\\pi\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x}\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&=\\dfrac{4\\pi}{x}.\n\\end{align}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The derivative of the natural logarithm is the reciprocal function:\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\ln x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x}\n\\]\nWe apply our formula and get\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(4\\pi\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=4\\pi\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=4\\pi\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x}\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\dfrac{4\\pi}{x}.\n\\end{align}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{4\\pi}{x}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{4\\pi}{x}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{4}{\\pi x}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{4}{\\pi x}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{\\pi}{4x}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{\\pi}{4x}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{4}{x+\\pi}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{4}{x+\\pi}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{x+\\pi}{4}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{x+\\pi}{4}$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$\\dfrac{4\\pi}{x}$",
            "text": "$\\dfrac{4\\pi}{x}$",
            "free_response": [],

          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $y=7\\pi\\ln x$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $y=7\\pi\\ln x$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The derivative of the natural logarithm is the reciprocal function:</p>\n<p>\\[ \n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\ln x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x}\n\\]</p>\n<p>We apply our formula and get</p>\n<p>\\begin{align}\n\t\t\t\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(7\\pi\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&=7\\pi\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&=7\\pi\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x}\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&=\\dfrac{7\\pi}{x}.\n\\end{align}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The derivative of the natural logarithm is the reciprocal function:\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\ln x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x}\n\\]\nWe apply our formula and get\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(7\\pi\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=7\\pi\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=7\\pi\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x}\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\dfrac{7\\pi}{x}.\n\\end{align}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",

        {
          "number": 3,
          "question_id": 17381,
          "task_id": 2828129,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1116,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Logarithmic Functions",
          "example_id": 3628,
          "example_title": "Differentiating a Sum Containing a Natural Logarithm Function",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:07:09.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:07 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 16673,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:17",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $y=\\ln x +e^x$, find the instantaneous rate of change at $x=1.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $y=\\ln x +e^x$, find the instantaneous rate of change at $x=1.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To find the instantaneous rate of change at a given point, we have to find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point.</p>\n<p>Taking the derivative, we have </p>\n<p>\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\ln x +e^x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\dfrac{1}{x}+e^x \\,.\n\\end{align}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the instantaneous rate of change at $x=1$ is \n\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\t\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=1}\n\t&=\\left.\\left(\\dfrac{1}{x}+e^x \\right)\\,\\right|_{x=1}\\\\[5pt]\n\t&=\\dfrac{1}{1}+e^1\\\\[5pt]\n\t&=1+e \\,.\n}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To find the instantaneous rate of change at a given point, we have to find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point.\nTaking the derivative, we have\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\ln x +e^x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\dfrac{1}{x}+e^x \\,.\n\\end{align}\nTherefore, the instantaneous rate of change at $x=1$ is\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=1}\n&=\\left.\\left(\\dfrac{1}{x}+e^x \\right)\\,\\right|_{x=1}\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\dfrac{1}{1}+e^1\\\\[5pt]\n&=1+e \\,.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$1+e$",
              "text": "$1+e$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$e$",
              "text": "$e$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$1$",
              "text": "$1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{e}{1+e}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{e}{1+e}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{1+e}{e}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{1+e}{e}$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$1+e$",
            "text": "$1+e$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },

        {

          "topic_id": 1116,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Logarithmic Functions",

          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:07:21.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:07 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 9676,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:10",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find $f'(x)$ for $f(x) = 2\\ln x - 5e^x.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find $f'(x)$ for $f(x) = 2\\ln x - 5e^x.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Taking the derivative, we have\n\\begin{eqnarray}\n    \\eqalign{\n \t f'(x)\t\t & = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(2\\ln x - 5e^x\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\n \t\t\t & =  2\\left(\\dfrac {1}{x}\\right) - 5e^x  \\\\[5pt]\n\n \t\t\t & =  \\dfrac {2}{x} - 5e^x.  \n \t\t\t\n       \n    }\n\\end{eqnarray}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Taking the derivative, we have\n\\begin{eqnarray}\n\\eqalign{\nf'(x) & = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(2\\ln x - 5e^x\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n& = 2\\left(\\dfrac {1}{x}\\right) - 5e^x \\\\[5pt]\n& = \\dfrac {2}{x} - 5e^x.\n}\n\\end{eqnarray}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\dfrac {2}{x} - 5e^x $",
              "text": "$\\dfrac {2}{x} - 5e^x $"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\\dfrac {2}{x} - e^x$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac {2}{x} - e^x$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\\dfrac {1}{x} - 5e^x$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac {1}{x} - 5e^x$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\\dfrac {2x}{3}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac {2x}{3}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$ 5e^x$",
              "text": "$ 5e^x$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {

            "text": "$\\dfrac {2}{x} - 5e^x $",

        {
          "number": 5,
          "question_id": 11908,
          "task_id": 2828129,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1116,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Logarithmic Functions",
          "example_id": 3629,
          "example_title": " Differentiating a Logarithmic Function With a General Base",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:08:27.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:08 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 20974,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:21",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $y=5\\log_7 x -5x+1$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $y=5\\log_7 x -5x+1$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To differentiate a logarithm with base $a,$ we use the formula</p>\n<p>\\[ \n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\log_a x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x\\ln a}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Therefore,</p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\t\t\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(5\\log_7 x -5x+1\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&=5\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\log_7 x\\right)-5\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(x)-\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(1)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&=5\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 7}-5\\cdot 1+0\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&=\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}-5.\n\n}\n\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To differentiate a logarithm with base $a,$ we use the formula\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\log_a x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x\\ln a}.\n\\]\nTherefore,\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(5\\log_7 x -5x+1\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=5\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\log_7 x\\right)-5\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(x)-\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(1)\\\\[5pt]\n&=5\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 7}-5\\cdot 1+0\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}-5.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}-5$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}-5$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 7}-5$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 7}-5$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}-5x+1$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}-5x+1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 7}-5x+1$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 7}-5x+1$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}-5$",
            "text": "$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}-5$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },

        {
          "number": 6,

          "task_id": 2828129,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1116,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Logarithmic Functions",
          "example_id": 3629,
          "example_title": " Differentiating a Logarithmic Function With a General Base",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:10:30.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:10 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 120870,
          "time_elapsed_display": "2:01",
          "result": "incorrect",
          "correct": false,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $y = 7 - 4 \\log_3 x$, find the instantaneous rate of change at $x=\\dfrac{1}{\\ln 3}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $y = 7 - 4 \\log_3 x$, find the instantaneous rate of change at $x=\\dfrac{1}{\\ln 3}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To differentiate a logarithm with base $a,$ we use the formula</p>\n<p>\\[ \n\t\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\log_a x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x\\ln a}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>To find the instantaneous rate of change at a given point, we have to find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point.</p>\n<p>Taking the derivative we have\n\\begin{align}\n\t\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}\n\t\t&= \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(7 - 4 \\log_3 x \\right)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&= \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(7)\n\t\t -4 \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\log_3 x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&= 0 - 4\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 3}\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&= - \\dfrac{4}{x \\ln 3}  \\,.\n\n\\end{align}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the instantaneous rate of change at $x=1/\\ln 3$ is \n\\begin{align}\n\t\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=1/\\ln 3}\n\t\t&= \\left.\\left( - \\dfrac{4}{x \\ln 3} \\right)\\,\\right|_{x=1/\\ln 3} \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&= - \\dfrac{4}{ 1} \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&= - 4.\n\\end{align}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To differentiate a logarithm with base $a,$ we use the formula\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\log_a x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x\\ln a}.\n\\]\nTo find the instantaneous rate of change at a given point, we have to find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point.\nTaking the derivative we have\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}\n&= \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(7 - 4 \\log_3 x \\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(7)\n-4 \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\log_3 x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&= 0 - 4\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 3}\\\\[5pt]\n&= - \\dfrac{4}{x \\ln 3} \\,.\n\\end{align}\nTherefore, the instantaneous rate of change at $x=1/\\ln 3$ is\n\\begin{align}\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=1/\\ln 3}\n&= \\left.\\left( - \\dfrac{4}{x \\ln 3} \\right)\\,\\right|_{x=1/\\ln 3} \\\\[5pt]\n&= - \\dfrac{4}{ 1} \\\\[5pt]\n&= - 4.\n\\end{align}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$- 4$",
              "text": "$- 4$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$1 - \\dfrac{1}{\\ln 3}$",
              "text": "$1 - \\dfrac{1}{\\ln 3}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$3$",
              "text": "$3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$0$",
              "text": "$0$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$ \\dfrac{1}{(\\ln 3)^2}$",
              "text": "$ \\dfrac{1}{(\\ln 3)^2}$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 3,
            "html": "$3$",
            "text": "$3$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],

        {
          "number": 7,
          "question_id": 1926,
          "task_id": 2828129,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1116,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Logarithmic Functions",
          "example_id": 3629,
          "example_title": " Differentiating a Logarithmic Function With a General Base",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:11:25.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:11 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 43693,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:44",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>$\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(2^{x} + \\log_2 x\\right) = $</p>\n",
          "question_text": "$\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(2^{x} + \\log_2 x\\right) = $",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To differentiate a logarithm with base $a,$ we use the formula</p>\n<p>\\[ \n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\log_a x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x\\ln a}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Therefore,</p>\n<p>\\begin{eqnarray}\n    \\eqalign{\n \t\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(2^{x} + \\log_2 x\\right) \n \t\t\t\t& =  2^x \\cdot \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x \\cdot \\ln 2} \\\\[5pt]\n \t\t\t\t& =  2^x \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x \\cdot \\ln 2}. \n \t\t\t\t\n \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\n       \n    }\n\\end{eqnarray}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To differentiate a logarithm with base $a,$ we use the formula\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\log_a x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x\\ln a}.\n\\]\nTherefore,\n\\begin{eqnarray}\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(2^{x} + \\log_2 x\\right)\n& = 2^x \\cdot \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x \\cdot \\ln 2} \\\\[5pt]\n& = 2^x \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x \\cdot \\ln 2}.\n}\n\\end{eqnarray}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$2^x\\ \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x\\ln 2} $",
              "text": "$2^x\\ \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x\\ln 2} $"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$2^x + \\dfrac {1}{x\\ln 2} $",
              "text": "$2^x + \\dfrac {1}{x\\ln 2} $"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$2^x + \\dfrac {1}{x}  $",
              "text": "$2^x + \\dfrac {1}{x} $"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$2^x\\ \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x} $",
              "text": "$2^x\\ \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x} $"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$2^x\\ \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{\\ln 2} $",
              "text": "$2^x\\ \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{\\ln 2} $"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {

            "html": "$2^x\\ \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x\\ln 2} $",
            "text": "$2^x\\ \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x\\ln 2} $",
            "free_response": [],

        {
          "number": 8,
          "question_id": 11900,
          "task_id": 2828129,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1116,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Logarithmic Functions",
          "example_id": 3629,
          "example_title": " Differentiating a Logarithmic Function With a General Base",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:11:44.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:11 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 16216,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:16",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $y=5\\log_4 x$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $y=5\\log_4 x$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To differentiate a logarithm with base $a,$ we use the formula</p>\n<p>\\[ \n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\log_a x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x\\ln a}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Therefore,</p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\t\t\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(5\\log_4 x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&=5\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\log_4 x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&=5\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 4}\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t&=\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 4}.\n\n}\n\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To differentiate a logarithm with base $a,$ we use the formula\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\log_a x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x\\ln a}.\n\\]\nTherefore,\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(5\\log_4 x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=5\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\log_4 x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=5\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 4}\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 4}.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 4}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 4}$"
            },

              "number": 2,

              "text": "$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 5}$"

            },

        {
          "number": 9,
          "question_id": 101726,
          "task_id": 2828129,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1116,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Logarithmic Functions",
          "example_id": 3630,
          "example_title": "Calculating the Slope of the Tangent Line of a Logarithmic Function at a Point",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:15:05.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:15 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 36101,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:36",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $y= 3 x- 5 + 8\\log_2 x$, find the slope of the tangent at $x=\\dfrac{1}{\\ln 2}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $y= 3 x- 5 + 8\\log_2 x$, find the slope of the tangent at $x=\\dfrac{1}{\\ln 2}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To find the slope of the tangent at a given point, we have to find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point.</p>\n<p>Taking the derivative, we have\n\\begin{align}\n\t\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}\n\t\t&=\t\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left( 3 x- 5 + 8\\log_2 x \\right)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&=\t\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(3 x)\n\t\t+\t\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(-5 \\right)\n\t\t+\t\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(8\\log_2 x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&=\t3\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}( x)\n\t\t-\t\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(5\\right)\n\t\t+\t8\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\log_2 x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&=\t3 - 0 + 8\\cdot \\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 2} \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&=\t3 + \\dfrac{8}{x \\ln 2} \\,.\n\\end{align}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=\\dfrac{1}{\\ln 2}$ is \n\\begin{align}\n\t\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=1/\\ln 2}\n\t\t&=\\left. \\left(3 + \\dfrac{8}{x \\ln 2} \\right)\\right|_{x=1/\\ln 2}\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&=3 + \\dfrac{8}{\\left( \\dfrac{1}{\\ln 2}\\cdot \\ln 2\\right) }\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&=3+8\\\\[5pt]\n        \t&=11 \\,.\n\\end{align}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To find the slope of the tangent at a given point, we have to find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point.\nTaking the derivative, we have\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}\n&= \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left( 3 x- 5 + 8\\log_2 x \\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(3 x)\n+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(-5 \\right)\n+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(8\\log_2 x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&= 3\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}( x)\n- \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(5\\right)\n+ 8\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\log_2 x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&= 3 - 0 + 8\\cdot \\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 2} \\\\[5pt]\n&= 3 + \\dfrac{8}{x \\ln 2} \\,.\n\\end{align}\nTherefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=\\dfrac{1}{\\ln 2}$ is\n\\begin{align}\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=1/\\ln 2}\n&=\\left. \\left(3 + \\dfrac{8}{x \\ln 2} \\right)\\right|_{x=1/\\ln 2}\\\\[5pt]\n&=3 + \\dfrac{8}{\\left( \\dfrac{1}{\\ln 2}\\cdot \\ln 2\\right) }\\\\[5pt]\n&=3+8\\\\[5pt]\n&=11 \\,.\n\\end{align}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$11$",
              "text": "$11$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$6$",
              "text": "$6$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$3+8\\ln 2$",
              "text": "$3+8\\ln 2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$8\\ln 2 - 2$",
              "text": "$8\\ln 2 - 2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$4$",
              "text": "$4$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$11$",
            "text": "$11$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],

          }

        {
          "number": 10,
          "question_id": 101722,
          "task_id": 2828129,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1116,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Logarithmic Functions",
          "example_id": 3630,
          "example_title": "Calculating the Slope of the Tangent Line of a Logarithmic Function at a Point",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:16:09.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:16 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 62120,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:02",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $y= 6\\log_{2}x $, find the slope of the tangent at $x=3.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $y= 6\\log_{2}x $, find the slope of the tangent at $x=3.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>To find the slope of the tangent at a given point, we have to find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point.</p>\n<p>Taking the derivative, we have\n\\[\n\t\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}=\\dfrac{6}{x\\ln 2} \\,.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Therefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=3$ is \n\\begin{align}\n\t\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=3}\n\t\t&=\\left.\\left(\\dfrac{6}{x\\ln 2} \\right)\\right|_{x=3}\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&=\\dfrac{6}{3\\ln 2}\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&=\\dfrac{2}{\\ln 2}  \\,.\n\\end{align}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "To find the slope of the tangent at a given point, we have to find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point.\nTaking the derivative, we have\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}=\\dfrac{6}{x\\ln 2} \\,.\n\\]\nTherefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=3$ is\n\\begin{align}\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=3}\n&=\\left.\\left(\\dfrac{6}{x\\ln 2} \\right)\\right|_{x=3}\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\dfrac{6}{3\\ln 2}\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\dfrac{2}{\\ln 2} \\,.\n\\end{align}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{2}{\\ln 2}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{2}{\\ln 2}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{1}{\\ln 2}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{1}{\\ln 2}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{1}{2\\ln 2}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{1}{2\\ln 2}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{1}{3\\ln 2}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{1}{3\\ln 2}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{3}{2\\ln 2}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{3}{2\\ln 2}$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$\\dfrac{2}{\\ln 2}$",
            "text": "$\\dfrac{2}{\\ln 2}$",
            "free_response": [],

      "question_count": 10,
      "questions": [
        {
          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 118272,
          "task_id": 2828116,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1100,
          "topic_name": "The Triangle Law for the Addition of Two Vectors",
          "example_id": 4272,
          "example_title": "Adding Two Vectors Using the Triangle Law",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T18:53:19.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 6:53 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 17443,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:17",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>The vectors $\\textbf{a}$ and $\\textbf{b}$ are shown in the diagram above. Which of the following could be $\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}?$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "The vectors $\\textbf{a}$ and $\\textbf{b}$ are shown in the diagram above. Which of the following could be $\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}?$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, we draw the vector that is equal to $\\mathbf{b}$ and has its tail placed at the head of $\\mathbf{a}.$ </p>\n<p>Now, according to the triangle law of addition, the resultant \n\\[\n\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}\n\\]\nis the vector that goes from the tail of $\\textbf{a}$ to the head of $\\textbf{b}$, as shown below.</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-q-118272-e-0\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/q-118272-e-0\" /></div>",
          "explanation_text": "First, we draw the vector that is equal to $\\mathbf{b}$ and has its tail placed at the head of $\\mathbf{a}.$\nNow, according to the triangle law of addition, the resultant\n\\[\n\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}\n\\]\nis the vector that goes from the tail of $\\textbf{a}$ to the head of $\\textbf{b}$, as shown below.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-118272-a-1\" />",
              "text": ""
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-118272-a-2\" />",
              "text": ""
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-118272-a-3\" />",

          "graphics": [
            {
              "id": 469570,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{arrows, arrows.meta} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usetikzlibrary{intersections} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n\t\n\t% Grid\n\t\\begin{scope}\n\t\t\\draw [black!20, step=0.5] (-3,-3) grid (3,3.5);\n\t\\end{scope}\n\t\n\t\\coordinate (A) at (-0.5,1)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (B) at (-2,1.5)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (C) at (0.5,-1)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (D) at (-0.5,3)  {};\n\n\t% Vector 1\n\t\\draw[fixstyle] (A) -- (B)\n\t\tnode[midway, below]{$\\textbf{a}$};\n\t\n\t% Vector 2\n\t\\draw[fixstyle, dashed] (0.5,-1) -- (2,0.5)\n\t\tnode[midway, below right=-2pt]{$\\textbf{b}$};\n\n\t% Vector 2\n\t\\draw[fixstyle] (B) -- (D)\n\t\tnode[midway, above left=-2pt]{$\\textbf{b}$};\n\t\n\t\t% Resultant\n\t\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue] (A) -- (D)\n\t\tnode[midway, right]{$\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118272-e-0"
            },
            {
              "id": 469569,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{arrows, arrows.meta} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usetikzlibrary{intersections} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, deepskyblue, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n\t\n\t% Grid\n\t\\begin{scope}\n\t\t\\draw [black!20, step=0.5] (-3,-1) grid (3,5.5);\n\t\\end{scope}\n\t\n\t\\coordinate (A) at (-0.5,1)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (B) at (-2,1.5)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (C) at (0.5,-1)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (D) at (-0.5,3)  {};\n\n\t% Vector 1\n%\t\\draw[->, line width=1.2pt, black, arrows={-latex}] (A) -- (B)\n\t\tnode[midway, below left]{$\\textbf{\\color{black}a}$};\n\t\n\t% Vector 2\n%\t\\draw[->, line width=1.2pt, black, opacity=0.5, dashed, arrows={-latex}] (0.5,-1) -- (2,0.5)\n\t\tnode[midway, below right]{$\\textbf{\\color{black}b}$};\n\n\t% Vector 2\n%\t\\draw[->, line width=1.2pt, black, arrows={-latex}] (B) -- (D)\n\t\tnode[midway, above left]{$\\textbf{\\color{black}b}$};\n\t\n\t\t% Resultant\n\t\\draw[fixstyle] (A) -- (1,3);\n%\t\tnode[sloped, midway, below]{$\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118272-a-5"
            },
            {
              "id": 469568,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{arrows, arrows.meta} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usetikzlibrary{intersections} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, deepskyblue, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n\t\n\t% Grid\n\t\\begin{scope}\n\t\t\\draw [black!20, step=0.5] (-3,-3) grid (3,3.5);\n\t\\end{scope}\n\t\n\t\\coordinate (A) at (-0.5,1)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (B) at (-2,1.5)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (C) at (0.5,-1)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (D) at (-0.5,3)  {};\n\n\t% Vector 1\n%\t\\draw[->, line width=1.2pt, black, arrows={-latex}] (A) -- (B)\n\t\tnode[midway, below left]{$\\textbf{\\color{black}a}$};\n\t\n\t% Vector 2\n%\t\\draw[->, line width=1.2pt, black, opacity=0.5, dashed, arrows={-latex}] (0.5,-1) -- (2,0.5)\n\t\tnode[midway, below right]{$\\textbf{\\color{black}b}$};\n\n\t% Vector 2\n%\t\\draw[->, line width=1.2pt, black, arrows={-latex}] (B) -- (D)\n\t\tnode[midway, above left]{$\\textbf{\\color{black}b}$};\n\t\n\t\t% Resultant\n\t\\draw[fixstyle] (C) -- (A);\n%\t\tnode[sloped, midway, below]{$\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118272-a-4"
            },
            {
              "id": 469567,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{arrows, arrows.meta} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usetikzlibrary{intersections} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, deepskyblue, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n\t\n\t% Grid\n\t\\begin{scope}\n\t\t\\draw [black!20, step=0.5] (-3,-3) grid (3,3.5);\n\t\\end{scope}\n\t\n\t\\coordinate (A) at (-0.5,1)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (B) at (-2,1.5)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (C) at (0.5,-1)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (D) at (-0.5,3)  {};\n\n\t% Vector 1\n%\t\\draw[->, line width=1.2pt, black, arrows={-latex}] (A) -- (B)\n\t\tnode[midway, below left]{$\\textbf{\\color{black}a}$};\n\t\n\t% Vector 2\n%\t\\draw[->, line width=1.2pt, black, opacity=0.5, dashed, arrows={-latex}] (0.5,-1) -- (2,0.5)\n\t\tnode[midway, below right]{$\\textbf{\\color{black}b}$};\n\n\t% Vector 2\n%\t\\draw[->, line width=1.2pt, black, arrows={-latex}] (B) -- (D)\n\t\tnode[midway, above left]{$\\textbf{\\color{black}b}$};\n\t\n\t\t% Resultant\n\t\\draw[fixstyle] (A) -- (C);\n%\t\tnode[sloped, midway, below]{$\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118272-a-3"

            {
              "id": 469566,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{arrows, arrows.meta} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usetikzlibrary{intersections} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, deepskyblue, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n\t\n\t% Grid\n\t\\begin{scope}\n\t\t\\draw [black!20, step=0.5] (-3,-1) grid (3,5.5);\n\t\\end{scope}\n\t\n\t\\coordinate (A) at (-0.5,1)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (B) at (-2,1.5)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (C) at (0.5,-1)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (D) at (-0.5,3)  {};\n\n\t% Vector 1\n%\t\\draw[->, line width=1.2pt, black, arrows={-latex}] (A) -- (B)\n\t\tnode[midway, below left]{$\\textbf{\\color{black}a}$};\n\t\n\t% Vector 2\n%\t\\draw[->, line width=1.2pt, black, opacity=0.5, dashed, arrows={-latex}] (0.5,-1) -- (2,0.5)\n\t\tnode[midway, below right]{$\\textbf{\\color{black}b}$};\n\n\t% Vector 2\n%\t\\draw[->, line width=1.2pt, black, arrows={-latex}] (B) -- (D)\n\t\tnode[midway, above left]{$\\textbf{\\color{black}b}$};\n\t\n\t\t% Resultant\n\t\\draw[fixstyle] (D) -- (A);\n%\t\tnode[sloped, midway, below]{$\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118272-a-2"

            {
              "id": 469565,

            {
              "id": 469564,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{arrows, arrows.meta} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usetikzlibrary{intersections} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\\usepgfplotslibrary{fillbetween} %% Can be useful, not essential\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty}\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n\t\n\t% Grid\n\t\\begin{scope}\n\t\t\\draw [black!20, step=0.5] (-3,-3) grid (3,3.5);\n\t\\end{scope}\n\t\n\t\\coordinate (A) at (-0.5,1)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (B) at (-2,1.5)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (C) at (0.5,-1)  {};\n\t\\coordinate (D) at (2,0.5)  {};\n\n\t% Vector 1\n\t\\draw[fixstyle] (A) -- (B)\n\t\tnode[midway, below]{$\\textbf{\\color{black}a}$};\n\t\n\t% Vector 2\n\t\\draw[fixstyle] (C) -- (D)\n\t\tnode[midway, below right=-2pt]{$\\textbf{\\color{black}b}$};\n\t\n\t\t% Resultant\n%\t\\draw[fixstyle] (A) -- (D)\n%\t\tnode[sloped, midway, below]{$\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118272"
