        {
          "number": 8,
          "question_id": 118289,
          "task_id": 2828116,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1100,
          "topic_name": "The Triangle Law for the Addition of Two Vectors",
          "example_id": 4359,
          "example_title": "Subtracting Two Vectors Using the Triangle Law",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:03:03.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:03 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 129094,
          "time_elapsed_display": "2:09",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>The diagram shows the vectors $\\mathbf{a}$ and $\\mathbf{b}.$  Which of the following diagrams illustrates the vector subtraction $-\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}.$ </p>\n",
          "question_text": "The diagram shows the vectors $\\mathbf{a}$ and $\\mathbf{b}.$ Which of the following diagrams illustrates the vector subtraction $-\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>We interpret  $-\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$ as the addition\n\\[\n(-\\mathbf{a})+\\mathbf{b},\n\\]\nwhere $-\\mathbf{a}$ is the vector that has the same magnitude as $\\mathbf{a}$ but goes in the direction <em>opposite</em> of $\\mathbf{a}.$</p>\n<p>Finally, we use the triangle law to add the vectors $-\\mathbf{a}$ and $\\mathbf{b}\\mathbin{:}$ </p>\n<ul>\n<li><p>We can start $\\mathbf{-a}$ from an arbitrary point on the plane. </p>\n</li>\n<li><p>Next, we draw the vector that is equal to $\\mathbf{b}$ and has its tail placed at the head of $\\mathbf{-a}.$ </p>\n</li>\n<li><p>Now, according to the triangle law of addition, the resultant is the vector that goes from the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ to the head of $\\textbf{b}$, as shown below.</p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<div id=\"graphic-q-118289-e-0\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/q-118289-e-0\" /></div>   ",
          "explanation_text": "We interpret $-\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$ as the addition\n\\[\n(-\\mathbf{a})+\\mathbf{b},\n\\]\nwhere $-\\mathbf{a}$ is the vector that has the same magnitude as $\\mathbf{a}$ but goes in the direction opposite of $\\mathbf{a}.$\nFinally, we use the triangle law to add the vectors $-\\mathbf{a}$ and $\\mathbf{b}\\mathbin{:}$\nWe can start $\\mathbf{-a}$ from an arbitrary point on the plane.\nNext, we draw the vector that is equal to $\\mathbf{b}$ and has its tail placed at the head of $\\mathbf{-a}.$\nNow, according to the triangle law of addition, the resultant is the vector that goes from the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ to the head of $\\textbf{b}$, as shown below.",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-118289-a-1\" />    ",
              "text": ""
            },
            {

          "graphics": [
            {
              "id": 469680,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\usepackage{xcolor}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n    \\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n    \\coordinate (b) at (-2,1) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (2,-1) {};\n \\coordinate (c) at (-2,1) {};\n  \\coordinate (d) at (-4,-1) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,-2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (-5,-5) {};\n  \\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n%\\draw[,  very thick, black,   arrows={-latex}]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, below right]{ $\\mathbf{a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway, above left=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$ };  \n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above right=-0.05cm, xshift=-3pt]{ $\\mathbf{-a}$}; \n%\\draw[->,  very thick, black,   arrows={-latex}]  (e) -- (f) node[midway, above left]{$\\mathbf{-b}$ };\n\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (d) -- (a) node[sloped,midway, below, xshift=-2pt]{ $-\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$};  \n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118289-a-4"
            },
            {
              "id": 469682,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\usepackage{xcolor}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n    \\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n    \\coordinate (b) at (-2,1) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (2,-1) {};\n \\coordinate (c) at (2,-1) {};\n  \\coordinate (d) at (0,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,-2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (-5,-5) {};\n  \\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[,  very thick, black,   arrows={-latex}]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, below right]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (x) node[midway, xshift=-3pt, above right=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway, below right=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n%\\draw[->,  very thick, black,   arrows={-latex}]  (e) -- (f) node[midway, above left]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (a) -- (d) node[midway, left]{$-\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$};  \n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118289-e-0"

            {
              "id": 469681,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\usepackage{xcolor}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n    \\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n    \\coordinate (b) at (-2,1) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (2,-1) {};\n \\coordinate (c) at (2,-1) {};\n  \\coordinate (d) at (0,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,-2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (-5,-5) {};\n  \\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[,  very thick, black,   arrows={-latex}]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, below right]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (x) node[midway, xshift=-3pt, above right=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway, below right=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n%\\draw[->,  very thick, black,   arrows={-latex}]  (e) -- (f) node[midway, above left]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (a) -- (x) node[sloped, pos=0.425, below]{$-\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$};  \n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",

              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\usepackage{xcolor}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n    \\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n    \\coordinate (b) at (-2,1) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (2,-1) {};\n \\coordinate (c) at (-2,1) {};\n  \\coordinate (d) at (-4,-1) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,-2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (-5,-5) {};\n  \\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n%\\draw[,  very thick, black,   arrows={-latex}]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, below right]{ $\\mathbf{a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway, above left=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$ };  \n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above right=-0.05cm]{ $\\mathbf{a}$}; \n%\\draw[->,  very thick, black,   arrows={-latex}]  (e) -- (f) node[midway, above left]{$\\mathbf{-b}$ };\n\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (a) -- (d) node[sloped,midway, below, xshift=-2pt]{ $-\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$};  \n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",

            {
              "id": 469677,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\usepackage{xcolor}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n    \\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n    \\coordinate (b) at (-2,1) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (2,-1) {};\n \\coordinate (c) at (2,-1) {};\n  \\coordinate (d) at (0,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,-2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (-5,-5) {};\n  \\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[,  very thick, black,   arrows={-latex}]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, below right]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (x) node[midway, xshift=-3pt, above right=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway, below right=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n%\\draw[->,  very thick, black,   arrows={-latex}]  (e) -- (f) node[midway, above left]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (a) -- (d) node[midway, left]{$-\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$};  \n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118289-a-1"

            {
              "id": 469676,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\usepackage{xcolor}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n    \\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n    \\coordinate (b) at (-2,1) {};\n \\coordinate (c) at (1,1) {};\n  \\coordinate (d) at (3,3) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (5,3) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (5,1.5) {};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above right=-0.04cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway, above left=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$}; \n\n\t\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118289"
            },
            {
              "id": 229187,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\usepackage{xcolor}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n    \\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n    \\coordinate (b) at (-2,1) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (2,-1) {};\n \\coordinate (c) at (2,-1) {};\n  \\coordinate (d) at (0,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,-2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (-5,-5) {};\n  \\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[,  very thick, black,   arrows={-latex}]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, below right]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (x) node[midway, xshift=-3pt, above right=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway, below right=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n%\\draw[->,  very thick, black,   arrows={-latex}]  (e) -- (f) node[midway, above left]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (a) -- (d) node[midway, left]{$-\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$};  \n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "1f304991cab2d7041811f168e8fa2eb7"
            }
          ],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12858573,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        },
        {
          "number": 9,
          "question_id": 118686,
          "task_id": 2828116,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1100,
          "topic_name": "The Triangle Law for the Addition of Two Vectors",
          "example_id": 4276,
          "example_title": "Adding More Than Two Vectors Using the Triangle Law",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:04:33.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:04 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 58869,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:59",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>The vectors $\\textbf{a},$ $\\textbf{b},$ and $\\textbf{c}$ are shown in the diagram above. Which of the following diagrams shows the vector $\\mathbf{-a-b+c}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "The vectors $\\textbf{a},$ $\\textbf{b},$ and $\\textbf{c}$ are shown in the diagram above. Which of the following diagrams shows the vector $\\mathbf{-a-b+c}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, we use the triangle law of addition to find \n\\[\n-\\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{b} = (-\\mathbf{a}) + (-\\mathbf{b}).\n\\]\nThe resultant $-\\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{b}$ is the vector that joins the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ and the head of $-\\textbf{b}\\mathbin{:}$</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-a824f6149dd1daf217887b59f439ff94\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/a824f6149dd1daf217887b59f439ff94\" /></div>\n\n<p>Now, we use the triangle law of addition to find \n\\[\n(-\\textbf{a}-\\textbf{b})+\\textbf{c}.\n\\]\nWe draw $\\textbf{c}$ from the head of $-\\textbf{a}-\\textbf{b}.$ So, we have the vector that goes from the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ to the head of $\\textbf{c}\\mathbin{:}$</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-07c918dd2646aec6be3e7e24c0ba9b22\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/07c918dd2646aec6be3e7e24c0ba9b22\" /></div>",
          "explanation_text": "First, we use the triangle law of addition to find\n\\[\n-\\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{b} = (-\\mathbf{a}) + (-\\mathbf{b}).\n\\]\nThe resultant $-\\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{b}$ is the vector that joins the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ and the head of $-\\textbf{b}\\mathbin{:}$\nNow, we use the triangle law of addition to find\n\\[\n(-\\textbf{a}-\\textbf{b})+\\textbf{c}.\n\\]\nWe draw $\\textbf{c}$ from the head of $-\\textbf{a}-\\textbf{b}.$ So, we have the vector that goes from the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ to the head of $\\textbf{c}\\mathbin{:}$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-118686-a-1\" />",
              "text": ""
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-118686-a-2\" />",
              "text": ""
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-118686-a-3\" />",
              "text": ""
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-118686-a-4\" />",
              "text": ""
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-118686-a-5\" />",
              "text": ""
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-118686-a-1\" />",
            "text": "",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [
            {
              "id": 230969,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.0,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (-3,-3.5) {};\n\\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway ,above right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway,above left=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway,left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (a) -- (d) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (f) node[sloped,midway, above=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b+c}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "a824f6149dd1daf217887b59f439ff94"
            },
            {
              "id": 230972,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.0,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (2,5) {};\n\\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway ,above right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway, below right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway,above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (d) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (a) -- (f) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b+c}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "07c918dd2646aec6be3e7e24c0ba9b22"
            },
            {
              "id": 469745,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.0,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (1,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (-3,1) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (-1,2) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-1,0) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (4,3) {};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, below left=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway, above left=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway, above left=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118686"
            },
            {
              "id": 469746,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.0,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (2,5) {};\n\\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway ,above right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway, below right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway,above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (d) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (a) -- (f) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b+c}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118686-a-1"
            },
            {
              "id": 469747,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.0,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (2,5) {};\n\\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway ,above right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway, below right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway,above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (d) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (f) -- (a) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b+c}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118686-a-2"
            },
            {
              "id": 469748,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.0,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (2,5) {};\n\\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway ,above right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway, below right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway,above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (d) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (b) -- (f) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b+c}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118686-a-3"

            {
              "id": 469749,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.0,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (2,5) {};\n\\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway ,above right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway, below right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway,above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (d) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (f) -- (b) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b+c}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118686-a-4"
            },
            {
              "id": 469750,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.0,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (2,5) {};\n\\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway ,above right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway, below right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway,above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (d) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (e) -- (f) node[sloped,midway, below right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b+c}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118686-a-5"
            },
            {
              "id": 469751,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.0,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (-3,-3.5) {};\n\\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway ,above right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway,above left=-0.05cm]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway,left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (a) -- (d) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (f) node[sloped,midway, above=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b+c}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118686-e-0"
            },
            {
              "id": 469752,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.0,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (-1,3) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (2,5) {};\n\\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway ,above right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (c) -- (d) node[midway, below right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway,above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (d) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (a) -- (f) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b+c}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-118686-e-1"

          ],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12858636,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        },
        {
          "number": 10,
          "question_id": 11746,
          "task_id": 2828116,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1100,
          "topic_name": "The Triangle Law for the Addition of Two Vectors",
          "example_id": 4276,
          "example_title": "Adding More Than Two Vectors Using the Triangle Law",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T19:05:33.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 7:05 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 56547,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:57",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>The vectors $\\textbf{a},$ $\\textbf{b},$ and $\\textbf{c}$ are shown in the diagram above. Which of the following diagrams shows the vector $\\mathbf{-a-b+c}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "The vectors $\\textbf{a},$ $\\textbf{b},$ and $\\textbf{c}$ are shown in the diagram above. Which of the following diagrams shows the vector $\\mathbf{-a-b+c}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, we use the triangle law of addition to find \n\\[\n-\\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{b} = (-\\mathbf{a}) + (-\\mathbf{b}).\n\\]\nThe resultant $-\\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{b}$ is the vector that joins the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ and the head of $-\\textbf{b}\\mathbin{:}$</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-7548200a617a59887c1d1478d8ab04d7\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/7548200a617a59887c1d1478d8ab04d7\" /></div>\n\n<p>Now, we use the triangle law of addition to find \n\\[\n(-\\textbf{a}-\\textbf{b})+\\textbf{c}.\n\\]\nWe draw $\\textbf{c}$ from the head of $-\\textbf{a}-\\textbf{b}.$ So, we have the vector that goes from the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ to the head of $\\textbf{c}\\mathbin{:}$</p>\n<div id=\"graphic-q-11746-e-1\" class=\"graphic\"><img src=\"/graphics/q-11746-e-1\" /></div>",
          "explanation_text": "First, we use the triangle law of addition to find\n\\[\n-\\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{b} = (-\\mathbf{a}) + (-\\mathbf{b}).\n\\]\nThe resultant $-\\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{b}$ is the vector that joins the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ and the head of $-\\textbf{b}\\mathbin{:}$\nNow, we use the triangle law of addition to find\n\\[\n(-\\textbf{a}-\\textbf{b})+\\textbf{c}.\n\\]\nWe draw $\\textbf{c}$ from the head of $-\\textbf{a}-\\textbf{b}.$ So, we have the vector that goes from the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ to the head of $\\textbf{c}\\mathbin{:}$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-11746-a-1\" />",
              "text": ""
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-11746-a-2\" />",
              "text": ""
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-11746-a-3\" />",
              "text": ""
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-11746-a-4\" />",
              "text": ""
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-11746-a-5\" />",
              "text": ""
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "<img src=\"/graphics/q-11746-a-1\" />",
            "text": "",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [
            {
              "id": 469306,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.5,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (2,3) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,-2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (-3,-3.5) {};\n\\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway ,above right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (x) node[midway, below right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (g) node[midway,above=0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway,left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (g) node[sloped,midway, above=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (a) -- (f) node[sloped,midway, above=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b+c}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "q-11746-e-1"
            },
            {
              "id": 230080,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.5,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (2,3) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,-2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (-3,-3.5) {};\n\\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway ,above right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (x) node[midway, below right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (g) node[midway,below left=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway,left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (a) -- (g) node[sloped,midway, above=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (f) node[sloped,midway, above=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b+c}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",
              "fileId": "7548200a617a59887c1d1478d8ab04d7"

            {
              "id": 469305,

            {
              "id": 469304,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.5,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (2,3) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,-2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (-3,-3.5) {};\n\\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway ,above right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (x) node[midway, below right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (g) node[midway,below left=-0.15cm]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway,left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (g) node[sloped,midway, above=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (a) -- (x) node[sloped,midway, above=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b+c}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",

            {
              "id": 469303,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.5,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, lightgray, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (2,3) {};\n\\coordinate (x) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (3,2) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (-2,-3) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (-3,-2) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (-3,-3.5) {};\n\\coordinate (g) at (-3,-2) {};\n\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n%\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway ,above right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (x) node[midway, below right=-0.1cm]{$\\mathbf{-a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (g) node[midway,below left=-0.15cm]{$\\mathbf{-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway,left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (g) node[sloped,midway, above=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle, ultra thick, deepskyblue]  (g) -- (a) node[sloped,midway, below=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{-a-b+c}$};\n\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",

            {
              "id": 469299,
              "tex": "\\documentclass{article}\n\\usepackage{pgfplots}\n\\usepackage{tikz}\n\\usetikzlibrary{shapes, calc, shapes, arrows, positioning}\n\\usetikzlibrary{decorations.markings, arrows.meta}\n\\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}\n\n\n\\begin{document}\n\\pagestyle{empty} %% IMPORTANT\n\\definecolor{deepskyblue}{rgb}{0.173,0.447,0.682}\n\\definecolor{orangepeel}{rgb}{0.902, 0.624, 0}\n\\definecolor{lightyellow}{rgb}{0.953,0.882,0.388}\n\\definecolor{darkgreen}{rgb}{0.271, 0.608, 0.463}\n\\definecolor{lightblue}{rgb}{0.484,0.702,0.894}\n\\definecolor{rustred}{rgb}{0.784,0.392,0.149}\n\\definecolor{fadedpink}{rgb}{0.753,0.494,0.643}\n\\begin{tikzpicture}[\nfont=\\boldmath, scale=1.5,\nfixstyle/.style={very thick, shorten <= 1pt, shorten >= 1pt, arrows={-Latex[scale=1]}}\n]\n    \n\\coordinate (a) at (0,0) {};\n\\coordinate (b) at (2,3) {};\n\\coordinate (c) at (4,2) {};\n\\coordinate (d) at (3,3) {};\n\\coordinate (e) at (5,3) {};\n\\coordinate (f) at (5,1.5) {};\n\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (a) -- (b) node[midway, above left=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{a}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (d) -- (c) node[midway ,above right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{b}$};\n\\draw[fixstyle]  (e) -- (f) node[midway,right=0.0cm]{$\\mathbf{c}$};\n\t\n\\end{tikzpicture}\n\\end{document}",

          "example_title": "Differentiating an Exponential Function with a Linear Exponent",

          "created_at": "2025-01-12T18:48:25.000Z",

          "time_elapsed_display": "0:20",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Calculate $f'(x)$ if $f(x)= -2e^{4x-1}+3.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Calculate $f'(x)$ if $f(x)= -2e^{4x-1}+3.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, we express $f(x)$ as the composite function\n\\[\nf = -2e^u+3, \\qquad u=4x-1.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Differentiating gives</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u} = -2e^u,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =4.\n\\]</p>\n<p>We now apply the chain rule, and get</p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\t\t\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}&=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}u} \\cdot \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}u} {\\textrm{d}x}\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t  &=-2e^u\\cdot 4\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t  &=-8e^u\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t  &=-8e^{4x-1}.\n\t\t    }\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, we express $f(x)$ as the composite function\n\\[\nf = -2e^u+3, \\qquad u=4x-1.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u} = -2e^u,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =4.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule, and get\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}&=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}u} \\cdot \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}u} {\\textrm{d}x}\\\\[5pt]\n&=-2e^u\\cdot 4\\\\[5pt]\n&=-8e^u\\\\[5pt]\n&=-8e^{4x-1}.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$-8e^{4x-1}$",
              "text": "$-8e^{4x-1}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$-8e^{4x-1}+3$",
              "text": "$-8e^{4x-1}+3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$-8e^{4x-2}$",
              "text": "$-8e^{4x-2}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$8e^{4x-1}+3$",
              "text": "$8e^{4x-1}+3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\dfrac 1 2e^{4x-1}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac 1 2e^{4x-1}$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$-8e^{4x-1}$",
            "text": "$-8e^{4x-1}$",
            "free_response": [],

          },
          "hints": [],

        },
        {
          "number": 2,
          "question_id": 48003,
          "task_id": 2828115,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1007,
          "topic_name": "The Chain Rule With Exponential Functions",
          "example_id": 727,
          "example_title": "Differentiating an Exponential Function with a Linear Exponent",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T18:48:39.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 6:48 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 11040,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:11",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $y =-3e^{5-x},$ find $\\dfrac {\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x}$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $y =-3e^{5-x},$ find $\\dfrac {\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x}$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, we express $y$ as the composite function\n\\[\ny =-3e^u, \\qquad u=5-x.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Differentiating gives</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} = -3e^u,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =-1.\n\\]</p>\n<p>We now apply the chain rule, and get</p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\t\t\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}&=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}u} \\cdot \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}u} {\\textrm{d}x}\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t  &=-3e^u\\cdot (-1)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t  &=3e^u\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t  &=3e^{5-x}.\n\t\t    }\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, we express $y$ as the composite function\n\\[\ny =-3e^u, \\qquad u=5-x.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} = -3e^u,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =-1.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule, and get\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}&=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}u} \\cdot \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}u} {\\textrm{d}x}\\\\[5pt]\n&=-3e^u\\cdot (-1)\\\\[5pt]\n&=3e^u\\\\[5pt]\n&=3e^{5-x}.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$3e^{5-x}$",
              "text": "$3e^{5-x}$"
            },
            {

              "html": "$-3e^{5-x}$",
              "text": "$-3e^{5-x}$"
            },

              "text": "$-e^{5-x}$"
            },
            {

              "text": "$-15e^{x-5}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$15e^{5-x}$",
              "text": "$15e^{5-x}$"
            }
          ],

        {
          "number": 3,
          "question_id": 107437,
          "task_id": 2828115,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1007,
          "topic_name": "The Chain Rule With Exponential Functions",
          "example_id": 729,
          "example_title": "Differentiating an Exponential Function with a Nonlinear Exponent",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T18:49:25.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 6:49 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 14829,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:15",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find $f'(x)$ for $f(x) =e^{x^5+1} .$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find $f'(x)$ for $f(x) =e^{x^5+1} .$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First we express $f(x)$ as the composite function</p>\n<p>\\[\nf = e^u, \\qquad u=x^5+1.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Differentiating gives</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u} = e^u,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =5x^4.\n\\]</p>\n<p>We now apply the chain rule, and get</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d x} &= \\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[5pt]\n&=e^u \\cdot (5x^4)\\\\[5pt]\n&=5x^4e^u\\\\[5pt]\n&=5x^4e^{x^5+1}.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First we express $f(x)$ as the composite function\n\\[\nf = e^u, \\qquad u=x^5+1.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u} = e^u,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =5x^4.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule, and get\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d x} &= \\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[5pt]\n&=e^u \\cdot (5x^4)\\\\[5pt]\n&=5x^4e^u\\\\[5pt]\n&=5x^4e^{x^5+1}.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$5x^4e^{x^5+1}$",
              "text": "$5x^4e^{x^5+1}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$x^5e^{x^5+1}$",
              "text": "$x^5e^{x^5+1}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$4x^5e^{x^5+1}$",
              "text": "$4x^5e^{x^5+1}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$-5x^4e^{x^5+1}$",
              "text": "$-5x^4e^{x^5+1}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{1}{5}x^2e^{x^5+1}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{1}{5}x^2e^{x^5+1}$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$5x^4e^{x^5+1}$",
            "text": "$5x^4e^{x^5+1}$",
            "free_response": [],

          },
          "hints": [],

        {
          "number": 4,

          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1007,
          "topic_name": "The Chain Rule With Exponential Functions",
          "example_id": 729,
          "example_title": "Differentiating an Exponential Function with a Nonlinear Exponent",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T18:49:40.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 6:49 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 12633,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:13",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find $f'(x)$ for $f(x) =e^{2x^6-4} .$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find $f'(x)$ for $f(x) =e^{2x^6-4} .$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First we express $f(x)$ as the composite function</p>\n<p>\\[\nf = e^u, \\qquad u=2x^6-4.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Differentiating gives</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u} = e^u,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =12x^5.\n\\]</p>\n<p>We now apply the chain rule, and get</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d x} &= \\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[5pt]\n&=e^u \\cdot (12x^5)\\\\[5pt]\n&=12x^5e^u\\\\[5pt]\n&=12x^5e^{2x^6-4}.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First we express $f(x)$ as the composite function\n\\[\nf = e^u, \\qquad u=2x^6-4.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u} = e^u,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =12x^5.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule, and get\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d x} &= \\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[5pt]\n&=e^u \\cdot (12x^5)\\\\[5pt]\n&=12x^5e^u\\\\[5pt]\n&=12x^5e^{2x^6-4}.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$12x^5e^{2x^6-4}$",
              "text": "$12x^5e^{2x^6-4}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$6x^5e^{2x^6-4}$",
              "text": "$6x^5e^{2x^6-4}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$2x^5e^{2x^6-4}$",
              "text": "$2x^5e^{2x^6-4}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$12x^4e^{x^6+4}$",
              "text": "$12x^4e^{x^6+4}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$4x^5e^{2x^6-4}$",
              "text": "$4x^5e^{2x^6-4}$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$12x^5e^{2x^6-4}$",
            "text": "$12x^5e^{2x^6-4}$",
            "free_response": [],

        {
          "number": 5,
          "question_id": 48633,
          "task_id": 2828115,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1007,
          "topic_name": "The Chain Rule With Exponential Functions",
          "example_id": 728,
          "example_title": "Differentiating a General Exponential Function Using the Chain Rule",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T18:51:41.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 6:51 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 75765,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:16",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find the slope of the tangent to the curve $y=2^{9-x^3}$ at the point where $x=2.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find the slope of the tangent to the curve $y=2^{9-x^3}$ at the point where $x=2.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, we express $y$ as the composite function\n\\[\ny = 2^u, \\qquad u=9-x^3.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Differentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} = 2^u\\ln 2,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =-3x^2.\n\\]</p>\n<p>We now apply the chain rule and get\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d} y}{\\textrm{d}x}\n&= \\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} \\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[5pt]\n&= 2^u \\ln 2\\cdot \\left(-3x^2\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&= -\\left(3x^2\\right)2^{9-x^3} \\ln 2.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Finally, to find the slope of the tangent to the curve at $x=2$, we have to evaluate $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=2}\n&= \\left.-\\left(3x^2\\right)2^{9-x^3} \\ln 2\\right|_{x=2}\\\\[5pt]\n&= -\\left(3(2)^2\\right)2^{9-(2)^3} \\ln 2\\\\[5pt]\n&= -24 \\ln 2\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, we express $y$ as the composite function\n\\[\ny = 2^u, \\qquad u=9-x^3.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} = 2^u\\ln 2,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =-3x^2.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule and get\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d} y}{\\textrm{d}x}\n&= \\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} \\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[5pt]\n&= 2^u \\ln 2\\cdot \\left(-3x^2\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&= -\\left(3x^2\\right)2^{9-x^3} \\ln 2.\n\\end{align*}\nFinally, to find the slope of the tangent to the curve at $x=2$, we have to evaluate $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=2}\n&= \\left.-\\left(3x^2\\right)2^{9-x^3} \\ln 2\\right|_{x=2}\\\\[5pt]\n&= -\\left(3(2)^2\\right)2^{9-(2)^3} \\ln 2\\\\[5pt]\n&= -24 \\ln 2\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$-24 \\ln 2$",
              "text": "$-24 \\ln 2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$12 \\ln 2$",
              "text": "$12 \\ln 2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$24 \\ln 2$",
              "text": "$24 \\ln 2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$-48 \\ln 2$",
              "text": "$-48 \\ln 2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$-12 \\ln 2$",
              "text": "$-12 \\ln 2$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$-24 \\ln 2$",
            "text": "$-24 \\ln 2$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],

          }
        },
        {
          "number": 6,
          "question_id": 71880,
          "task_id": 2828115,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1007,
          "topic_name": "The Chain Rule With Exponential Functions",
          "example_id": 728,
          "example_title": "Differentiating a General Exponential Function Using the Chain Rule",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T18:52:07.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 6:52 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 22783,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:23",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>If $f(x)=4^{x^3},$ find $f'(x).$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "If $f(x)=4^{x^3},$ find $f'(x).$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, we express $f(x)$ as the composite function</p>\n<p>\\[\nf = 4^u, \\qquad u=x^3.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Differentiating gives</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u} = 4^u\\ln 4,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =3x^2.\n\\]</p>\n<p>We now apply the chain rule, and get</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d x} &= \\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[5pt]\n&=4^u\\ln 4 \\cdot (3x^2)\\\\[5pt]\n&=3x^2 \\cdot 4^u\\ln 4\\\\[5pt]\n&=3x^2 \\cdot 4^{x^3}\\ln 4.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, we express $f(x)$ as the composite function\n\\[\nf = 4^u, \\qquad u=x^3.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u} = 4^u\\ln 4,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =3x^2.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule, and get\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d x} &= \\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[5pt]\n&=4^u\\ln 4 \\cdot (3x^2)\\\\[5pt]\n&=3x^2 \\cdot 4^u\\ln 4\\\\[5pt]\n&=3x^2 \\cdot 4^{x^3}\\ln 4.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$3x^2 \\cdot 4^{x^3}\\ln 4$",
              "text": "$3x^2 \\cdot 4^{x^3}\\ln 4$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$3\\cdot 4^{2x^3}\\ln 4$",
              "text": "$3\\cdot 4^{2x^3}\\ln 4$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$x^2 \\cdot 4^{x^3}$",
              "text": "$x^2 \\cdot 4^{x^3}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$3x^7 \\cdot 4^{x^5}\\ln 4$",
              "text": "$3x^7 \\cdot 4^{x^5}\\ln 4$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$3x^2 \\cdot 4^{x^2}$",
              "text": "$3x^2 \\cdot 4^{x^2}$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$3x^2 \\cdot 4^{x^3}\\ln 4$",
            "text": "$3x^2 \\cdot 4^{x^3}\\ln 4$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],

          "example_title": "Evaluating Combinations",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T18:39:25.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 6:39 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 6848,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:07",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Evaluate ${}_{10}C_1.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Evaluate ${}_{10}C_1.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>In general, the number of combinations of $n$ objects taken $r$ at a time is given by the formula\n\\[\n{}_nC_r = \\dfrac{n!}{(n-r)!\\,r!}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Substituting $n=10$ and $r=1,$ we get</p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\require{cancel}\n\t\\eqalign{\n                            {}_{10}C_1 &= \\dfrac {10!} {(10 - 1)!\\,1!} \\\\[5pt]\n                            &= \\dfrac {10!} {9!\\,1!} \\\\[5pt]\n                            &= \\dfrac {10 \\cdot \\cancel{9!}} {\\cancel{9!}1!} \\\\[5pt]\n                            &= \\dfrac {10 } {1} \\\\[5pt]\n                            &= 10.\n\t}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "In general, the number of combinations of $n$ objects taken $r$ at a time is given by the formula\n\\[\n{}_nC_r = \\dfrac{n!}{(n-r)!\\,r!}.\n\\]\nSubstituting $n=10$ and $r=1,$ we get\n\\[\n\\require{cancel}\n\\eqalign{\n{}_{10}C_1 &= \\dfrac {10!} {(10 - 1)!\\,1!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {10!} {9!\\,1!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {10 \\cdot \\cancel{9!}} {\\cancel{9!}1!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {10 } {1} \\\\[5pt]\n&= 10.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$10$",
              "text": "$10$"

          "number": 2,

          "topic_id": 705,

          "created_at": "2025-01-12T18:39:58.000Z",

          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Evaluate $_{20}C_{3}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Evaluate $_{20}C_{3}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>In general, the number of combinations of $n$ objects taken $r$ at a time is given by the formula</p>\n<p>\\[\n{}_nC_r = \\dfrac{n!}{(n-r)!\\,r!}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Substituting $n=20$ and $r=3,$ we get</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\require{cancel}\n\t\\eqalign{\n                          {}_{20}C_3 &= \\dfrac {20!} {(20 - 3)!\\,3!} \\\\[5pt]\n                            &= \\dfrac {20!} {17!\\,3!} \\\\[5pt]\n                            &= \\dfrac {20 \\cdot 19 \\cdot 18 \\cdot \\cancel{17!}} {\\cancel{17!}3!} \\\\[5pt]\n                            &= \\dfrac {20 \\cdot 19 \\cdot 18 } {6} \\\\[5pt]\n                            &= 1\\,140.\n\t}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "In general, the number of combinations of $n$ objects taken $r$ at a time is given by the formula\n\\[\n{}_nC_r = \\dfrac{n!}{(n-r)!\\,r!}.\n\\]\nSubstituting $n=20$ and $r=3,$ we get\n\\[\n\\require{cancel}\n\\eqalign{\n{}_{20}C_3 &= \\dfrac {20!} {(20 - 3)!\\,3!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {20!} {17!\\,3!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {20 \\cdot 19 \\cdot 18 \\cdot \\cancel{17!}} {\\cancel{17!}3!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {20 \\cdot 19 \\cdot 18 } {6} \\\\[5pt]\n&= 1\\,140.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {

        {

          "created_at": "2025-01-12T18:42:41.000Z",

          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Evaluate $\\displaystyle{{100}\\choose{98}}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Evaluate $\\displaystyle{{100}\\choose{98}}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, note that $\\displaystyle{{100}\\choose{98}}$ means the same thing as $_{100}C_{98}.$</p>\n<p> In general, the number of combinations of $n$ objects taken $r$ at a time is given by the formula\n\\[\n{}_nC_r = \\dfrac{n!}{(n-r)!\\,r!}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Substituting $n=100$ and $r=98,$ we get</p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\require{cancel}\n\t\\eqalign{\n                            _{100}C_{98} &= \\dfrac {100!} {(100 - 98)!\\,98!} \\\\[5pt]\n                            &= \\dfrac {100!} {2!\\,98!} \\\\[5pt]\n                            &= \\dfrac {100\\cdot 99\\cdot\\cancel{98!}} {2\\,\\cancel{98!}} \\\\[5pt]\n                                     &= 4\\,950.\n\t}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, note that $\\displaystyle{{100}\\choose{98}}$ means the same thing as $_{100}C_{98}.$\nIn general, the number of combinations of $n$ objects taken $r$ at a time is given by the formula\n\\[\n{}_nC_r = \\dfrac{n!}{(n-r)!\\,r!}.\n\\]\nSubstituting $n=100$ and $r=98,$ we get\n\\[\n\\require{cancel}\n\\eqalign{\n_{100}C_{98} &= \\dfrac {100!} {(100 - 98)!\\,98!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {100!} {2!\\,98!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {100\\cdot 99\\cdot\\cancel{98!}} {2\\,\\cancel{98!}} \\\\[5pt]\n&= 4\\,950.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$4\\,950$",
              "text": "$4\\,950$"

              "number": 5,

            }
          ],

        {
          "number": 4,

          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T18:42:56.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 6:42 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 12902,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:13",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Evaluate $\\begin{pmatrix}15\\\\2\\end{pmatrix}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Evaluate $\\begin{pmatrix}15\\\\2\\end{pmatrix}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, note that $\\displaystyle{{15}\\choose{2}}$ means the same thing as $_{15}C_2.$</p>\n<p> In general, the number of combinations of $n$ objects taken $r$ at a time is given by the formula\n\\[\n{}_nC_r = \\dfrac{n!}{(n-r)!\\,r!}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Substituting $n=15$ and $r=2,$ we get</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\require{cancel}\n\t\\eqalign{   _{15}C_2\\ &= \\dfrac{15!}{(15-2)!2!} \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t&= \\dfrac{15!}{13!\\cdot 2!} \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t&= \\dfrac{15 \\cdot 14 \\cdot \\cancel{13!}}{\\cancel{13!}\\cdot 2!} \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t&= 105.\n\t\t\n\t}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, note that $\\displaystyle{{15}\\choose{2}}$ means the same thing as $_{15}C_2.$\nIn general, the number of combinations of $n$ objects taken $r$ at a time is given by the formula\n\\[\n{}_nC_r = \\dfrac{n!}{(n-r)!\\,r!}.\n\\]\nSubstituting $n=15$ and $r=2,$ we get\n\\[\n\\require{cancel}\n\\eqalign{ _{15}C_2\\ &= \\dfrac{15!}{(15-2)!2!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{15!}{13!\\cdot 2!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{15 \\cdot 14 \\cdot \\cancel{13!}}{\\cancel{13!}\\cdot 2!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= 105.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$105$",
              "text": "$105$"

          ],

        {
          "number": 5,
          "question_id": 86823,
          "task_id": 2828114,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 705,
          "topic_name": "Combinations",
          "example_id": 5677,
          "example_title": "Calculating the Number of Combinations of Objects Chosen From a Group Fewer Than Ten",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T18:44:13.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 6:44 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 42001,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:42",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>There were $9$ chess players who took part in a chess tournament. How many games were played in the tournament if each player faced every other player exactly once?</p>\n",
          "question_text": "There were $9$ chess players who took part in a chess tournament. How many games were played in the tournament if each player faced every other player exactly once?",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The number of combinations of $n$ objects taken $r$ at a time is given by the formula\n\\[\n{}_nC_r = \\dfrac{n!}{(n-r)!\\,r!}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>There are $9$ players, so $n=9.$ We want to select $2$ of those players for each of the matches, so we have $r=2.$</p>\n<p>Therefore, the number of games is</p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\require{cancel}\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\t{}_9C_2 &= \\dfrac {9!} {(9 - 2)!\\,2!} \\\\[5pt]\n                        &= \\dfrac {9!} {7!2!} \\\\[5pt]\n                        &= \\dfrac {9 \\cdot 8 \\cdot  \\cancel{7!}} {\\cancel{7!} \\cdot 2!} \\\\[5pt]\n                        &= \\dfrac {9 \\cdot 8} {2 \\cdot 1} \\\\[5pt]\n                        &= \\dfrac {72} {2} \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t&= 36.\n\n         }\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The number of combinations of $n$ objects taken $r$ at a time is given by the formula\n\\[\n{}_nC_r = \\dfrac{n!}{(n-r)!\\,r!}.\n\\]\nThere are $9$ players, so $n=9.$ We want to select $2$ of those players for each of the matches, so we have $r=2.$\nTherefore, the number of games is\n\\[\n\\require{cancel}\n\\eqalign{\n{}_9C_2 &= \\dfrac {9!} {(9 - 2)!\\,2!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {9!} {7!2!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {9 \\cdot 8 \\cdot \\cancel{7!}} {\\cancel{7!} \\cdot 2!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {9 \\cdot 8} {2 \\cdot 1} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {72} {2} \\\\[5pt]\n&= 36.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": " $36$",
              "text": "$36$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": " $72$",
              "text": "$72$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": " $64$",
              "text": "$64$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": " $32$",
              "text": "$32$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": " $17$",
              "text": "$17$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": " $36$",
            "text": "$36$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",

        {
          "number": 6,
          "question_id": 86825,
          "task_id": 2828114,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 705,
          "topic_name": "Combinations",
          "example_id": 5677,
          "example_title": "Calculating the Number of Combinations of Objects Chosen From a Group Fewer Than Ten",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T18:44:46.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 6:44 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 31588,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:32",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>There are $8$ members in a band.  In how many ways can two soloists be selected?</p>\n",
          "question_text": "There are $8$ members in a band. In how many ways can two soloists be selected?",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The number of combinations of $n$ objects taken $r$ at a time is given by the formula\n\\[\n{}_nC_r = \\dfrac{n!}{(n-r)!\\,r!}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>There are $8$ members, so $n=8.$ We want to select $2$ of those, so we have $r=2.$</p>\n<p>Therefore, the number of different selections of two soloists is</p>\n<p>\\[\n\t\\require{cancel}\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\t{}_8C_2 &= \\dfrac {8!} {(8 - 2)!\\,2!} \\\\[5pt]\n                        &= \\dfrac {8!} {6!2!} \\\\[5pt]\n                        &= \\dfrac {8 \\cdot 7 \\cdot  \\cancel{6!}} {\\cancel{6!}\\cdot 2!} \\\\[5pt]\n                                 &= \\dfrac {56} {2} \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t\t&= 28.\n\n         }\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The number of combinations of $n$ objects taken $r$ at a time is given by the formula\n\\[\n{}_nC_r = \\dfrac{n!}{(n-r)!\\,r!}.\n\\]\nThere are $8$ members, so $n=8.$ We want to select $2$ of those, so we have $r=2.$\nTherefore, the number of different selections of two soloists is\n\\[\n\\require{cancel}\n\\eqalign{\n{}_8C_2 &= \\dfrac {8!} {(8 - 2)!\\,2!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {8!} {6!2!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {8 \\cdot 7 \\cdot \\cancel{6!}} {\\cancel{6!}\\cdot 2!} \\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac {56} {2} \\\\[5pt]\n&= 28.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$28$",
              "text": "$28$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$70$",
              "text": "$70$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$56$",
              "text": "$56$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$64$",
              "text": "$64$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$80$",
              "text": "$80$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {

            "text": "$28$",
