      "completed_at": "2025-01-12T12:09:15.000Z",
      "completed_date": "Sun, Jan 12th, 2025",
      "completed_time": "12:09 PM",
      "started_at": "2025-01-12T12:00:15.000Z",
      "points_possible": 12,
      "points_awarded": 15,
      "progress": 1,
      "progress_display": "100%",
      "review_url": "/learn?taskId=2824126",
      "topic_url": "/topics/1108",
      "prerequisites": [
        {
          "id": 986,
          "name": "Calculating the Equation of a Tangent Line Using Differentiation",
          "url_slug": null,
          "topic_url": "/topics/986"
        }
      ],
      "question_count": 6,
      "questions": [
        {
          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 282,
          "task_id": 2824126,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1108,
          "topic_name": "The Chain Rule for Differentiation",
          "example_id": 181,
          "example_title": "Differentiating a Polynomial Raised to a Power Using the Chain Rule",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T12:02:49.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 12:02 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 17109,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:17",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x} $ when $f(x) = (3+2x)^4.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x} $ when $f(x) = (3+2x)^4.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, we express $f(x)$ as a composite function:</p>\n<p>\\[\nf = g^4, \\qquad g = 3+2x\n\\]</p>\n<p>The chain rule states that</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x} = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}g} \\cdot \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}g} {\\textrm{d}x}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Differentiating, we have</p>\n<p>\\[\n \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}g}  = 4g^3,\n\\]</p>\n<p>and</p>\n<p>\\[\n \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}g} {\\textrm{d}x} = 2.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Finally, multiplying the two together and substituting for $g$ gives</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}  \n&= 4g^3 \\cdot 2\\\\\n&= 8(3+2x)^3\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, we express $f(x)$ as a composite function:\n\\[\nf = g^4, \\qquad g = 3+2x\n\\]\nThe chain rule states that\n\\[\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x} = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}g} \\cdot \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}g} {\\textrm{d}x}.\n\\]\nDifferentiating, we have\n\\[\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}g} = 4g^3,\n\\]\nand\n\\[\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}g} {\\textrm{d}x} = 2.\n\\]\nFinally, multiplying the two together and substituting for $g$ gives\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\n&= 4g^3 \\cdot 2\\\\\n&= 8(3+2x)^3\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$8(3+2x)^3$",
              "text": "$8(3+2x)^3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$4(3+2x)^3$",
              "text": "$4(3+2x)^3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$8x(3+2x)^3$",
              "text": "$8x(3+2x)^3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$10(3+2x)^5$",
              "text": "$10(3+2x)^5$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{2(3+2x)^3}{x}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{2(3+2x)^3}{x}$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$8(3+2x)^3$",
            "text": "$8(3+2x)^3$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12837608,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        },
        {
          "number": 2,
          "question_id": 107287,
          "task_id": 2824126,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1108,
          "topic_name": "The Chain Rule for Differentiation",
          "example_id": 181,
          "example_title": "Differentiating a Polynomial Raised to a Power Using the Chain Rule",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T12:03:47.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 12:03 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 55549,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:56",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x} $ when $f(x) = \\left(x^2 - x\\right)^6.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x} $ when $f(x) = \\left(x^2 - x\\right)^6.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, we express $f(x)$ as a composite function:</p>\n<p>\\[\nf = g^6, \\qquad g = x^2- x\n\\]</p>\n<p>The chain rule states that</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x} = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}g} \\cdot \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}g} {\\textrm{d}x}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Differentiating, we have</p>\n<p>\\[\n \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}g}  = 6g^5,\n\\]</p>\n<p>and</p>\n<p>\\[\n \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}g} {\\textrm{d}x} = 2x-1.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Finally, multiplying the two together and substituting for $g$ gives</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}  \n&= 6g^5 \\cdot (2x - 1) \\\\[5pt]\n&= 6\\left(x^2-x\\right)^5\\left(2x-1\\right).\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, we express $f(x)$ as a composite function:\n\\[\nf = g^6, \\qquad g = x^2- x\n\\]\nThe chain rule states that\n\\[\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x} = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}g} \\cdot \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}g} {\\textrm{d}x}.\n\\]\nDifferentiating, we have\n\\[\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}g} = 6g^5,\n\\]\nand\n\\[\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}g} {\\textrm{d}x} = 2x-1.\n\\]\nFinally, multiplying the two together and substituting for $g$ gives\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\n&= 6g^5 \\cdot (2x - 1) \\\\[5pt]\n&= 6\\left(x^2-x\\right)^5\\left(2x-1\\right).\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$6\\left(x^2-x\\right)^5\\left(2x-1\\right)$",
              "text": "$6\\left(x^2-x\\right)^5\\left(2x-1\\right)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$6(2x-1)^5$",
              "text": "$6(2x-1)^5$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$6(x^2 - x)^5$",
              "text": "$6(x^2 - x)^5$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{2x(x^2- x)}{6}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{2x(x^2- x)}{6}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$12x(x^2 - x)^5$",
              "text": "$12x(x^2 - x)^5$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$6\\left(x^2-x\\right)^5\\left(2x-1\\right)$",
            "text": "$6\\left(x^2-x\\right)^5\\left(2x-1\\right)$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],

        {
          "number": 3,
          "question_id": 107290,
          "task_id": 2824126,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1108,
          "topic_name": "The Chain Rule for Differentiation",
          "example_id": 182,
          "example_title": "Differentiating a Radical Function Using the Chain Rule",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T12:05:50.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 12:05 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 40416,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:40",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find $\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x}$ for $y = \\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt{3x-2}}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find $\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x}$ for $y = \\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt{3x-2}}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>We use the chain rule in the form</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x} = \\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Let $u=3x-2.$ Then $y = \\dfrac 1{\\sqrt u} = u^{-1/2}.$ Differentiating, we have</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} = -\\dfrac{1}{2}u^{-3/2} = -\\dfrac{1}{2\\sqrt{u^3}}\n\\]</p>\n<p>and</p>\n<p>\\[\n \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}  = 3.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Multiplying the two together gives</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x} \n&= -\\dfrac{1}{2\\sqrt{u^3}}\\cdot 3\\\\[5pt]\n&=- \\dfrac{3}{2\\sqrt{(3x-2)^3}}.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "We use the chain rule in the form\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x} = \\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}.\n\\]\nLet $u=3x-2.$ Then $y = \\dfrac 1{\\sqrt u} = u^{-1/2}.$ Differentiating, we have\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} = -\\dfrac{1}{2}u^{-3/2} = -\\dfrac{1}{2\\sqrt{u^3}}\n\\]\nand\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} = 3.\n\\]\nMultiplying the two together gives\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x}\n&= -\\dfrac{1}{2\\sqrt{u^3}}\\cdot 3\\\\[5pt]\n&=- \\dfrac{3}{2\\sqrt{(3x-2)^3}}.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$- \\dfrac{3}{2\\sqrt{(3x-2)^3}}$",
              "text": "$- \\dfrac{3}{2\\sqrt{(3x-2)^3}}$"
            },

        {
          "number": 4,
          "question_id": 48851,
          "task_id": 2824126,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1108,
          "topic_name": "The Chain Rule for Differentiation",
          "example_id": 182,
          "example_title": "Differentiating a Radical Function Using the Chain Rule",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T12:07:13.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 12:07 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 79432,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:19",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find $\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x}$ for $y = -\\dfrac{2}{\\sqrt{3x-1}}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find $\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x}$ for $y = -\\dfrac{2}{\\sqrt{3x-1}}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>We use the chain rule in the form</p>\n<p>\\[\n        \\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x} = \\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} \\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Let $u =3x-1.$ Then $y = -2\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt{u}}=-2u^{-1/2}.$ Differentiating, we have</p>\n<p>\\[\n        \\dfrac{\\textrm d  y}{\\textrm d u} = -2\\left(- \\dfrac{1}{2u^{3/2}}\\right) =  \\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt{u^3}} \n\\]</p>\n<p>and</p>\n<p>\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} = 3.\n\\]</p>\n<p>Multiplying the two together gives</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n         \\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x} &= \\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} \\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[3pt]\n                  &= \\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt{u^3}}\\cdot 3\\\\[3pt]\n                  &= \\dfrac{3}{\\sqrt{(3x-1)^3}}.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "We use the chain rule in the form\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x} = \\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} \\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}.\n\\]\nLet $u =3x-1.$ Then $y = -2\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt{u}}=-2u^{-1/2}.$ Differentiating, we have\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} = -2\\left(- \\dfrac{1}{2u^{3/2}}\\right) = \\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt{u^3}}\n\\]\nand\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} = 3.\n\\]\nMultiplying the two together gives\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x} &= \\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} \\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[3pt]\n&= \\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt{u^3}}\\cdot 3\\\\[3pt]\n&= \\dfrac{3}{\\sqrt{(3x-1)^3}}.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{3}{\\sqrt{(3x-1)^3}}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{3}{\\sqrt{(3x-1)^3}}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{3}{\\sqrt[3]{(3x-1)^2}}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{3}{\\sqrt[3]{(3x-1)^2}}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$-\\dfrac{3}{\\sqrt{(3x-1)^3}}$",
              "text": "$-\\dfrac{3}{\\sqrt{(3x-1)^3}}$"
            },

              "html": "$\\dfrac{3}{2\\sqrt{(3x-1)^3}}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{3}{2\\sqrt{(3x-1)^3}}$"

        {
          "number": 5,
          "question_id": 107293,
          "task_id": 2824126,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1108,
          "topic_name": "The Chain Rule for Differentiation",
          "example_id": 183,
          "example_title": "Differentiating a Function Using the Chain Rule and Prime Notation",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T12:08:10.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 12:08 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 16105,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:16",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $f(x)=\\left(3x + 4 \\right)^5,$ find $f'(x).$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $f(x)=\\left(3x + 4 \\right)^5,$ find $f'(x).$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Let $g(x)=3x+4$ and $f(g(x)) = g(x)^{5}.$ </p>\n<p>Differentiating, we have\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(g(x)) \t \t& = ( g^{5})' = 5g^{4}, \\\\[5pt]\ng'(x)  \t& = (3x + 4)' = 3.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Then, using the chain rule, </p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nf'(x)\t\t& = f'(g(x))\\cdot g'(x)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t& = 5g^{4} \\cdot 3\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t& = 15(3x + 4)^4.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Let $g(x)=3x+4$ and $f(g(x)) = g(x)^{5}.$\nDifferentiating, we have\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(g(x)) & = ( g^{5})' = 5g^{4}, \\\\[5pt]\ng'(x) & = (3x + 4)' = 3.\n\\end{align*}\nThen, using the chain rule,\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x) & = f'(g(x))\\cdot g'(x)\\\\[5pt]\n& = 5g^{4} \\cdot 3\\\\[5pt]\n& = 15(3x + 4)^4.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$15(3x + 4)^4$",
              "text": "$15(3x + 4)^4$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$5(3x + 4)^4$",
              "text": "$5(3x + 4)^4$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$3(3x + 4)^5$",
              "text": "$3(3x + 4)^5$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$9(3x + 4)^4$",
              "text": "$9(3x + 4)^4$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$20(3x + 4)^4$",
              "text": "$20(3x + 4)^4$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$15(3x + 4)^4$",
            "text": "$15(3x + 4)^4$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12837863,

        {
          "number": 6,
          "question_id": 107298,
          "task_id": 2824126,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1108,
          "topic_name": "The Chain Rule for Differentiation",
          "example_id": 183,
          "example_title": "Differentiating a Function Using the Chain Rule and Prime Notation",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "H",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyHard",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T12:09:15.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 12:09 PM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 62124,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:02",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find $f'(x)$ for $f(x) = -\\dfrac{4}{\\sqrt{x- x^2}}.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find $f'(x)$ for $f(x) = -\\dfrac{4}{\\sqrt{x- x^2}}.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Let $g(x)=x - x^2$ and $f(g(x)) = -\\dfrac{4}{\\sqrt{g}} = -4g^{-1/2}.$ </p>\n<p>Differentiating, we have\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(g(x)) \t \t& = -4( g^{-1/2})' = 4\\cdot \\dfrac{1}{2} g^{-3/2} = \\dfrac 2{g^{3/2}} , \\\\[5pt] \ng'(x)  \t& = (x - x^2)' = 1 - 2x.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Then, using the chain rule, </p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\nf'(x)\t\t& = f'(g(x))\\cdot g'(x)\\\\[3pt]\n\t\t& = \\dfrac{2}{g^{3/2}}  \\cdot (1 - 2x)\\\\[3pt]\n\t%\t& = \\dfrac{2 - 4x}{g^{3/2} } \\\\[3pt]\n\t\t& = \\dfrac{2 - 4x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Let $g(x)=x - x^2$ and $f(g(x)) = -\\dfrac{4}{\\sqrt{g}} = -4g^{-1/2}.$\nDifferentiating, we have\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(g(x)) & = -4( g^{-1/2})' = 4\\cdot \\dfrac{1}{2} g^{-3/2} = \\dfrac 2{g^{3/2}} , \\\\[5pt]\ng'(x) & = (x - x^2)' = 1 - 2x.\n\\end{align*}\nThen, using the chain rule,\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x) & = f'(g(x))\\cdot g'(x)\\\\[3pt]\n& = \\dfrac{2}{g^{3/2}} \\cdot (1 - 2x)\\\\[3pt]\n% & = \\dfrac{2 - 4x}{g^{3/2} } \\\\[3pt]\n& = \\dfrac{2 - 4x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{2 - 4x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{2 - 4x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{1- 2x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{1- 2x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$-\\dfrac{1 - 2x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }$",
              "text": "$-\\dfrac{1 - 2x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$-\\dfrac{2 + 4x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }$",
              "text": "$-\\dfrac{2 + 4x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{2 + 4x}{\\sqrt{x - x^2} }$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{2 + 4x}{\\sqrt{x - x^2} }$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$\\dfrac{2 - 4x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }$",
            "text": "$\\dfrac{2 - 4x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }$",

      "question_count": 6,
      "questions": [
        {
          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 101590,
          "task_id": 2823599,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1114,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Exponential Functions",
          "example_id": 3624,
          "example_title": "Differentiating the Product of a Constant and the Exponential Function, Plus a Constant",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T11:55:57.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:55 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 9538,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:10",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $f(x)=2e^x - \\ln 11$, find the slope of the tangent at $x=1.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $f(x)=2e^x - \\ln 11$, find the slope of the tangent at $x=1.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The derivative of $e^x$ is just $e^x$ itself. On the other hand, $-\\ln{11}$ is a constant, so its derivative is $0.$</p>\n<p>First, using the addition rule, we get</p>\n<p>\\begin{align}\n\tf'(x)\t& = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left( 2e^x-\\ln 11 \\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t& = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(2e^x)+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(-\\ln{11}) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t& = 2\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^x)+0\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t& = 2e^x.\n\\end{align}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=1$ is\n\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\t\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=1}\n\t&=\\left.\\left(2e^x\\right)\\,\\right|_{x=1}\\\\[3pt]\n\t&=2e^{1}\\\\[3pt]\n\t&=2e.\n}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The derivative of $e^x$ is just $e^x$ itself. On the other hand, $-\\ln{11}$ is a constant, so its derivative is $0.$\nFirst, using the addition rule, we get\n\\begin{align}\nf'(x) & = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left( 2e^x-\\ln 11 \\right) \\\\[5pt]\n& = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(2e^x)+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(-\\ln{11}) \\\\[5pt]\n& = 2\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^x)+0\\\\[5pt]\n& = 2e^x.\n\\end{align}\nTherefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=1$ is\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=1}\n&=\\left.\\left(2e^x\\right)\\,\\right|_{x=1}\\\\[3pt]\n&=2e^{1}\\\\[3pt]\n&=2e.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$2e$",
              "text": "$2e$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$0$",
              "text": "$0$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$1$",
              "text": "$1$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$2$",
              "text": "$2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$e^2$",
              "text": "$e^2$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$2e$",
            "text": "$2e$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12837272,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        },
        {
          "number": 2,
          "question_id": 71057,
          "task_id": 2823599,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1114,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Exponential Functions",
          "example_id": 3624,
          "example_title": "Differentiating the Product of a Constant and the Exponential Function, Plus a Constant",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T11:56:20.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:56 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 19195,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:19",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $f(x) =4 -3e^x-\\ln 6,$ find $f'(x).$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $f(x) =4 -3e^x-\\ln 6,$ find $f'(x).$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>The derivative of $e^x$ is just $e^x$ itself. On the other hand, $4$ and $-\\ln{6}$ are constants, so their derivatives are $0.$</p>\n<p>Using the addition rule, then, we have </p>\n<p>\\begin{eqnarray}\n    \\eqalign{\nf'(x)\t& = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(4-3e^x-\\ln 6\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n\n& =\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(4)+\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(-3e^x)+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(-\\ln{6}) \\\\[5pt]\n\n& =0-3\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^x)+0\\\\[5pt]\n\n& = -3e^x.\n    }\n\\end{eqnarray}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "The derivative of $e^x$ is just $e^x$ itself. On the other hand, $4$ and $-\\ln{6}$ are constants, so their derivatives are $0.$\nUsing the addition rule, then, we have\n\\begin{eqnarray}\n\\eqalign{\nf'(x) & = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(4-3e^x-\\ln 6\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n& =\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(4)+\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(-3e^x)+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(-\\ln{6}) \\\\[5pt]\n& =0-3\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^x)+0\\\\[5pt]\n& = -3e^x.\n}\n\\end{eqnarray}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$f'(x)=-3e^x$ ",
              "text": "$f'(x)=-3e^x$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$f'(x)=-3e^x-\\dfrac 1 6$  ",

        {
          "number": 3,
          "question_id": 101599,
          "task_id": 2823599,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1114,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Exponential Functions",
          "example_id": 3625,
          "example_title": "Differentiating the Product of a Constant and the Exponential Function, Plus a Polynomial",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T11:57:02.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:57 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 20108,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:20",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $f(x)=10 - 2x^4 - 7e^x$, find the slope of the tangent at $x=-1.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $f(x)=10 - 2x^4 - 7e^x$, find the slope of the tangent at $x=-1.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, using the addition and the power rules, we get\n\\begin{align}\n\tf'(x)\t& = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}( 10 - 2x^4 - 7e^x) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t& = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(10 ) + \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(- 2x^4)+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(-7e^x) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t& = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(10 ) - 2\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(x^4) - 7\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^x)  \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t& =   - 8x^3 - 7e^x.\n\\end{align}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=-1$ is\n\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\t\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=-1}\n\t&=\\left.\\left(- 8x^3 - 7e^x\\right)\\,\\right|_{x=-1}\\\\[3pt]\n\t&=-8\\cdot (-1)^3 - 7\\cdot e^{-1}\\\\[3pt]\n\t&=-8 \\cdot (-1) - 7\\cdot \\dfrac{1}{e}\\\\[3pt]\n\t&=8 -\\dfrac{7}{e}.\n}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, using the addition and the power rules, we get\n\\begin{align}\nf'(x) & = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}( 10 - 2x^4 - 7e^x) \\\\[5pt]\n& = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(10 ) + \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(- 2x^4)+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(-7e^x) \\\\[5pt]\n& = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(10 ) - 2\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(x^4) - 7\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^x) \\\\[5pt]\n& = - 8x^3 - 7e^x.\n\\end{align}\nTherefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=-1$ is\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=-1}\n&=\\left.\\left(- 8x^3 - 7e^x\\right)\\,\\right|_{x=-1}\\\\[3pt]\n&=-8\\cdot (-1)^3 - 7\\cdot e^{-1}\\\\[3pt]\n&=-8 \\cdot (-1) - 7\\cdot \\dfrac{1}{e}\\\\[3pt]\n&=8 -\\dfrac{7}{e}.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$8- \\dfrac{7}{e}$",
              "text": "$8- \\dfrac{7}{e}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$-7$",
              "text": "$-7$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$7e - 8$",
              "text": "$7e - 8$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$8 +e$",
              "text": "$8 +e$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{7}{e}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{7}{e}$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$8- \\dfrac{7}{e}$",
            "text": "$8- \\dfrac{7}{e}$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",

        {
          "number": 4,
          "question_id": 140433,
          "task_id": 2823599,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1114,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Exponential Functions",
          "example_id": 3625,
          "example_title": "Differentiating the Product of a Constant and the Exponential Function, Plus a Polynomial",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T11:57:30.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:57 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 23482,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:23",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $f(x)=7e^{x} + 5x^2 + e^2$, find the slope of the tangent at $x=-1.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $f(x)=7e^{x} + 5x^2 + e^2$, find the slope of the tangent at $x=-1.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>First, using the addition and the power rules, we get\n\\begin{align}\n\tf'(x)\t& = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}( 7e^{x} + 5x^2 + e^2) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t& = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(7e^{x}) + \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(5x^2)+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^2) \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t& = 7\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^{x} ) + 5\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(x^2) +0  \\\\[5pt]\n\t\t& =  7e^x +10x.\n\\end{align}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=-1$ is\n\\[\n\t\\eqalign{\n\t\t\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=-1}\n\t&=\\left.\\left( 7e^x +10x\\right)\\,\\right|_{x=-1}\\\\[3pt]\n\t&=7 \\cdot e^{-1} + 10\\cdot (-1) \\\\[3pt]\n\t&=7\\cdot \\dfrac{1}{e} - 10\\\\[3pt]\n\t&=\\dfrac{7}{e} - 10.\n}\n\\]</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "First, using the addition and the power rules, we get\n\\begin{align}\nf'(x) & = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}( 7e^{x} + 5x^2 + e^2) \\\\[5pt]\n& = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(7e^{x}) + \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(5x^2)+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^2) \\\\[5pt]\n& = 7\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^{x} ) + 5\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(x^2) +0 \\\\[5pt]\n& = 7e^x +10x.\n\\end{align}\nTherefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=-1$ is\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=-1}\n&=\\left.\\left( 7e^x +10x\\right)\\,\\right|_{x=-1}\\\\[3pt]\n&=7 \\cdot e^{-1} + 10\\cdot (-1) \\\\[3pt]\n&=7\\cdot \\dfrac{1}{e} - 10\\\\[3pt]\n&=\\dfrac{7}{e} - 10.\n}\n\\]",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{7}{e} - 10$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{7}{e} - 10$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$7e + 10$",
              "text": "$7e + 10$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$7e$",
              "text": "$7e$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{7}{e}$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{7}{e}$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\dfrac{7}{e} + 5$",
              "text": "$\\dfrac{7}{e} + 5$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {

            "html": "$\\dfrac{7}{e} - 10$",

            "free_response": [],

        {
          "number": 5,
          "question_id": 1924,
          "task_id": 2823599,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1114,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Exponential Functions",
          "example_id": 3626,
          "example_title": "Differentiating an Exponential Function with a Base Other Than e",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T11:58:51.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:58 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 33684,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:34",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>$\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(-4^{x} + 7e^x\\right) = $</p>\n",
          "question_text": "$\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(-4^{x} + 7e^x\\right) = $",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Applying the formula, we get</p>\n<p>\\begin{eqnarray}\n    \\eqalign{\n \tf'(x)&=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(-4^{x} + 7e^x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(-4^{x}\\right)+\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(7e^x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(-4^{x}\\right)+7\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(e^x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n \t\t\t\t\t\t & = -4^x \\cdot \\ln 4 + 7e^x  \\\\[5pt]\n \t\t\t\t\t\t& = -4^x\\ \\ln 4 + 7e^x. \n \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\n       \n    }\n\\end{eqnarray}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Applying the formula, we get\n\\begin{eqnarray}\n\\eqalign{\nf'(x)&=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(-4^{x} + 7e^x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(-4^{x}\\right)+\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(7e^x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(-4^{x}\\right)+7\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(e^x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n& = -4^x \\cdot \\ln 4 + 7e^x \\\\[5pt]\n& = -4^x\\ \\ln 4 + 7e^x.\n}\n\\end{eqnarray}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$-4^x\\ \\ln 4 + 7e^x$",
              "text": "$-4^x\\ \\ln 4 + 7e^x$"

              "text": "$3e^x$"

              "text": "$6\\ln 4\\cdot 4^x$"

              "number": 5,
              "html": "$-4^x + 7e^x$",

          "student_answer": {

            "html": "$-4^x\\ \\ln 4 + 7e^x$",

        {
          "number": 6,
          "question_id": 51210,
          "task_id": 2823599,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 1114,
          "topic_name": "Differentiating Exponential Functions",
          "example_id": 3626,
          "example_title": "Differentiating an Exponential Function with a Base Other Than e",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "M",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyModerate",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T11:59:30.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:59 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 35824,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:36",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Given that $f(x)=3\\cdot 2^x-3x^4-4$,  find the instantaneous rate of change at $x=0.$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Given that $f(x)=3\\cdot 2^x-3x^4-4$, find the instantaneous rate of change at $x=0.$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Applying the formula, we get\n\\begin{align*}\n\tf'(x)&= \\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(3\\cdot 2^x-3x^4-4\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&= \\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(3\\cdot 2^x\\right)-\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(3x^4\\right)-\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(4\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&= 3\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(2^x\\right)-3\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(x^4\\right)-\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(4\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{x} - 3 \\cdot 4x^3-0\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&=3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{x}-12x^3.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the slope at $x=0$ is \n\\begin{align*}\n\t\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=0}\n\t\t&=\\left.\\left( 3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{x}-12x^3\\right)\\right|_{x=0}\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&=3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{0}-12(0)^3\\\\[5pt]\n\t\t&=3 \\ln 2.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Applying the formula, we get\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x)&= \\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(3\\cdot 2^x-3x^4-4\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&= \\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(3\\cdot 2^x\\right)-\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(3x^4\\right)-\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(4\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&= 3\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(2^x\\right)-3\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(x^4\\right)-\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(4\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&=3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{x} - 3 \\cdot 4x^3-0\\\\[5pt]\n&=3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{x}-12x^3.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the slope at $x=0$ is\n\\begin{align*}\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=0}\n&=\\left.\\left( 3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{x}-12x^3\\right)\\right|_{x=0}\\\\[5pt]\n&=3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{0}-12(0)^3\\\\[5pt]\n&=3 \\ln 2.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$3 \\ln 2$",
              "text": "$3 \\ln 2$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$6 \\ln 2$",
              "text": "$6 \\ln 2$"
            },

              "text": "$3 \\ln 2 - 4$"

              "text": "$3 \\ln 2 - 12$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\ln 6$",
              "text": "$\\ln 6$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$3 \\ln 2$",
            "text": "$3 \\ln 2$",
            "free_response": [],

          },

    {
      "task_id": 2817541,

        {
          "id": 332,

      "question_count": 5,
      "questions": [
        {
          "number": 1,
          "question_id": 10845,
          "task_id": 2817541,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 986,
          "topic_name": "Calculating the Equation of a Tangent Line Using Differentiation",
          "example_id": 144,
          "example_title": "Calculating the Equation of a Tangent Line to a Curve at a Point",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T11:46:54.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:46 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 87562,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:28",
          "result": "incorrect",
          "correct": false,
          "question_html": "<p>Find the equation of the tangent to the curve $y = \\dfrac{2}{x^5} + 7x$ at the point $(1,9).$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find the equation of the tangent to the curve $y = \\dfrac{2}{x^5} + 7x$ at the point $(1,9).$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Let $f(x)=\\dfrac{2}{x^5}+7x.$ Computing the derivative $f'(x)$ gives</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n \tf'(x)  & =  \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(\\dfrac{2}{x^5} + 7x\\right) \\\\ \n\t\t& =  \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(2x^{-5} + 7x\\right) \\\\\n \t\t\t\t&= -10x^{-6} + 7.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>We then compute $f'(1)$, which gives\n\\begin{align*}\n \tf'(1) = -10(1)^{-6} + 7 = -3. \n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Finally, we use the point-slope form with $m=-3$ and the coordinates of our point $(1,9),$ and get\n\\begin{align*}\n \ty - y_1 &= m(x-x_1) \\\\\n \ty - (9) &= -3(x-1) \\\\\n \ty    & =-3x + 3 +9\\\\\n \ty    & =-3x + 12.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Let $f(x)=\\dfrac{2}{x^5}+7x.$ Computing the derivative $f'(x)$ gives\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x) & = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(\\dfrac{2}{x^5} + 7x\\right) \\\\\n& = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(2x^{-5} + 7x\\right) \\\\\n&= -10x^{-6} + 7.\n\\end{align*}\nWe then compute $f'(1)$, which gives\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(1) = -10(1)^{-6} + 7 = -3.\n\\end{align*}\nFinally, we use the point-slope form with $m=-3$ and the coordinates of our point $(1,9),$ and get\n\\begin{align*}\ny - y_1 &= m(x-x_1) \\\\\ny - (9) &= -3(x-1) \\\\\ny & =-3x + 3 +9\\\\\ny & =-3x + 12.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$y = -3x + 12$",
              "text": "$y = -3x + 12$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$y = -3x + 9$",
              "text": "$y = -3x + 9$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$y = -3x + 6$",
              "text": "$y = -3x + 6$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$y = -3x + 3$",
              "text": "$y = -3x + 3$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$y = -3x + 1$",
              "text": "$y = -3x + 1$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 3,
            "html": "$y = -3x + 6$",
            "text": "$y = -3x + 6$",
            "free_response": [],
            "raw_choices": null
          },
          "hints": [],
          "hint_status": "No hint field is exposed by the Math Academy completed-task review API.",
          "graphics": [],
          "raw": {
            "answer_id": 12836831,
            "select_id": null,
            "question_format": "MC"
          }
        },
        {
          "number": 2,
          "question_id": 10838,
          "task_id": 2817541,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 986,
          "topic_name": "Calculating the Equation of a Tangent Line Using Differentiation",
          "example_id": 144,
          "example_title": "Calculating the Equation of a Tangent Line to a Curve at a Point",
          "format": "MC",

          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T11:49:38.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:49 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 155430,
          "time_elapsed_display": "2:35",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find the equation of the tangent to the curve $y=x^2+5x$ at the point $(-1,-4).$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find the equation of the tangent to the curve $y=x^2+5x$ at the point $(-1,-4).$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Let $f(x)=x^2+5x.$ Computing the derivative $f'(x)$ gives</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n \tf'(x)  =  \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(x^2 + 5x\\right)= 2x+5.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>We then compute $f'(-1)$, which gives\n\\begin{align*}\n \tf'(-1) = 2(-1)+5 = 3 \\,. \n\\end{align*}\nFinally, we use the point-slope form with $m=3$ and the coordinates of our point $(-1, -4),$ and get\n\\begin{align*}\n \ty - y_1 &= m(x-x_1) \\\\\n \ty - (-4) &= 3(x-(-1)) \\\\\ny +4 &= 3(x+1) \\\\\n \ty    & = 3x +3 -4\\\\\n \ty &= 3x-1.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Let $f(x)=x^2+5x.$ Computing the derivative $f'(x)$ gives\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x) = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(x^2 + 5x\\right)= 2x+5.\n\\end{align*}\nWe then compute $f'(-1)$, which gives\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(-1) = 2(-1)+5 = 3 \\,.\n\\end{align*}\nFinally, we use the point-slope form with $m=3$ and the coordinates of our point $(-1, -4),$ and get\n\\begin{align*}\ny - y_1 &= m(x-x_1) \\\\\ny - (-4) &= 3(x-(-1)) \\\\\ny +4 &= 3(x+1) \\\\\ny & = 3x +3 -4\\\\\ny &= 3x-1.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$y=3x-1$",
              "text": "$y=3x-1$"
            },

              "text": "$y=2x-3$"
            },

              "text": "$y=3x-6$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {
            "selected_choice": 1,
            "html": "$y=3x-1$",
            "text": "$y=3x-1$",

          "number": 3,

          "topic_name": "Calculating the Equation of a Tangent Line Using Differentiation",
          "example_id": 144,
          "example_title": "Calculating the Equation of a Tangent Line to a Curve at a Point",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T11:50:30.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:50 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 49595,
          "time_elapsed_display": "0:50",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find the equation of the tangent to the curve $y = x^2 - 3x$ at the point $(-2,10).$</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find the equation of the tangent to the curve $y = x^2 - 3x$ at the point $(-2,10).$",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Let $f(x)=x^2-3x.$ Computing the derivative $f'(x)$ gives</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n \tf'(x)  =  \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(x^2 - 3x\\right)= 2x-3.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>We then compute $f'(-2)$, which gives\n\\begin{align*}\n \tf'(-2) = 2(-2)-3 = -7. \n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Finally, we use the point-slope form with $m=-7$ and the coordinates of our point $(-2, 10),$ and get\n\\begin{align*}\n \ty - y_1 &= m(x-x_1) \\\\\n        y - (10) &= -7(x-(-2)) \\\\\n \ty - 10 &= -7(x+2) \\\\\n \ty    & = -7x - 14 +10\\\\\n \ty &= -7x -4.\n\\end{align*}</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Let $f(x)=x^2-3x.$ Computing the derivative $f'(x)$ gives\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x) = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(x^2 - 3x\\right)= 2x-3.\n\\end{align*}\nWe then compute $f'(-2)$, which gives\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(-2) = 2(-2)-3 = -7.\n\\end{align*}\nFinally, we use the point-slope form with $m=-7$ and the coordinates of our point $(-2, 10),$ and get\n\\begin{align*}\ny - y_1 &= m(x-x_1) \\\\\ny - (10) &= -7(x-(-2)) \\\\\ny - 10 &= -7(x+2) \\\\\ny & = -7x - 14 +10\\\\\ny &= -7x -4.\n\\end{align*}",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$y = -7x -4$",
              "text": "$y = -7x -4$"
            },

              "number": 2,
              "html": "$y = -7x +4$",
              "text": "$y = -7x +4$"

              "text": "$y = 6x -2$"

              "text": "$y = -x -2$"

              "text": "$y = 6x +2$"

          ],

        {
          "number": 4,
          "question_id": 103332,
          "task_id": 2817541,
          "task_type": "Lesson",
          "topic_id": 986,
          "topic_name": "Calculating the Equation of a Tangent Line Using Differentiation",
          "example_id": 146,
          "example_title": "Determining where a Tangent Line Intersects an Axis",
          "format": "MC",
          "difficulty": "E",
          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T11:53:36.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:53 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 74548,
          "time_elapsed_display": "1:15",
          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find where the tangent to the curve $y = 3x^2+ 5x$ at the point $(-1,-2)$ intersects the $y$-axis.</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find where the tangent to the curve $y = 3x^2+ 5x$ at the point $(-1,-2)$ intersects the $y$-axis.",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Let $f(x)=3x^2+ 5x.$ Let&#39;s first calculate the derivative:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n \tf'(x)  & =  \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(3x^2+ 5x\\right) \n \t\t\t\t= 6x+5\\\\\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>So, the slope of the tangent at our point is\n\\[\n \tm=f'(-1)  = 6(-1) +5 = -1 \\,.  \n\\]</p>\n<p>Therefore, the equation of the tangent is</p>\n<p>\\begin{align}\n \ty - y_1 &= m(x-x_1) \\\\\n \ty + 2 &= -(x+1) \\\\\n \ty    & = -x-1-2\\\\\n \ty &= -x-3.\n\\end{align}</p>\n<p>To find where the tangent line intercepts the $y$-axis, we set $x=0$ and solve:\n\\begin{align*}\ny\t& = -0-3\\\\\ny\t& = -3\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the point of intersection is $\\left(0,-3\\right).$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Let $f(x)=3x^2+ 5x.$ Let's first calculate the derivative:\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x) & = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(3x^2+ 5x\\right)\n= 6x+5\\\\\n\\end{align*}\nSo, the slope of the tangent at our point is\n\\[\nm=f'(-1) = 6(-1) +5 = -1 \\,.\n\\]\nTherefore, the equation of the tangent is\n\\begin{align}\ny - y_1 &= m(x-x_1) \\\\\ny + 2 &= -(x+1) \\\\\ny & = -x-1-2\\\\\ny &= -x-3.\n\\end{align}\nTo find where the tangent line intercepts the $y$-axis, we set $x=0$ and solve:\n\\begin{align*}\ny & = -0-3\\\\\ny & = -3\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the point of intersection is $\\left(0,-3\\right).$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": " ",
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\left(0,-3\\right)$",
              "text": "$\\left(0,-3\\right)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\\left(0,5\\right)$",
              "text": "$\\left(0,5\\right)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\\left(0,2\\right)$",
              "text": "$\\left(0,2\\right)$"
            },

              "html": "$\\left(0,-1\\right)$",
              "text": "$\\left(0,-1\\right)$"
            },
            {

              "html": "$\\left(0,0\\right)$",
              "text": "$\\left(0,0\\right)$"
            }
          ],
          "student_answer": {

            "html": "$\\left(0,-3\\right)$",
            "text": "$\\left(0,-3\\right)$",

          "example_id": 146,

          "format": "MC",

          "difficulty_class": "difficultyEasy",
          "created_at": "2025-01-12T11:55:04.000Z",
          "created_display": "Sun, Jan 12th @ 11:55 AM",
          "time_elapsed_ms": 85759,

          "result": "correct",
          "correct": true,
          "question_html": "<p>Find where the tangent to the curve $y = x^4-5$ at the point $(-1,-4)$ intersects the $x$-axis.</p>\n",
          "question_text": "Find where the tangent to the curve $y = x^4-5$ at the point $(-1,-4)$ intersects the $x$-axis.",
          "explanation_html": "<p>Let $f(x)=x^4-5.$ Let&#39;s first calculate the derivative:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n \tf'(x)  & =  \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(x^4-5\\right) \n \t\t\t\t= 4x^3\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>So, the slope of the tangent at our point is\n\\[\n \tm=f'(-1)  = 4(-1)^3 = -4.  \n\\]</p>\n<p>Therefore, the equation of the tangent is</p>\n<p>\\begin{align}\n \ty - y_1 &= m(x-x_1) \\\\\n \ty - (-4) &= -4(x+1) \\\\\n \ty    & = -4x-4-4\\\\\n \ty &= -4x-8.\n\\end{align}</p>\n<p>To find where the tangent line intercepts the $x$-axis, we set $y=0$ and solve:</p>\n<p>\\begin{align*}\n0\t& = -4x-8\\\\\nx\t& = -2\n\\end{align*}</p>\n<p>Therefore, the point of intersection is $\\left(-2, 0\\right).$</p>\n",
          "explanation_text": "Let $f(x)=x^4-5.$ Let's first calculate the derivative:\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x) & = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(x^4-5\\right)\n= 4x^3\n\\end{align*}\nSo, the slope of the tangent at our point is\n\\[\nm=f'(-1) = 4(-1)^3 = -4.\n\\]\nTherefore, the equation of the tangent is\n\\begin{align}\ny - y_1 &= m(x-x_1) \\\\\ny - (-4) &= -4(x+1) \\\\\ny & = -4x-4-4\\\\\ny &= -4x-8.\n\\end{align}\nTo find where the tangent line intercepts the $x$-axis, we set $y=0$ and solve:\n\\begin{align*}\n0 & = -4x-8\\\\\nx & = -2\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the point of intersection is $\\left(-2, 0\\right).$",
          "free_answer_choice_html": null,
          "free_answer_choice_text": "",
          "choices": [
            {
              "number": 1,
              "html": "$\\left(-2, 0\\right)$",
              "text": "$\\left(-2, 0\\right)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 2,
              "html": "$\\left(0, 1\\right)$",
              "text": "$\\left(0, 1\\right)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 3,
              "html": "$\\left(0, 0\\right)$",
              "text": "$\\left(0, 0\\right)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 4,
              "html": "$\\left(-5, 0\\right)$",
              "text": "$\\left(-5, 0\\right)$"
            },
            {
              "number": 5,
              "html": "$\\left(4, 0\\right)$",
              "text": "$\\left(4, 0\\right)$"
            }
          ],

            "html": "$\\left(-2, 0\\right)$",
            "text": "$\\left(-2, 0\\right)$",
